As a result, an exemption under Section 43(2) of the FOI legislation was applied. As this remains the company’s position, we are currently prevented from releasing this information into the public domain.	For the Member’s future reference, Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of financial support offered to every business over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal which can be accessed from the following link: Open Data NI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether Invest NI's target to secure 65 per cent of investment outside the Belfast Metropolitan Area, refers to project count or financial value.
answer_text: The Invest NI strategic target refers to the number of investments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail her Department's definition of supported investment.
answer_text: My Department and its Partner Organisations offer a range of financial and non-financial support to advance my economic vision for the North – the Department’s Business Plan 2025-26 provides further information in that regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of the 19 new investors into Northern Ireland in 2024-25, that have located in the Derry City and Strabane District local council area.
answer_text: Over the period 1st April 2024 to the 31st March 2025, Invest NI supported the location of 1 new inward investor in the Derry City and Strabane District council area. In addition, 4 of the remaining 18 investors will implement remote/hybrid working projects that have the potential to benefit multiple areas across the North.	For the Member’s future reference, Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of financial support offered to every business over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal which can be accessed from the following link: Open Data NI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement she has had with Invest NI,  in regards to the setting of a target for the number of inward investors, outside the Belfast Metropolitan Area.
answer_text: In developing its Business Strategy, Invest NI worked closely with me and my officials to ensure full alignment with my economic vision, particularly the aim of stronger regional balance.  For the first time, the agency has adopted a geographically based target that by year three of the Strategy, 65% of all Invest NI-supported investments will be located outside the Belfast Metropolitan Area (BMA).  This remains a key strategic focus for the agency. There have been a number of very positive investments in the north west across the last 18 months from international investors such as EY, FinTrU, NTT/Alchemy and Seagate.  Each of these were mobile investments which could have gone to other locations, either within the North, or globally, and for which we had to compete.	Through our subregional plans and the work of Local Economic Partnerships, we are laying the foundations to accelerate this in the years ahead; such as pursuing and promoting joined up delivery, building strong regional propositions, enhancing local assets, and engagement with stakeholders to ensure suitable land and property for investment.  The focus remains clear – driving inclusive growth to ensure that every part of the North benefits from economic opportunity.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 30954/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 30954/22-27 was answered on 16 December 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he has secured Executive agreement on the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.
answer_text: My response of 24 November 2025 to question AQW 35855/22-27 refers.The position remains unchanged.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Enhanced Investment Zone.
answer_text: Subject to the Executive’s approval, the intention is to seek final approval from the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government early in 2026 with the aim of Programme spend to commence in this financial year.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 33603/22-27, to detail any engagement the Minister for Infrastructure has had with the Delivery Unit, in the last six months.
answer_text: All Ministers have engaged with the work of the Delivery Unit as members of the Executive, with Executive agreement to the Unit’s first priority area of focus secured in June.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether it is still her intention to introduce the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill, in full, in January 2026.
answer_text: The Bill will be introduced to the Assembly once it is approved by the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail her Department's current definition of regional economic balance.
answer_text: I define a more regionally balanced and prosperous economy as one with opportunities, pay and prosperity more equally spread across different parts of the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline any discussions he has had with the Minister of Finance in relation to ring-fencing funds for the Rates Support Grant ahead of the next budgetary period.
answer_text: I have had no discussions with the Minister of Finance in relation to ring-fencing funds for the RSG ahead of the next budgetary period.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Translink business case for service enhancements on the North West rail corridor.
answer_text: I fully recognise the need to address regional imbalance and better connect our communities including those within the Northwest. Translink submitted a revised business case in September 2025 for the provision of additional services on the North West rail corridor. This is being considered by my officials.I am pleased that Translink have also delivered significant improvements to public transport in the Northwest in recent years, this has included:Adding over 46,000 additional seats by converting 232 rail services per week to 6 car operations.Improvements to Goldliner services. Since April 2024 when the A6 works was completed, Translink has introduced seven additional direct X212 services to and from Derry. The scheduled journey time on X212 services is 1 hour 45 min representing a 15-minute improvement on the standard 212 journey time.Derry is the first city on this island to operate with a full zero emission bus fleet.There has also been significant capital funding in public transport in the Northwest including:Northwest Transport Hub - £27mZero Emission buses - £59mDerry – Coleraine Phase 3 - £97.7mAs I have mentioned, in my 2025/26 budget I have also included ring fenced funding of £5.79m, which will allow Translink to progress Phase 3 of the Derry to Coleraine track renewal project. Work has continued including the production of the detailed design of the required track improvements and preparation for the procurement phase of the project. It has also included the manufacture of railway sleepers in advance of the main works.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether it is her intention to ban the use of all zero-hours contracts.
answer_text: I refer you to the statement on the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill that I made to the Assembly on 28 April 2025 which may be found here: https://www.economy-ni.gov.uk/articles/oral-statement-good-jobs-employment-rights-bill-28-april-2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any steps he is taking to ensure parents continue to be supported in the absence of a childcare strategy.
answer_text: To date the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme (NICSS) has made a tangible difference to thousands of families with the costs of their childcare and I am committed to further improving the affordability of childcare for parents. I have no plans to discontinue the NICSS, however, implementation of actions beyond March 2026 is subject to Executive agreement and the availability of the funding required. There are various sources of support for families with the cost of their childcare depending on their individual circumstances.  I would encourage all parents to ensure they are aware of the existing financial support available by contacting Employers for Childcare’s Family Benefits Advice Service as follows: Employers For Childcare | Family Benefits Advice Service The draft Early Learning and Childcare Strategy for public consultation was issued to my Executive colleagues on Friday 14 November; it is not yet agreed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme will still be in place after March 2026.
answer_text: To date the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme (NICSS) has made a tangible difference to thousands of families with the costs of their childcare and I am committed to further improving the affordability of childcare for parents. I have no plans to discontinue the NICSS, however, implementation of actions beyond March 2026 is subject to Executive agreement and the availability of the funding required. There are various sources of support for families with the cost of their childcare depending on their individual circumstances.  I would encourage all parents to ensure they are aware of the existing financial support available by contacting Employers for Childcare’s Family Benefits Advice Service as follows: Employers For Childcare | Family Benefits Advice Service The draft Early Learning and Childcare Strategy for public consultation was issued to my Executive colleagues on Friday 14 November; it is not yet agreed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he will remove the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme once the Executive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy is published.
answer_text: To date the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme (NICSS) has made a tangible difference to thousands of families with the costs of their childcare and I am committed to further improving the affordability of childcare for parents. I have no plans to discontinue the NICSS, however, implementation of actions beyond March 2026 is subject to Executive agreement and the availability of the funding required. There are various sources of support for families with the cost of their childcare depending on their individual circumstances.  I would encourage all parents to ensure they are aware of the existing financial support available by contacting Employers for Childcare’s Family Benefits Advice Service as follows: Employers For Childcare | Family Benefits Advice Service The draft Early Learning and Childcare Strategy for public consultation was issued to my Executive colleagues on Friday 14 November; it is not yet agreed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail (i) the number of pooled budgets currently in operation between Executive Departments, and (ii) their purpose.
answer_text: It is not possible to determine the number of pooled budgets in operation as departments transfer funds between each other on a regular basis and Department of Finance (DoF) does not hold information on whether those transactions relate to shared or pooled budgets or functions.  My department has produced guidance on pooled budgets and I would encourage departments to engage with DoF if they wish to pool budgets and require support in doing so.https://www.finance-ni.gov.uk/publications/guidance-pooling-budgets

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of action plans, from the Local Economic Partnerships, that have been submitted to her Department for approval.
answer_text: As of 25 November, two Action Plans have been submitted for approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work her Department is undertaking to improve the capacity of battery storage.
answer_text: Research is underway to provide evidence for the development of electricity storage policy here and is expected to be completed shortly. This research will include analysis on the effect of electricity storage on the cost to local consumers, security of supply and decarbonisation, and help inform policy development on both short and long duration electricity storage.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether any of the £45 million funding, which has been allocated to the Local Economic Partnerships, has been spent.
answer_text: I am delighted that all 11 Local Economic Partnerships (LEP) have been established, and development of their Action Plans is progressing. Three  have recently been approved and launched - Newry, Mourne and Down;  Causeway, Coast and Glens; and Lisburn and Castlereagh City. Several more LEPs anticipate submitting Action Plans for approval in the coming weeks, while the remaining LEPs are expected to submit Action Plans early in the New Year. Councils have begun to spend their allocation from the Regional Balance Fund in this financial year while the  remainder has been profiled over the remaining two years to March 2028.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the work of the Local Economic Partnerships.
answer_text: All 11 Local Economic Partnerships have been established, and development of their Action Plans progressing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of battery storage in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: One of my priorities is to reduce carbon emissions and generating sufficient renewable electricity is key to facilitate this change. It is important to build on our previous success in renewables development and the Executive needs to take a collaborative, whole system approach to deliver the 80% by 2030 target.  I recognise the important role that long duration energy storage will play in decarbonising the electricity grid, in particular its ability to utilise excess renewable electricity and export power back to the grid at times of high demand.	Research is underway to provide evidence for the development of electricity storage policy here and is expected to be completed shortly. This research will include analysis on the effect of electricity storage on the cost to local consumers, security of supply and decarbonisation, and help inform policy development on both short and long duration electricity storage.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to outline any action it is taking to improve the reliability of digital systems used by MLAs, with specific reference to cyber security.
To ask the Minister of Justice whether her Department holds any data on progress being made on ending violence against women and girls.
To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she has received Executive approval to introduce her Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill in January 2026.
answer_text: My Department is continuing to work with the Office of the Legislative Counsel on drafting the Bill. Once drafting has been completed, I will seek the agreement of my Executive colleagues to the introduction of the Bill to the Assembly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the cross-border collaboration between Queen's University Belfast and Dundalk Institute of Technology, whether her Department has engaged with Ulster University to see if a similar cross-border collaboration could take place in the North West.
answer_text: I am committed to supporting cross border collaboration in Higher Education across the island and welcome the partnership between Queen’s University Belfast and Dundalk Institute of Technology. As autonomous bodies, decisions of this nature rest with the institutions themselves. 		My Department engages with the North West Tertiary Education Cluster which is the established strategic alliance of all four publicly funded tertiary education providers in the North West.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy has been agreed by the Executive.
answer_text: The draft Executive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy for public consultation was issued to my Executive colleagues on Friday 14 November; it is not yet agreed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the memorandum of understanding between her Department and the Department for Enterprise, Tourism and Employment, for her assessment of the potential benefits that can be achieved as a result of this.
answer_text: The Memorandum of Understanding signed by my Department and the Department for Enterprise, Tourism and Employment, seeks to build on the positive working relationship between the two Departments around areas of mutual interest in advancement of the all-island economy. 	We both want to see thriving economies, growing exports, and successful businesses across the island. This MoU will encourage closer working on issues of common need, allowing us to seek, where appropriate, common policy approaches to all-island trade issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the cross-border collaboration between Queen's University Belfast and Dundalk Institute of Technology, to detail any steps her Department is taking to eliminate barriers, which may prevent students from studying on a cross-border basis.
answer_text: I am committed to ensuring that students are able to access opportunities to study anywhere on this island. My Department has been working to address barriers and has made significant progress across a range of areas. This includes the publication of improved guidance for students and the introduction of a tuition fee loan for postgraduate students from here studying in the south. Following the review by Universities Ireland, students from here are no longer required to achieve four top A-level grades to obtain the maximum number of CAO points. Both I, and my officials, continue to engage with our counterparts in DFHERIS to address other areas which impact all-island mobility including A-level equivalencies with CAO points, as well as language requirements for entry to southern institutions.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 34477/22-27, to detail the date on which they were last briefed by the Delivery Unit.
answer_text: We refer the Member to our response to AQW 34477/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement her Department has had with United Postal Service, to ensure it has a full understanding of its requirements, under the Windsor Framework.
answer_text: International postal services and the arrangements for the movement of consumer parcels under the Windsor Framework, are the responsibility of the British Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Utility Regulator (Support for Decarbonisation Preparation) Bill.
answer_text: I have received confirmation that the Bill is within the legislative competence of the Assembly. A remaining issue is being considered. Subject to Executive approval, the Department intends to introduce the Bill to the Assembly early next year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work her Department is undertaking to improve the Northern Ireland dispatch down rate.
answer_text: Officials are collaborating with the System Operator for Northern Ireland (SONI), NIE Networks, and the Utility Regulator to address Dispatch Down constraints through both policy development and ongoing engagement. My Department has set up a grid development monitoring group with SONI, NIE Networks and the Utility Regulator, with the aim of ensuring that key grid developments progress in a timely manner and in alignment with the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 targets.SONI has established a Dispatch Down Working group which monitors the progress of the ongoing Dispatch Down Action Plan, published by SONI in 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she last engaged with the Grid Development Monitoring Group.
answer_text: The Grid Development Monitoring Group is attended by senior officials on my behalf. It comprises of senior representatives from SONI, NIE Networks, the Utility Regulator, and officials from my Department.		The latest meeting of the Grid Development Monitoring Group was on the 25 November 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department has considered reviewing the current firm access methodology, for those who are wishing to invest in the electricity network.
answer_text: Connection access methodology is the remit of the network companies.  SONI’s Forward Work Plan for 2025/26 includes a plan consult on firm access in early 2026. Following this they will update the methodology for calculating Firm Access Quantity.The Utility Regulator in its Forward Work Plan has also kicked off preparation work for a potential firm access review in 2026-2027.  This project will scope out, set principles, and consider potential customer impacts, ahead of consulting on any amendments to the process for awarding firm access to large renewable generators in 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she will publish the terms and conditions for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme.
answer_text: Delivering additional renewable electricity generation is complex, demanding a careful balance between security of supply, affordability, and legislative compliance.My officials are prioritising the Primary Bill needed to make the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme operational. Publication of the Terms and Conditions, now expected later in 2026, will follow the introduction of the Bill to the Assembly and once all necessary arrangements are in place.I am confident that we are building the right foundations to incentivise sufficient electricity generation whilst ensuring the scheme delivers the best outcomes for energy consumers and that local communities benefit from the transition to net zero.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he has received Executive sign-off on the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.
answer_text: The draft Executive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy for public consultation was issued to my Executive colleagues on Friday 14 November; sign-off has not been received.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 33899/22-27.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 34478/22-27, to detail which Ministers (i) have engaged; (ii) and have not engaged, with the work of the Delivery Unit.
answer_text: All Ministers have engaged with the work of the Delivery Unit. The establishment of the Unit is a commitment within the Executive’s Programme for Government and Executive agreement on the Unit’s first priority area of focus (reducing unacceptably long health service waiting times) has been secured.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any actions the Delivery Unit has recommended to the Department of Health, to address health waiting lists.
answer_text: We refer the Member to our answer to AQW 33605/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he is aware of special needs school support staff positions being filled without Access NI checks in place.
answer_text: I am aware of the statement released by Unite the Union that incorrectly reported a 200% increase in the number of special needs school support staff positions that had been filled without Access NI checks.  If these figures had been quoted correctly, they would have shown a 87% reduction in outstanding Access NI checks.All school support staff are required to complete an Access NI check.  However, in the case of a short-term temporary hire or emergency cover appointment, it is not always possible to finalise the Access NI check prior to engagement.  In such situations, the Access NI forms must be completed on the first day of employment and the school principal is required to complete a risk assessment and take all necessary steps to ensure the safeguarding of children and young people.  This includes arranging for staff without an Access NI check to be supervised at all times by someone who is vetted to work in regulated activity, until the Access NI check is complete.  Given that the Education Authority process over 2,000 Access NI applications a month during peak periods, there will always be a number of checks that are ongoing at a particular point in time.  Staff cannot be paid until the Access NI check is complete, so there is an onus to have these checks processed as quickly as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will consider launching a review into the methodology used for the Rates Support Grant.
answer_text: My Department commissioned an independent review of the mechanism of the Rates Support Grant (RSG). Innovation and Consultancy Services in the Department of Finance carried out the review and provided their final report on 26th March 2025. The report concludes that RSG is still required, however, no major changes are proposed to the current RSG methodology. The report and the Department's response to the recommendations have been published on the DfC website - https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/publications/review-rates-support-grant-rsgMy Department will continue to work with councils on the actions required from the recommendations in the report.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail whether their Department has now matched the £1.5 million in funding provided by the Irish Government to the North West Regional Development Fund.
answer_text: We remain committed in our support for the Nort West Region.  The Northern Ireland Executive has allocated £130,000 to the North West Development Fund for the current year.  Our officials are continuing to work with Irish Government counterparts and Local Council officials to support the structures in the North West Region.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 27335/22-27, to detail how they plan to best utilise current structures, like the North West Strategic Growth Partnership, going forward.
answer_text: A key role of the North West Strategic Growth Partnership (NWSGP) is to harness the opportunities in the region and to support Local and Central Government to deliver the strategic priorities of the region.  The NWSGP, given its role and membership, is best placed to consider and determine how the structures, of which it is a core part, can support the transformative change required in the region.  Our officials will continue to support the current structures and participate in the NWSGP.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 27335/22-27, to detail when they last engaged with the Irish Government regarding the North West Regional Development Fund.
answer_text: We attended the 30th Plenary meeting of the North South Ministerial Council hosted by the Irish Government in Dublin on 17 October 2025 and the North West Development Fund (NWDF) was included in the papers discussed at that meeting.  Our officials engage on a regular basis with their Irish Government counterparts in relation to the NWDF.

Translink submitted a revised business case in September 2025 for the provision of additional services on the North West rail corridor. This is being considered by my officials.

answer_text: We receive regular written and verbal reports on the work of our Delivery Unit.

answer_text: My Department consulted in summer 2024 on how best to implement the new right to safe leave and plans to have legislation in place before the end of the current Assembly mandate to operationalise this important new right.

answer_text: There are no plans to reconfigure the delivery of any existing population screening programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any work his Department is undertaking to mitigate the risks posed to local people by the (i) elevated levels of ammonia in surface water; and (ii) Japanese knotweed, at the former Duncastle Road landfill site, Newbuildings.
answer_text: During a proactive survey in June 2025, elevated levels of ammonia in an adjacent waterway were recorded by my department. The site owners, Derry City and Strabane District Council, have been notified of the findings with a view to minimising the risk of recurrence.The risks posed from the presence of Japanese knotweed are non-existent if it is contained within the former landfill site. My department have made advice available to local councils and landowners.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide a timeline for the creation of any redress scheme established by the Inquiry (Mother and Baby Institutions, Magdalene Laundries and Workhouses) and Redress Scheme Bill.
answer_text: We are committed to a standardised payment scheme which provides initial financial redress to thousands of victims and survivors in a simple and supported way. The Redress Service will be formally created once the Inquiry (Mother and Baby Institutions, Magdalene Laundries and Workhouses) and Redress Scheme Bill becomes an Act. The scheme can open for applications once the associated secondary legislation is passed by the Assembly. As you will be aware, the Committee stage report is due in January 2026. We hope, subject to Assembly consideration and approval, the Bill will become an Act in mid 2026. While it is difficult to provide a definitive timeline until closer to the end of the primary legislation process, we are committed to the scheme opening as soon as possible. While the legislation is progressing, a Shadow Redress Service is being taken forward by the Department of Justice and key preparatory work for the scheme is underway, including policies and processes, IT system and applicant support model.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether her Department has considered commissioning an independent financial audit of the family court system.
answer_text: I refer the member to my answer to AQW 31836/22-27 dated 2 October 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail how his Department ensures that financial resources within the child protection system are used in a way that promotes the best interests of the child and aligns with relevant statutory and international obligations.
answer_text: The Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 provides the legislative framework within which Health and Social Care Trusts take steps to safeguard and protect children and young people. The Order reflects the core tenet outlined in Article 3 of the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child that in all actions concerning children, the best interest of the child shall be the paramount consideration. The Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 establishes a comprehensive legal framework for promoting the welfare of children and young people including the statutory duty resting with Health and Social Care Trusts to investigate and provide a range and level of personal social services appropriate to children at risk of significant harm. The Safeguarding Board for Northern Ireland’s procedures manual provides specific guidance upon the actions which must be taken when there are concerns about the welfare of a child/young person. My officials oversee how Health and Social Care Trusts meet these statutory responsibilities through the Statutory Function reporting process. This process provides a structured framework within which Trusts report upon their performance in meeting a broad suite of targets. The process facilitates a robust examination by Departmental officials of performance in each of the region’s Health and Social Care Trusts and affords an opportunity to identify gaps in performance to assist in the development of individual action plans.  My Department’s Support and Intervention Framework provides a platform for supporting Health and Social Care Trusts in addressing gaps in performance. It is the responsibility of Health and Social Care Trusts to make use of the financial resources available to them to meet their statutory obligations, including the financial obligation to breakeven. From an operational perspective, the task of protecting children and young people from significant harm and providing support to ensure that children reach their full potential is typified by a shared commitment between statutory and voluntary sector bodies to work collaboratively in providing support and enhancing levels of protection where this is required. Within this context other Departments and organisations play an important role in providing a range of additional services for children, young people and their families and in working alongside Health and Social Care Trusts in circumstances where children have been assessed to be at risk of significant harm.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what checks are in place to ensure that all life support systems in Health and Social Care Trusts are properly maintained.
answer_text: The Health and Social Care Trusts and the Northern Ireland Ambulance Service have confirmed that they have policies and procedures in place to ensure that life support systems are properly maintained.While arrangements vary between organisations the procedures include Planned Preventative Maintenance (PPM) contracts and in house PPM programmes. This work is carried out by appropriately qualified and competent external contractors, manufacturers or in house staff.The HSC Trusts and NIAS have incident management and reporting systems so that any faults or failures in life support systems are logged for repair.  Repair may be carried out by appropriately qualified and competent in-house staff or external sub-contractors.HSC Trusts employ professionally qualified clinical engineers and technicians who are responsible for monitoring and ensuring the maintenance of life support systems is carried out. In line with an organisation’s governance arrangements for policies and procedures routine and random compliance audits and assurance checks are undertaken. This may be through internal audit or external accreditation bodies.  Any non-compliances are reviewed and acted upon through the organisation’s governance structures.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he has been made aware of any issues around the maintenance of life support systems across all Health and Social Care Trusts, in the last five years.
answer_text: I have not been made aware of any issues on the maintenance of life support systems across the Health and Social Care Trusts in my time as Minister of Health.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the cost of ad hoc inspections on life support systems, in the last five years, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The Health and Social Care Trusts have advised that they would entail significant costs in collating this data. It is not easily extractable from finance systems without manual investigation into the various contract files.  Available data would be dependent on the type of contract in place for the maintenance of the equipment over the relevant time period. Ad hoc costs would not be able to be identified for fully comprehensive contracts. The Northern Ireland Ambulance Service have advised that the cost of ad hoc inspections to their patient monitoring and cardiac defibrillator devices between 2021 and present is approximately £154,000.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to provide an update on the progress of the Mobuoy Remediation Strategy.
answer_text: The public consultation on the draft Remediation Strategy for the Mobuoy site closed on 2 October 2025.NIEA are now carefully considering the consultation responses and will determine any necessary amendments to the draft Remediation Strategy. Any implementation plan and timetable for remediation remains subject to the outcome of the public consultation and will be considered in the context of the department’s responsibility to protect water quality and the challenging constraints of public finances.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 33082/22-27, whether they will provide a copy of the job description for each member of staff.
answer_text: Staff employed in the Delivery Unit are NICS general service administrative staff employed across a range of grades. Job descriptions and the work carried out are in line with the NICS HR Policies, TEO’s business plan and the Delivery Unit’s priority areas of focus.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how he plans to expand the Dental Access Scheme into (i) Derry city; and (ii) the Western Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: A Northern Ireland wide Dental Access Scheme (DAS) was launched on 1st August 2024 with the aim of improving access to health service dental care for unregistered patients experiencing an emergency, urgent or pressing oral health need during the working week.My Department issued two expressions of interest to all dental practices who treat health service patients to join the DAS, on the 28th May 2024 and 4th March 2025 respectively.  These expressions of interest have led to 37 dental practices participating in the DAS across Northern Ireland. There are 4 practices in the Western Local Commissioning Group (LCG) area, three in Limavady and one in Strabane. Unfortunately, there were no applications from any practices in the Derry/Londonderry city area nor in the Omagh and Fermanagh district council area, despite very attractive fees being paid through the scheme. Participation in the DAS is on a voluntary basis and my Department cannot compel practices to join the scheme.I am pleased to report that as of 31st October 2025, 34,321 patients have been treated under the scheme of which 4,682 of these patients were seen in the Western LCG area.The most recent monitoring report shows that activity has increased to such an extent that the expenditure has been consistently exceeding the allocated budget week on week and as a result no further applications are being sought at the moment. This will be kept under review.  Should any approaches from dental practices in the Derry/Londonderry city area or the Western LCG area be received, they will be given due consideration by my officials.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail which Ministers have engaged with the Delivery Unit, since it was established.
answer_text: In June, we secured Executive agreement that the Delivery Unit should focus initially on reducing the unacceptably long health service waiting times that impact on so many people in our society. The Unit has been engaging with Department of Health officials, who report to the Health Minister.  In addition, all Ministers have been invited to suggest other priority areas where they consider the Delivery Unit can help to drive meaningful change and improvement, and engagement has taken place at departmental official level to identify further potential priorities for Delivery Unit support.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail when they last engaged with the work of the Delivery Unit.
answer_text: The Delivery Unit is directly accountable to us as First Minister and deputy First Minister. We are briefed on its work on a regular basis.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the rationale as to why her Department does not consider moss on pavements to be a defect, under its current limited service road maintenance policy.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. As a result, my Department has had to introduce a “Limited Service” policy in respect to routine repairs and maintenance to roads and footways. The removal of moss is deemed to be of a low priority under the policy and is undertaken if and when resources are available.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether traders in Pump Street, Derry, will be offered rates mitigation, in light of the closure of the street, since 5 October 2025.
answer_text: The rating and valuation system is determined by established case law and a long-standing statutory framework that supports the stability of the tax base. Businesses affected may apply to the District Valuer requesting a review of their Net Annual Value (NAV) to determine if a temporary allowance is warranted. The degree to which the NAV of premises in a locality may be affected is based on the duration of the works required and the severity of impact on the business. In advance of applications from affected businesses, Land & Property Services (LPS) is currently gathering information on the recent events in the locality and has reached out to the owners of the damaged property and Derry City & Strabane District Council for further information and timescales of works.

answer_text: A total of 173 planning applications for renewable energy projects were pending as of 30 June 2025.  The length of time that those applications have been waiting for determination is contained within the table below. Renewable energy projects include applications for wind farms, single wind turbines, solar panels, hydroelectric applications, and biomass/anaerobic digesters (including landfill gas) as well as other applications for renewable energy including waste incineration and heat pumps. 75 out of the 103 applications which have been waiting for a planning decision for more than one year are for single wind turbines, a further 23 are ‘other’ renewable energy projects; all of these are  being processed by local planning authorities. question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she last met with the Energy Strategy Oversight Group.
answer_text: I meet regularly with the Chair of the Energy Strategy Oversight Group in relation to policy development and delivery of those aspects of energy that are within my remit.	In addition, I am briefed on the outcome of each Energy Strategy Action Plan and approve publication of a progress report at the end of each year alongside an action plan for the upcoming year. Ministers from contributing departments approve progress and actions relating to their own departmental remit prior to publication of the reports and action plans.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any immediate actions she will take following the Audit Office report into the Northern Ireland Energy Strategy.
answer_text: I acknowledge the Audit Office’s report on the approach and implementation of the NI Energy Strategy and will endeavour to swiftly implement its recommendations.	My Department’s ongoing Mid-Term Review of the Energy Strategy will be published by the end of this year and will highlight the significant progress achieved to date, and set out delivery milestones up to 2030, to maximise our capacity to realise these targets. 	I remain committed to the delivery of a net zero energy system powered by local renewables, smart infrastructure, and shaped by the people who live and work here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department will carry out a feasibility assessment of proposed actions, prior to publishing her Energy Strategy Action Plan.
answer_text: I acknowledge the Audit Office’s report on the approach and implementation of the NI Energy Strategy and will endeavour to swiftly implement its recommendations including that in relation to a feasibility assessment of proposed actions in annual action plans. 	I remain committed to the delivery of a net zero energy system powered by local renewables, smart infrastructure, and shaped by the people who live and work here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will commission a review of the effectiveness of governance and performance reporting arrangements, to ensure  (i) a sustained focus on delivery of planned actions; (ii) the achievement of key milestones; and (iii) the pace of progress, towards meeting her Energy Strategy key targets.
answer_text: I acknowledge the Audit Office’s report on the approach and implementation of the NI Energy Strategy and will endeavour to swiftly implement its recommendations.	The Department’s mid-term review of the Strategy is ongoing and aims to be published by the end of 2025. This review will highlight the significant progress achieved to date and set out further delivery milestones up to 2030.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the Audit Office report into the Northern Ireland Energy Strategy.
answer_text: I acknowledge the Northern Ireland Audit Office’s report on the approach and implementation of the NI Energy Strategy and will endeavour to swiftly implement its recommendations.The Department’s mid-term review of the Strategy is ongoing and aims to be published by the end of 2025. This review will highlight the significant progress achieved to date and set out further delivery milestones up to 2030. I remain committed to the delivery of a net zero energy system powered by local renewables, smart infrastructure, and shaped by the people who live and work here.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he has brought the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy to the Executive for agreement.
answer_text: I will bring forward a draft Early Learning and Childcare Strategy for Executive consideration and public consultation in the coming weeks.

answer_text: AQW 31975/22-27 was answered on 07 November 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 31977/22-27.
