question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline when the (i) terms of reference; and (ii) members, of the Review of Higher Education Funding, will be published.
answer_text: I have recently endorsed the draft Terms of Reference for the Review of Higher Education Funding and these have been shared with key stakeholders including the Committee, local Higher Education Institutions, as well as unions. All feedback, when received, will be considered before next steps are determined. I have also endorsed the approach to the Review and my officials are now taking steps to establish appropriate governance structures. Further information regarding these structures will be available in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any engagement his Department has had with the Delivery Unit.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Translink business case for service enhancements on the North West rail corridor.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail what engagement the Delivery Unit has with the Executive in order to inform Ministers of the work it is undertaking.
answer_text: The Delivery Unit is directly accountable to us as First Minister and deputy First Minister. We brought an Executive Paper to Executive Colleagues in June 2025, and the Executive Committee agreed to support the work of the Delivery Unit and to identify possible areas of focus. We are briefed on its work on a regular basis.  The Unit will also report to the Executive as required.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail what specific actions the Delivery Unit has recommended to the Department of Health, to address long health waiting lists.
answer_text: In June, we secured Executive agreement that the Delivery Unit should focus initially on reducing the waiting times. The Unit has been working closely with officials in the Department of Health to better understand the challenges of long waiting times, review current plans, and identify specific areas where it can support Health in improving delivery.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail what engagement the Minister of Health has had with the Delivery Unit, in the last six months.
answer_text: As agreed by the Executive, the Delivery Unit is currently focused on work to tackle health waiting times. The Unit has been engaging with Department of Health officials, who report to the Health Minister.  The Delivery Unit reports directly to the First Minister and deputy First Minister. Further Ministerial and official level engagement will be arranged as needed.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail what engagement the Minister for Infrastructure has had with the Delivery Unit, in the last six months.
answer_text: A Delivery Unit has been established to drive reform and transformation across government and wider public services. It will work across departments to help ensure that the Executive’s top priorities receive the scrutiny, support and attention required to make the greatest possible impact on people’s lives.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement she has had with the work of the Regional Balance Unit, over the last six months.
answer_text: Regional Balance is one of my four key priorities. I have regular engagement with the work of this unit as it continues to deliver on the actions set out in my Department’s Sub-Regional Economic Plan, in particular establishment of the Local Economic Partnerships and delivery of the Regional Balance Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she plans to establish a co-design group with local councils, Invest NI, Enterprise NI, and local enterprise agencies to develop a redesigned, evidence-led model of enterprise support to replace the current Go Succeed programme when it concludes in March 2026.
answer_text: My Department does not have a role in the administration of Go Succeed, which is funded through the Shared Prosperity Fund. It was anticipated that this funding would transfer to the Executive following the British Government’s Spending Review in June, and preparations were made to convene a co-design group accordingly.		However, the British Government has since assigned a role to the NIO. I have written to the Secretary of State seeking clarity on the future of this important programme. Unfortunately, that clarity has not yet been provided.		I share your concern about the importance of ensuring that our enterprise support infrastructure is robust and responsive.  I am continuing to press for clarification on future funding for Go Succeed and will keep stakeholders informed as the situation develops.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 31976/22-27, to detail the work that each member of staff is undertaking within the Regional Balance Unit.
answer_text: Given work pressures, staff cover a range of work areas and it is not possible to give a definitive list of individual staff responsibilities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 32955/22-27, to detail how Invest NI is expanding its capacity and capability within its regional Derry office.
answer_text: Invest NI is expanding its capacity and capability across its regional network, with a target to increase the number of staff working in its regional offices by 40%. Recruitment is underway for a new Head of Regional Business for the North West, who will lead on delivery of the Invest NI business strategy in the area. A new Client Officer for the North West has recently been appointed and a new Client Executive is in the process of being recruited. Further appointments are expected to be made as Invest NI continues to implement its new regional approach.    Invest NI is also committed to increasing the number of businesses that are managed through its regional offices and is in the process of implementing a new approach where client companies, with a turnover of up to £2m, will now be handled by the teams in these local offices.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 30954/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 30954/22-27 was answered on 16 December 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 31414/22-27, for her assessment of the number of Invest NI staff employed in Derry.
answer_text: Invest NI is implementing a new strategic and operational approach to deliver my Regional Balance objectives. This includes the development of a new regional structure, a new regional operating model, and increased resourcing of its regional teams across the North. In the North-West, recruitment is underway for a new Head of Regional Business, who will lead on delivery of Invest NI’s regional strategy. A new Client Officer for the North-West has been appointed, and a new Client Executive is currently being recruited.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of staff within their Department, who are allocated to the Delivery Unit.
answer_text: In line with best practice and learning from other jurisdictions, our Delivery Unit is intentionally small and agile with a current complement of four full-time staff. It is currently focused on work to tackle hospital waiting times, as agreed by the Executive. The Unit will remain flexible and responsive to any additional priority areas of focus determined by the Executive, and there will be scope to expand the team as its remit grows.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 31414/22-27, to outline what plans her Department has in place to increase the number of people employed for Invest NI in Derry.
answer_text: Invest NI currently employs 13 staff which are based in its North West office in Derry. Invest NI published its new strategy last year which is aligned with the Department for the Economy and Ministerial policy, and delivery has already commenced. As part of this strategy Invest NI is implementing a new regional approach which involves, developing a new regional structure, a new regional operating model, and increased resourcing of its regional teams across the North. To deliver against the strategy Invest NI is in the process of increasing its headcount by 40% across all its regional offices, leading to 20 additional staff.Recruitment is underway for a new Head of Regional Business for the North West, who will lead on delivery of the Invest NI business strategy in the area. A new Client Officer for the North West has been appointed and a new Client Executive is in the process of being recruited. Further appointments are expected to be made as Invest NI continues to implement its new regional approach.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 31417/22-27, to specifically detail the amount of the £16 million investment by Allstate will be invested in its operation in Derry.
answer_text: The recently announced £16 million Allstate investment is across all Northern operations and all of their locations in the region.  The specific amount to be invested in its operation in Derry is not available.  I refer you to the response provided previously to AQW 31417 22/27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 30335/22-27, to detail why Translink submitted a revised business case for the provision of additional services to the North West rail corridor.
answer_text: Translink submitted a revised business case in September 2025 for the provision of additional services on the North West rail corridor to ensure that the predicated costs and revenue projections are up to date and accurate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the Assembly motion on addressing regional imbalance, approved on 6 October 2025, to detail the steps she will take to ensure all Executive Departments contribute to the successful implementation of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, including any proposals that aim to strengthen accountability where regional balance is not being delivered.
answer_text: The Sub-Regional Economic Plan contained actions for my Department, including establishing 11 Local Economic Partnerships and creating a £45m Regional Balance Fund to support them. Additionally, regional balance has been raised as a whole government issue within the new Programme for Government (PfG). A PfG Delivery Report will be published by the Executive on an annual basis. This report will set out progress made towards the PfG Priority “A Globally Competitive and Sustainable Economy”. This will include an update on work related to regional balance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the Assembly motion on addressing regional imbalance, approved on 6 October 2025, when she will publish an assessment on the progress of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: I highlighted the progress made on the Sub-Regional Economic Plan in the Assembly on 6 October 2025.This includes establishing 11 Local Economic Partnerships and creating a £45m Regional Balance Fund to support them.  Invest NI has set a regional balance target for the first time ever and is expanding its capacity and capability across its regional offices. This work is underway, and real progress is being made. A further update on the progress of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan will be provided as part of my Department’s Year Two Progress Report, which will be published early next year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current doctor to patient ratio, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: The latest available information on GP to patient ratios, outlined by constituency area, can be found in the table below. Number of GPs (headcount) per 100,000 registered patients at 30th June 2025Assembly Area (2008)GPs per 100,000 Registered PatientsBELFAST EAST85BELFAST NORTH82BELFAST SOUTH72BELFAST WEST71EAST ANTRIM92EAST LONDONDERRY70FERMANAGH AND SOUTH TYRONE57FOYLE62LAGAN VALLEY72MID ULSTER67NEWRY AND ARMAGH65NORTH ANTRIM78NORTH DOWN76SOUTH ANTRIM69SOUTH DOWN70STRANGFORD77UPPER BANN70WEST TYRONE51Northern Ireland71GP numbers are based on headcount and do not reflect differences in their working patterns.The figures presented for GPs refer to Unrestricted Principals or Equivalents (UPEs), Salaried GPs and Retainers and do not include Locum GPs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of train faults in the last two years, broken down by route.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:The following table outlines the number of train failures resulting in 30+ minute delays, by line, over the past 2 years (02/10/2023 to 28/09/2025): BangorLinePortadownLineLarneLineDerryLinePortrushBranchCross-Border LineTOTALNetworkTrain Faults >30 minutes0241124261Total No. of Services59,54569,16345,43026,85326,06215,228242,281Faults as a % of Services0.000%0.003%0.009%0.041%0.008%0.276%0.025%For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount of spend allocated to rail stock, over the last five years.
answer_text: My Department has invested £38.3m on rail rolling stock in the last five years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when she will answer AQW 30335/22-27.
answer_text: I answered AQW 30335/22-27 on 7 October 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the remaining life of the existing rail stock; (ii) when new rail stock will be ordered; and (iii) when the new rail stock will be in operation.
answer_text: The Enterprise Train sets are due to be replaced in six years. The Class 3000 trains have an expected life exceeding 10 years, and the Class 4000 trains have a life exceeding 16 years. The procurement for the new Enterprise Train Fleet is currently ongoing, with the new trains scheduled to enter service by 2031.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide an update on the redevelopment of building 30, Ebrington Square.
answer_text: Outstanding matters regarding the lease for Building 30, Ebrington Square are currently sitting with our tenant, Ebrington Holdings Ltd, for progression. Officials are continuing to monitor the situation to ensure that any remaining legal work can be completed as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the number of times train faults have disrupted services on the Derry to Belfast line, over the last two years; and (ii) Translink's area of responsibility on the Belfast to Dublin Enterprise service.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:(i) The number of times train faults have disrupted services on the Derry to Belfast line, over the last two yearsThere have been 26,853 train services on the Derry to Belfast corridor in the past 2 years.  There have been just 11 train mechanical defects, where the train has been delayed by over 30 minutes.   This equated to 0.04% of all services. (ii) Translink's area of responsibility on the Belfast to Dublin Enterprise serviceNI Railways jointly operates 15 return journeys per day with Iarnród Éireann, and they are operated jointly utilising a shared fleet and shared train crew. Translink maintains the infrastructure between Belfast and the Border whilst Iarnród Eireann maintains the infrastructure between the Border and Dublin.  The fleet is jointly maintained between Translink and Iarnród Eireann Fleet maintenance departments. Translink also shares a booking system for tickets on Enterprise services with Iarnród Éireann. Both companies’ websites have the facility to book journeys in advance and tickets are also available from Translink stations and onboard services.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement her Department had with the UK Government in advance of the announcement of the proposed Digital ID scheme.
answer_text: My Department did not have any prior engagement with the British Government on the proposed Digital ID scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail when the Department intends to introduce legislation to ensure fire safety in high-rise residential buildings, including social housing, in order to bring Northern Ireland in line with post-Grenfell standards in England.
answer_text: I am currently finalising work on building safety policy and subject to Executive agreement, my Department intends to introduce a Building Safety Bill early in the next mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, given that patients from the Western Health and Social Care Trust currently travel to Belfast while fasting and often seriously ill, whether he plans to provide PET scanning facilities at Altnagelvin Area Hospital.
answer_text: My Department continues to work with Trusts to increase imaging capacity, however the scale and pace of expansion is subject to the availability of both capital and revenue funding. Over the period 2021/22 to current year, more than £33 million of capital funding was prioritised for imaging and diagnostics equipment and related enabling works across the HSC. Additional projects for regional imaging diagnostic services have been added to my Department’s draft 10-year Capital Investment Plan. This draft plan includes the proposal to install a PET scanner in the North West Cancer Centre, to complement the PET service already provided by Belfast Trust. At present, the draft capital investment plan has an installation date for 2027/28.  Implementation however, is dependent on prioritisation of the scheme on the plan and allocation of associated revenue funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the reason controlled schools are not permitted greater flexibility in sourcing materials at best value; and (ii) whether he will review the Education Authority’s procurement framework to ensure it delivers value for money for all schools.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is designated as the Centre of Procurement Expertise (CoPE) for the education sector.  This designation means that the EA holds direct responsibility for the procurement of goods and services on behalf of schools. As a public body, the EA, along with all budget holders, including controlled and maintained schools, is required to adhere to the Northern Ireland Executive’s Procurement Policy Statement, Northern Ireland Procurement Policy Notes (PPNs), the Northern Ireland Procurement Act 2023 and the Procurement Regulations 2024.I recently endorsed the update to Procurement Policy Note 04/21, revised in March 2025, that increased the thresholds for lower value non-contract purchases. These changes are intended to provide schools with greater flexibility and autonomy. Specifically:the threshold requiring evidence of two price checks has increased from £5,000 to £10,000.andthe range requiring three written quotations has been revised to apply to purchases between £10,000 and £50,000.These revised thresholds offer discretion in managing non-contract procurement within their delegated budgets, supporting clearer and more flexible decision making. In recognition of the need for greater flexibility, I have instructed Officials in my Department and the EA to explore options to enhance how schools engage with existing frameworks to ensure value for money for all schools. This includes a potential pilot initiative that would allow schools increased autonomy in purchasing low-value consumables within their delegated budgets. Under this pilot, schools would have the choice to procure items either through EA established frameworks or from alternative suppliers, where appropriate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Translink business case for service enhancements on the North West rail corridor.
answer_text: I fully recognise the need to address regional imbalance and better connect our communities including those within the Northwest. In September Translink submitted a business case to enhance services to the Northwest which my officials are considering.However, any decision to implement any of these service changes will need to take account of the financial pressures on my Department’s budget.The funding of an effective public transport service is a key priority for my department. While I am keen to deliver what I can within the remainder of my mandate, I will need to consider which areas of the Department to prioritise as funding opportunities become available. This is likely to require some difficult decisions and bearing down on costs will be essential to protect essential public service delivery.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Investment Strategy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Investment Strategy remains under consideration and we hope to be able to bring it to the Executive in the near future. The timeline for approval and publication of it will then be a matter for the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps his Department will take to achieve regional balance in its policies, strategies and plans.
answer_text: My Department seeks to achieve regional balance across its remit by ensuring that strategies, polices and plans are underpinned by robust evidence on need and compliant with relevant statutory obligations, including section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and the Rural Needs Act (NI) 2016. The Department’s Economic and Social Research Programme and core analytical work informs consideration of regional issues and is committed to evidenced-based decision-making and strengthening the evidence base on the challenges we face.   The Departmental Business Plan 2025/26 outlines the key activities the Department aims to deliver during the current financial year.  Developed with regard to the Department’s constrained budgetary position, the Business Plan prioritises the provision of continued support to meet the needs of people, families and communities across the whole of Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide an update on the work of the Regional Balance Unit.
answer_text: The work of the Regional Balance Unit is focused on the operation of the Local Economic Partnerships (LEPs) and delivery of £45 million in funding to support the work of the LEPs. This is a key part of delivering Regional Balance, as set out in the Sub-Regional Economic Plan. All 11 LEPs have been established and are working at pace to develop and refine action plans designed to address locally identified priorities and needs and are at differing stages of development. Some of these are well developed and will be going through approval processes within the relevant Council before being submitted to the Department for approval.  Others continue to identify, develop and refine projects and priorities. Officials from the Regional Balance Unit sit on each LEP and provide advice, support and guidance to the Council and the LEP in a range of areas related to this work. The responsible division also takes forward work on entrepreneurship and social economy which are important strategic priorities for delivering improved regional balance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of staff allocated to the Regional Balance Unit.
answer_text: While 10 staff are allocated to the work of the Regional Balance Unit, promoting regional balance is part of everyone’s work across the Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the specific measurements and indices used within his Department to monitor progress towards ensuring they are meeting the Executive's target to build an economy where everyone has a fair chance, can participate equally, and has the best possible opportunity to succeed, as outlined in the Programme for Government 2024-2027.
answer_text: My Department does not own the economy priority in the Programme for Government, nor do we provide any targets towards it.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she will answer AQW 30694/22-27.
answer_text: The answer issued on 26 September 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what consideration he has given to enabling patients with endometriosis, including those requiring urgent referral, to access treatment across multiple Health and Social Care Trusts, to reduce waiting times for specialist assessment and care.
answer_text: There is currently considerable work underway across the health and care system to try to reduce waiting times and develop pathways for gynaecology specific conditions such as endometriosis in each Trust across Northern Ireland.The Department has established a Regional HSC Gynaecology Services Forum, with Trusts, the Public Health Agency, and General Practitioner representation. This forum is overseeing ongoing service improvement, and the implementation and development of gynaecology service pathways at primary, intermediate, secondary and tertiary care levels. as well as overseeing the implementation of the “Getting It Right First Time” (GIRFT) Gynaecology Report. An Endometriosis Clinical Pathway Task and Finish group has been established which is working to develop a pathway for endometriosis, for patients that have minor, intermediate or severe diagnosis.  The development of a pathway will ensure that services are available to meet the needs of women at specific aspects of the condition’s progression. The pathway developed will be standardised across NI, and will ensure that the duplication of services is minimised.My officials are also working to establish a Regional Engagement Forum, this will allow the Department to garner expert user feedback from stakeholders.Additional funding has also been approved non-recurrently for waiting list initiatives (both independent sector and in-house) to help reduce waiting times for patients requiring red flag and other time critical assessments and treatments across a range of specialties, including gynaecology. Although I acknowledge that the current financial position may make progress difficult for non-red flag or time critical assessments and treatment such as endometriosis, unless clinically indicated.My officials will continue to explore options to strengthen pathways to improve care for women suffering from endometriosis and other gynaecology conditions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the mechanisms in place to allow individuals to raise concerns about the use of public funds within the family court system.
answer_text: There are a number of mechanisms in place to ensure that concerns can be raised and addressed. My Department has published the DoJ ‘Raising a Concern’ Policy, and this specifically states that if the concern is in relation to possible fraud the Department will deal with it under our Fraud Policy and Fraud Response Plan. In respect of the Legal Services Agency, the Agency’s webpage indicates that an individual who wishes to raise an objection to the grant of legal aid or to provide the Agency with copies of pertinent third-party correspondence should do so by contacting enquiries@lsani.gov.uk.The Agency’s webpage also indicates that if an individual suspects any abuse of the legal aid system, they may contact the Agency’s dedicated counter-fraud hotline to report concerns:Counter-Fraud Hotline – 08003287360Email Address - counterfraud@lsani.gov.ukIn addition, the NI Direct website has an online legal aid fraud reporting facility which also provides the option to report a concern anonymously. The fraud reporting portal is available at Report legal aid fraud online | nidirect. The Agency has published its Internal Fraud Referral Policy which sets out how the Agency investigates and refers potential or suspected fraud to the appropriate authorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what percentage of the overall health and social care budget has been allocated to children’s social services, including foster placements, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The percentage of the overall health and social care budget that has been allocated to children’s social services over the last 5 years, rounded to the nearest whole percentage, is set out in the following table. 2021/ 2022 2022/ 2023 2023/ 2024 2024/ 20252025/ 2026 Children's Social Care Budget per SRF (£m)313330357397406% of Overall Budget that is Children's Social Care Budget5%5%5%5%5%This information is based upon the Strategic Resources Framework (SRF) document which is produced to represent opening annual recurrent budget allocation levels and associated planned investment across Programmes of Care by HSC Trusts, SPPG and the PHA.  As such this will include not only direct expenditure, but also an apportionment of other costs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail how his Department ensures that financial resources within the child protection system are used in a way that promotes the best interests of the child and aligns with relevant statutory and international obligations.
answer_text: The Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 provides the legislative framework within which Health and Social Care Trusts take steps to safeguard and protect children and young people. The Order reflects the core tenet outlined in Article 3 of the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child that in all actions concerning children, the best interest of the child shall be the paramount consideration. The Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 establishes a comprehensive legal framework for promoting the welfare of children and young people including the statutory duty resting with Health and Social Care Trusts to investigate and provide a range and level of personal social services appropriate to children at risk of significant harm. The Safeguarding Board for Northern Ireland’s procedures manual provides specific guidance upon the actions which must be taken when there are concerns about the welfare of a child/young person. My officials oversee how Health and Social Care Trusts meet these statutory responsibilities through the Statutory Function reporting process. This process provides a structured framework within which Trusts report upon their performance in meeting a broad suite of targets. The process facilitates a robust examination by Departmental officials of performance in each of the region’s Health and Social Care Trusts and affords an opportunity to identify gaps in performance to assist in the development of individual action plans.  My Department’s Support and Intervention Framework provides a platform for supporting Health and Social Care Trusts in addressing gaps in performance. It is the responsibility of Health and Social Care Trusts to make use of the financial resources available to them to meet their statutory obligations, including the financial obligation to breakeven. From an operational perspective, the task of protecting children and young people from significant harm and providing support to ensure that children reach their full potential is typified by a shared commitment between statutory and voluntary sector bodies to work collaboratively in providing support and enhancing levels of protection where this is required. Within this context other Departments and organisations play an important role in providing a range of additional services for children, young people and their families and in working alongside Health and Social Care Trusts in circumstances where children have been assessed to be at risk of significant harm.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether her Department has considered commissioning an independent financial audit of the family court system.
answer_text: Having recently launched my Enabling Access to Justice Programme, I have no current plans to commission an independent financial audit of the family court system.	The Programme was informed by a wide range of evidence including previous reviews and existing data; the experience of other jurisdictions including those which have different delivery models and deliver at lower cost; a call for evidence on the Foundational Review of Civil Legal Aid; research commissioned by the Department under section 29 of the Domestic Abuse and Civil Proceedings Act; and a priority setting exercise for victims of domestic abuse I jointly initiated with the Commissioner designate for Victims of Crime. That evidence has informed proposals which seek to ensure the justice system is accessible and fair, proportionate and responsive and, therefore, to address concerns regarding the financial and other costs of family proceedings by focusing resources on supporting the most vulnerable; addressing need at the earliest point; incentivising and supporting early resolution; ensuring the system and public legal services are not weaponised to perpetrate abuse and that the principle of equality of arms is supported.  Those objectives are key to ensuring the long-term viability of the system, that costs are not a barrier to justice and that advice and representation is available for the most vulnerable who cannot navigate the system alone.  I consulted earlier this year on the Programme Delivery Plan which includes a number of actions to that end including piloting alternative early resolution, introducing specialist support for specific vulnerable groups including victims of domestic abuse, reviewing the scope of legal aid and reviewing financial eligibility rules and merits testing for legal aid. Responses to the consultation on the programme Delivery Plan are currently being considered and it is anticipated that an updated plan will be published in the coming weeks.It is also important to note that most of the policy and legislative frameworks concerning family courts in Northern Ireland are the responsibility of the Departments of Finance and Health.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether an assessment has been made of the impact, the delay of the first auction of the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee, will have on the Executive's ability to meet its climate change targets.
answer_text: Delivering additional renewable electricity generation is complex, demanding a careful balance between security of supply, affordability, and legislative compliance.It requires joined-up working, across government and with our delivery partners. My Department does not hold all the levers, so collaboration is essential to unlock progress.The Energy Strategy Action Plan 2025 outlines a range of actions my Department is taking forward this year to increase the levels of renewable electricity generation and support delivery of climate change targets.I am confident that we are building the right foundations to ensure the scheme delivers the best outcomes for energy consumers and that local communities benefit from the transition to net zero.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline what assessment has been made of any risk to the necessary legislation for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee, not being passed within the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: My Department is targeting to introduce the required primary legislation for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) to the Assembly before the end of this year. Once the primary Bill is in place, subordinate legislation will be taken forward to set out the detailed arrangements. All legislative change is subject to Assembly process and scrutiny. Any associated risk is therefore considered within the wider risk assessment for all projects and policy development processes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the steps her Department is taking to support local business, impacted by changes to legal migration rules for family and work visas in December 2024.
answer_text: My Department continues to push the British Government for mitigations to the current immigration rules, recognising the unique economic environment of sharing a border with an EU member state and the case for lower salary thresholds in the region. Officials are currently engaged with the Migration Advisory Committee and their ongoing reviews of the Temporary Shortage List and salary thresholds, which will provide analysis of visa terms and conditions and eligible job roles. Additionally, the Department has a range of strategies designed to directly support local businesses, namely the SME Productivity Booster, Assured Skills Academies, Skills Focus, InnovateUs, and Higher-Level Apprenticeships.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline when she intends to introduce the necessary legislation for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee to the Assembly.
answer_text: My Department is targeting to introduce the required primary legislation for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) to the Assembly before the end of this year. Once the primary Bill is in place, subordinate legislation will be taken forward to set out the detailed arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the rationale for the delay in launching the first Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee auction, which is set to take place in the first quarter of 2027, when it was previously meant to take place in late 2026.
answer_text: The Final Scheme Design for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) was published on 19 September 2025.Delivering additional renewable electricity generation is complex, demanding a careful balance between security of supply, affordability, and legislative compliance.I am confident that we are building the right foundations to ensure the scheme delivers the best outcomes for energy consumers and that local communities benefit from the transition to net zero.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he will present his proposals for an Independent Environmental Protection Agency to the Executive.
answer_text: The final report from the independent panel, setting out its proposals to strengthen environmental governance in Northern Ireland, is expected to be finalised in the coming weeks, following which, I plan to bring my proposed way forward to the Executive.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount spent by Invest NI in the Foyle constituency, in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: During the year 2024-25 financial year, Invest NI offered support to businesses within the Foyle Parliamentary Constituency Area totalling £16.80m.	For the Member’s future reference, Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of financial support offered to every business over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal which can be accessed from the following link: Invest NI Open Data.

answer_text: Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of support offered to businesses over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal. question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of the £16 million investment by Allstate going towards its operation in Derry.
answer_text: The recently announced £16 million Allstate investment is across all its operations in the North. With around one third of Allstate’s workforce based in Derry and the North West, this investment will have a positive impact for Allstate’s NW region’s workforce, putting it at the heart of its global digital transformation.  As the company evolves into a digital centre of excellence, Allstate remains deeply committed to the region and the strong talent pool in the North-West.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any action her Department is taking to improve (i) productivity; and (ii) export intensity, in light of the findings of the Ulster University Economic Policy Centre report, Delivering Balanced Regional Economic Growth in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I spoke at the launch of the UU Economic Policy Centre report earlier this year and I welcome its analysis and conclusions, which underline the importance of delivering my regional balance priority. In line with its findings, I am committed to supporting place-based interventions with local leadership through the Local Economic Partnerships, supported by the £45 million Regional Balance Fund.  The Partnerships bring together all regional partners and stakeholders to identify the specific needs of a region and build bottom-up solutions and interventions that can transform our regions over the long term.  Eleven Partnerships have now been set up across the North. I will look to augment and align future initiatives to maximise the regional impact of wider funding and interventions, including City and Growth Deals, Enhanced Investment Zone initiatives and support offered through my department and its key delivery bodies, such as Invest NI. The Local Economic Partnerships are currently in the process of developing action plans for their area.  This approach ensures that the Partnerships have the flexibility to identify the local issues that are inhibitors to productivity growth rather than setting a common programme of activities for all areas. Invest NI has productivity improvement as a central thread in its current business strategy, and it delivers several targeted, high-impact productivity programmes and initiatives focussed on: operational excellence; supply chain; leadership and capability development; the Cluster Acceleration Programme; and a range of programmes to promote innovation, research and development. Invest NI is also bringing forward enhanced capital investment programmes to drive transformative investments across the north.  In July 2024 the £46m Agri-Food Investment initiative was launched to target improved productivity in our agri-food and drink sector, which is a critical sector in supporting regional balance given its spread across the north.  Building on this initiative, Invest NI is now working on a new Productive Investment Initiative that will help all other manufacturing companies undertake the productive capital investment needed to maximise their efficiency, profitability and long-term sustainability and success. Improving our skills profile across the North is also an important determinant of productivity growth, and the work of the regional further education colleges continues to have a significant impact in their local areas.  Programmes such as the SME Productivity Booster and InnovateUs are also supporting many SMEs to improve their productivity with the right training for employees and support to grow market share through innovative products and practices. In relation to supporting greater trade intensity, businesses across the north benefit from unique dual market access, enabling seamless, tariff-free trade with both Britain and the European Union.  This distinctive position continues to underpin our efforts to s

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the steps she is taking to ensure Invest NI will meet its target of delivering 65 per cent of its investments outside the Belfast Metropolitan area by the end of 2026-27.
answer_text: As part its new Business Strategy, Invest NI is implementing a new regional approach. This has included implementing a new 5 region model, increasing investment in its regional teams, enhanced promotion programmes of support across the regions, working with councils and partners to deliver pipeline of businesses ‘new to Invest NI’ and working collaboratively through new Local Economic Partnerships.  This new approach is designed to increase regional economic activity and foreign direct investment across the North. These measures will contribute to helping Invest NI deliver against the objective of improving regional balance and delivering 65 percent of its investments outside of the Belfast Metropolitan area by the final year of its current strategy period, 2026/27

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the timeline of when he decided which applications would receive stadia funding from the Northern Ireland Football Fund.
answer_text: I selected the applicants in the Prioritised Cohort on 14th August 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will make public the papers he received for consideration during the decision making process for the awarding of stadia funding from the Northern Ireland Football Fund.
answer_text: I will consider requests for any information made in line with terms of the relevant legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she was made aware that a member of the board of Invest NI had resigned in June 2025.
answer_text: I was informed of the resignation of a member from the Board of Invest NI on 10 July 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) to detail any factors, in addition to the published application scoring system, which were applied in determining which clubs progressed through the Northern Ireland Football Fund process for stadia funding; and (ii) to publish (a) the scoring breakdown; (b) any additional criteria; and (c) the rationale, applied in each case.
answer_text: No additional factors were applied in determining which clubs progressed through to the next stage of the programme. Each applicant was scored solely on the information provided in their application against the published scoring criteria. 	I will consider publishing the information requested in line with terms of the relevant legislation. Application Guidance and the Scoring Matrix is still available online on the Department for Communities website The Northern Ireland Football Fund Performance Programme documents | Department for Communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of jobs created by foreign direct investment in Derry, in each of the last five years.

The portal contains the following information which will facilitate access to the data requested:Portal | Invest NI Total Tables 1 April 2020 and 31 March 2025Provides a complete picture of Invest NI support through summary tables that aggregate both published and non-published data (indirect support, like advice and guidance, and offers that cannot be released due to data protection issues). Does not include indirect support, like advice and guidance, or offers that cannot be released due to data protection issues.Portal | Invest NI Open Data GuidanceProvides guidance on the data contained in 1 and 2 above.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline any work her Department is undertaking to increase (i) export intensity; and (ii) the number of firms that export.
answer_text: I am working with my department’s main economic delivery bodies, InterTradeIreland and Invest NI to maximise the north’s dual market access arrangements to grow the export base wider and deeper. Both organisations are taking forward a range of activity in that regard. For example, last year InterTradeIreland launched its First-Time Exporters Accelerator, a fully funded programme providing mentoring, strategy development, market analysis and networking to support SMEs taking their first steps into exporting.Since April, Invest NI has delivered 13 international trade missions and exhibitions, supporting 97 businesses across multiple sectors. It also offers tailored programmes such as Grow Beyond and Going Dutch to help early-stage exporters and SMEs increase export intensity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of foreign direct investment secured by Invest NI, within the last six months.
answer_text: Invest NI reports its performance on a financial year basis with 2024-25, ending 31st March 2025, being the most recent year available.  Over the period 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2025 Invest NI secured investment commitments of £266m through inward investment projects.  These investment commitments are a combination of business investment and assistance offered by Invest NI in support of a business’s project. For the Member’s future reference, Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of financial support offered to every business over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal which can be accessed from the following link: Invest NI Open Data.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline the work of the Junior Ministers.
answer_text: Junior Ministers have an important role in supporting the work of the First Minister and deputy First Minister across our departmental portfolio. They are appointed under a Determination made in accordance with Section 19 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and which specifies that the function of the Junior Ministers is to assist the First Minister and deputy First Minister in the exercise of their functions in relation to the Executive Office.  They may therefore be involved in all aspects of the department’s work.  Junior Ministers have specific responsibility for taking the Inquiry (Mother and Baby Institutions, Magdalene Laundries and Workhouses) and Redress Scheme Bill through its Assembly legislative stages.  They also chair the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Oversight Board. The Board reviews the implementation of the Strategic Framework, related Delivery Plans, and manages interfaces across government Departments and the public sector. In addition, Junior Ministers work has included two visits to the Executive Office in Brussels, speaking to Motions in the Assembly and answering Oral Questions during Question Time to the First Minister and deputy First Minister, attending Business Committee on behalf of the Executive, and meeting with stakeholders associated with key Executive objectives such as Good Relations projects and the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls strategy.  They regularly engage in significant work with NSMC and BIC Institutional bodies.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether any consideration has been given to prioritising areas contained within the Inquiry (Mother and Baby Institutions, Magdalene Laundries and Workhouses) and Redress Scheme Bill to ensure a contingency plan is in place should the inquiry take longer than three years.
answer_text: Victims and survivors have waited many years for a public inquiry to help us all better understand what happened, why it happened and who was responsible. The inquiry needs to be as efficient as possible given the age of many of those who have been affected by the institutions. The public inquiry will have the benefit of the work we have commissioned PRONI to do, which has digitised over 5,500 records. Additionally, the Independent Panel led the collection of over 230 new victims and survivors’ testimonies. This should make the public inquiry more timely, more coherent and focused, if the current scope of the legislation is maintained, with its existing in-built flexibility also present. The inquiry will in part be driven by the number of people who chose to come forward, which is difficult to estimate at this point. More generally, public inquiries can take a significant period of time, as there are always legal and operational considerations which need to be considered, but a key safeguard is a deliverable scope, otherwise there are time, cost and fairness implications.Operational aspects are already being considered and taken forward to make sure the inquiry can make good progress once the legislation has passed. We remain committed to a timely inquiry to help survivors get the closure they deserve.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the work of the Derry City and Strabane Local Economic Partnership.
answer_text: Derry City and Strabane Local Economic Partnership have met several times and have made excellent progress. A draft action plan has been developed, and Council officials are actively working with Department officials towards the finalisation of same.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) the number of meetings the Delivery Unit has held to date; and (ii) which of these meetings they have attended.
answer_text: Delivery Unit officials continue to engage with external stakeholders to explore the operation of and learning from similar units in other jurisdictions, and to refine the Unit’s operating model and specific areas of focus. We receive regular updates and briefing from our officials on the work of the Unit, in accordance with the commitment within the PfG.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department has an Interconnector policy.
answer_text: Research has been commissioned to provide evidence for the development of interconnection and storage policy here. This research is expected to be completed shortly. The output of this research will inform the development of both interconnector and energy storage policy. My Department plans to develop interconnection policy by Q4 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the terms of reference for the Review of Higher Education Funding will be published.
answer_text: I have recently endorsed the draft Terms of Reference for the Review of Higher Education Funding and these have been shared with key stakeholders including the Committee, local Higher Education Institutions, as well as unions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 28331/22-27, to detail whether school enhancement money is included in this data.
answer_text: Expenditure in relation to school enhancement projects was not included in the response to AQW 28331/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Review of Higher Education Funding.
answer_text: I have recently endorsed the draft Terms of Reference for the Review of Higher Education Funding and these have been shared with key stakeholders including the Committee, local Higher Education Institutions, as well as unions.	The first stage of the Review, a Financial Needs Assessment of our Higher Education Institutions is underway and due to be completed during the autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether it is still her intention to introduce her Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill, in full, by the end of the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: Yes

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide an update on the North South Interconnector.
answer_text: NIE Networks commenced construction works on the North South Interconnector in Quarter 4 of 2024. The Judicial Review concerning the discharge of planning conditions for the North South Interconnector was dismissed with consent on the 8th of August 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement she has had with the British Government in relation to United States of America military planes stopping at any airport in Northern Ireland before travelling to Israel.
answer_text: My Department has no information in relation to this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any engagement her Department has had with the PSNI, within the last six months, in regard to addressing street drinkers.
answer_text: My Department has not had any engagement with the PSNI on the specific issue of addressing street drinkers within the last six months. Addressing this issue is an operational matter for the PSNI which is operationally independent from both the Justice Minister and the Department of Justice and is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board. I know that the PSNI work closely with wider partners in the statutory and voluntary sector, and with local councils, and have established protocols to ensure vulnerable people are directed to appropriate and available support, as well as ensuring the safety of the wider public.On the wider issue of drinking in public, and the legislative framework used to tackle same, I have recently sought the Chief Constable’s views on a joint DoJ/DfC proposal and am also aware that DfC is currently seeking feedback from councils on this issue. This engagement is ongoing with a view to finalising a solution to this long running issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Renewable Electricity Support Scheme.
answer_text: The introduction of the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme is crucial in enabling delivery of additional renewable electricity capacity and I plan to bring primary legislation before the Assembly this year, with subordinate regulations to follow as we move towards implementation. The upcoming publication on the final scheme design, which will publish imminently, will provide a full update on the roadmap, eligibility criteria, delivery roles and responsibilities, and an outline of the contract allocation process.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the work of the Delivery Unit.
answer_text: In June, we secured Executive agreement that the Delivery Unit should focus initially on reducing the unacceptably long health waiting times that impact on so many people in our society. Since then, the Unit has been working with officials in the Department of Health to better understand the challenges of long waiting times, review current plans, and identify specific areas where it can support Health in improving delivery. The Unit has also been discussing Health’s approach to monitoring delivery, to inform regular reporting to us as First Minister and deputy First Minister, as well as to the wider Executive. In tandem, the Unit continues to engage with other departments to identify further priorities, as all Ministers have been invited to suggest areas where we can help improve the efficiency, effectiveness and sustainability of public services.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the review of anti-social behaviour legislation, including any potential action to deal with the drinking of alcohol in public places.
answer_text: Action to deal with the drinking of alcohol in public places will likely be a cross-cutting issue, as elements fall within the remit of the Department for Communities (DfC). As such my officials are working with partners in DfC in finalising proposals in this area.  Following recent written correspondence with the Chief Constable on potential solutions my officials are continuing to engage with relevant stakeholders with a view to bringing forward legislation in the next assembly mandate. This will be subject to Executive and Assembly approval. Also under consideration are amendments to the Anti-Social Behaviour Order (ASBO). Work has been undertaken to consider a number of issues raised via the review, subsequent public consultation and the consideration of the ASBO by a multi-agency advisory group which included representation from the Community and Voluntary sector. Again, it is proposed that any potential amendment will be taken forward in the next Assembly mandate, subject to Executive and Assembly approval; however, policy development work will continue in preparation for that in this mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Translink business case for service enhancements on the North West rail corridor.
answer_text: In September 2025, Translink submitted a revised business case for the provision of additional services to the North West rail corridor. My Department is now considering the revised business case to examine what service changes could be introduced, subject to budget availability, to improve connectivity to the North-West.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to provide an update on the publication of the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.
answer_text: I plan to bring forward a draft Early Learning and Childcare Strategy for Executive consideration and public consultation in the coming weeks.  My Written Ministerial Statement of 15 May sets out my early learning and childcare measures for 2025/26, including next steps.  The Statement can be found at the link below.Written Ministerial Statement - Early Learning and Childcare Measures 2025/26 | Department of Education

answer_text: My officials have engaged with the sector over the past number of months regarding the review and I am currently considering the draft Terms of Reference. When finalised, these will be provided to relevant stakeholders.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Translink business case for service enhancements on the North West rail corridor.
answer_text: My officials are currently reviewing the business case submitted by Translink to ensure that any costs submitted are accurate and that they reflect the full cost of the proposed service enhancements.  In addition to this, Translink is presently carrying out a review of the current potential passenger growth predictions alongside the impact on Infrastructure costs. This is anticipated to be concluded by the end of Summer 2025.
