answer_text: We refer the Member to the answer given to AQW 24635/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she will answer AQW 27803/22-27.
answer_text: I can confirm my Department responded to AQW 27803/22-27 on 11 June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 25101/22-27, to provide more detail as to the formula which is used to determine fair shares of funding provided to Health and Social Care Trusts.
answer_text: Most modern health economies have established capitation funding models to inform resource allocations. The NI Capitation Formula is a complex statistical formula which has been developed specifically around the current core service user groupings (Programmes of Care). It is used to measure the relative need for resources across local populations by identifying fair shares for local populations.	Each Programme of Care has an associated model which comprises of 3 elements:Relevant Population - The client group on which Programme of Care is based;Age / Gender weighting - Age/gender structure of a population affects its need for Health and Social Care, this is thus compensated for;Additional needs weighting - Differential needs for services are likely to be due to socio economic factors e.g. deprivation – The relevant factors are chosen for each Programme of Care.The Programme of Care models are consolidated to produce locality fair shares.  Two adjustments are then made to compensate the localities for the differing cost of provision of service to their populations:Rurality – The additional cost of providing services in rural/urban areasEconomies of Scale adjustment – The scale effect of hospital and community infrastructure	The result is a formula which establishes the fair share of available resources that each locality should receive based on relative, not absolute, need.		Whilst the local areas are coterminous with the 5 Trust boundaries it must be borne in mind that the fair shares are for a locality and residents often access to services outside their locality (cross boundary flow). Therefore, the locality fair share of resource does not equate to Trust fair share. 	The demand for HSC is greater than the resources available, therefore the formula cannot guarantee that all care needs are met but does help to ensure that populations have fair access to the resources that exist. The formula provides a direction of travel for strategic investment decisions. Equity reviews are carried out by my Department on a periodic basis comparing planned and actual expenditure on baseline investments with Capitation fair shares, together with an assessment of variances in quality and performance.  These help inform the Department on overall funding policy decisions. The HSC has experienced unprecedented financial challenges in recent years. This has meant the ability to use new money to effect capitation shifts has been limited. However, in order not to destabilise services, baseline allocations to local Trusts have remained relatively unchanged in recent years but new money has been skewed where possible, for example demography funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she will introduce her Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: It remains my intention to introduce the Employment Rights Bill to the Assembly in January 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail which local councils have not set up their Local Economic Partnerships.
answer_text: All eleven local councils have established their Local Economic Partnerships.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how the £7 million, allocated to the higher education sector following the 2025-26 June monitoring round, will be spent.
answer_text: The £7m allocated as part of the June monitoring round will help ensure each of our local Higher Education Institutions can receive a flat cash settlement for teaching grant in Academic Year 2025/26 compared to funding in the prior year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail what work her Department is carrying out in relation to aviation policy.
answer_text: My officials are progressing an aviation policy based on recommendations from the York Aviation Report on Air Connectivity published in June 2024.  To facilitate this process my Department has taken over responsibility for airport policy and we are engaging with stakeholders in both the aviation and tourism sectors to identify suitable interventions which can enhance our air connectivity and promote route development. Most recently, officials met with the three local airport CEOs to discuss policy development and gain sectoral expertise to help identify both monetary and non-monetary levers that can support the aviation sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she last engaged with her counterpart in the Irish Government in relation to the North South Interconnector.
answer_text: There is regular engagement between our Departments on energy matters, including the North/South Interconnector. My last meeting with Minister O'Brien was on 11 June.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail their final financial contribution to the repair of the Mute Meadows Installation at Ebrington Square.
answer_text: The Department is currently liaising with Derry City and Strabane District Council on the repair of Mute Meadows and is supportive of the project.  On receipt of the Council’s confirmed final budget toward the costs of the repairs, TEO officials will consider a contribution.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a breakdown of the amount of compensation her Department has paid out due to road defects, broken down by constituency, in each of the last three financial years.
answer_text: My Department holds management information in relation to roads related public liabilityclaims which can be found online at: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/road-asset-maintenance-directorate-management-information-roads-related-claims-period-20202021-20242025 My Department maintains its records by Roads Section Office, which broadly align with Council areas. Table 7 in the above link provides details of the compensation paid out for roads related defects during the last three full financial years broken down by Roads Section Office.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the rational behind the decision to cut funding for the Community Crisis Intervention Service, Derry.
answer_text: The Community Crisis Intervention Service (CCIS) was previously funded through the Mental Health Support Fund (MHSF), a one-off funding initiative launched by the Department of Health in April 2021. This fund, established from unspent resources originally allocated to Covid-19 response efforts, was designed to equip and enable local charities to continue providing vital mental health services in the community during the pandemic and its aftermath. This was a one off, non-recurrent fund which has now ended.Following the end of this funding, the Department identified £42,300 of non-recurrent slippage funding, through the Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG), to support the continued operation of the CCIS until the end of June 2025. I greatly appreciate services such as CCIS, which have been of vital importance to those in the community who need it most, and which have offered much needed support to statutory services. However the Department remains in a significantly challenging budgetary position, with a funding gap of some £600 million. Therefore, there is currently no capacity within the current budget to replace the MHSF or provide additional funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will commit to meeting Echo Echo Dance Theatre Company once the review by the Arts Council has concluded.
answer_text: It is not appropriate for me to get involved with a specific funding decision which is a matter for the Arts Council of Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to provide a list of all consultant referral waiting lists that have been transitioned to a regional model in the past two years, whereby patients are offered the earliest available appointment regardless of their Health and Social Care Trust area; and (ii) whether this regional approach is being applied across multiple specialties.
answer_text: (i)A regional waiting list for breast cancer assessment became operational on 8 May 2025. This initiative is part of a broader review of Breast Services and is designed to ensure that all patients across Northern Ireland have access to the earliest available appointment, regardless of their home Trust.With the introduction of a single regional list, waiting times are measured regionally rather than by individual Trusts.   (ii)A collaborative approach to waiting list management is well developed across all Health and Social Care Trusts.  The Elective Care Management Team meets regularly and provides a forum for Trusts to highlight pressures that may require assistance, alongside discussion of opportunities to equalise waiting times across Trusts.  All Trusts are represented at a senior level.This regional approach has been further embedded by the development of the regional day procedure centres (DPCs), which provide capacity to treat patients who require low acuity procedures across a range of specialities.  Patients travel from all Trusts to access this capacity.  This supports the efficient use of core Trust capacity, which often focuses on more complex procedures.  The DPCs have supported reductions in the number of patients waiting and the associated waiting times for procedures such as endoscopy.  In addition a number of specialist centres have been created for Urological conditions which can be accessed by patients from all Trusts, with the complex stone service in Craigavon Area Hospital and the bladder outflow service at the Ulster Hospital are examples of this.This collaborative approach continues to be strengthened through service and technical developments.  The implementation the encompass programme has provided the opportunity to develop single regional waiting lists that will ensure patients are assessed in chronological order on a regional basis, rather than within their local Trust area.  This will allow patients to be assessed in the next available clinic slot, which may be in any Trust, mitigating Trust specific service pressures that can impact waiting times.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any consideration he has given to updating the curriculum to expand the teaching provided on financial guidance.
answer_text: Financial capability, including money management and saving, is currently a statutory and integral part of the Northern Ireland Curriculum. It is embedded across all key stages and supported through pupils’ everyday experiences both within and beyond the classroom. Schools are expected to provide opportunities for pupils to develop practical skills in planning, managing, and using resources—encouraging independence across various areas of learning.In addition, over the last five years, Council for the Curriculum, Examinations and Assessment (CCEA) has developed a strong working relationship with Young Enterprise Northern Ireland (YENI) and the Money and Pensions Service (MaPS) to promote financial capability and develop resources for teachers to use. The Strategic Review of the Northern Ireland Curriculum was published on 3 June 2025 and provides a compelling case for comprehensive curriculum reform.  The report makes twenty-one recommendations and sets out the need for a new curriculum framework that is purpose-led; knowledge-rich; continuous and coherent; specific and focussed; and flexible and inclusive. I have announced my intention to establish a Curriculum Taskforce to lead the design and development of a new curriculum framework that embodies the principles outlined in the Review and meets the evolving needs of learners across Northern Ireland.  The Taskforce will be operational in Autumn 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the steps she is taking to ensure that Invest NI is able to reach its targets of investment in low carbon and the green economy, as set in its Business Strategy 2024-27.
answer_text: Across its Developing and Achieving Sustainability strategic priority, Invest NI’s activity is split into two main areas of focus; 	(i).	Supporting businesses to reduce emissions, enhance sustainability and efficiency and drive greater levels of productivity across businesses; and, 	(ii).	Identify and exploit Green Economy opportunities to grow investment and sectoral capability in the Low Carbon and Green Economy.Invest NI has exceeded by some margin, the one-year targets for the number and value of Energy & Resource Efficiency investments.  However, as part of its End of Year reporting, Invest NI acknowledged that, while it exceeded its one-year target for the number of low carbon and green economy investments, it did not meet its target for the value of these investments. Invest NI has advised me that this Year 1 shortfall is due to a delay in the timing of a small number of large projects. It is expected that businesses will seek to implement these projects in 2025/26 and Invest NI is confident it will achieve its three-year low carbon and green economy investment target.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 25101/22-27.
To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she is actively seeking to expand the number of routes currently on offer at the City of Derry Airport.
answer_text: The City of Derry Airport management team works closely with Derry City & Strabane District Council to explore opportunities for route development. Since April 2024, new routes have been secured to Birmingham, Edinburgh and Liverpool, and I welcome the announcement by the Irish Government that preparation is underway to establish the PSO route between CoDA and Dublin in 2026 which has the potential to provide significant benefits to the North West. My officials are working closely with key stakeholders to support the delivery of this important development.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 24635/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 24635/22-27 was answered on 26 June 2025.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide an update on the establishment of a Delivery Unit.
answer_text: A Delivery Unit has been established to drive reform and transformation across government and wider public services. It will work across departments to help ensure that the Executive’s top priorities receive the scrutiny, support and attention required to make the greatest possible impact on people’s lives

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what steps she is taking to ensure that Invest NI is able to deliver its Business Strategy 2024-2027.
answer_text: Invest NI’s 2024/25 performance results demonstrate that the organisation is making good progress in delivering against the targets and objectives in its Business Strategy 2024-27. I recognise that Invest NI’s 2025/26 budget allocation presents challenges in delivering the ambitions set out in its Business Strategy. However, I am confident that Invest NI will utilise its current and future allocations to maximise delivery in 2025/26 and 2026/27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the location of the 59 per cent of Invest NI investments which have taken place outside the Belfast Metropolitan Area, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: Invest NI is currently in the process of finalising data analysis at constituency level and this information will be published in the coming weeks. The detailed information requested will therefore be provided in due course.	For the Member’s reference, the Belfast Metropolitan Area (BMA) is defined by the following District Council Areas – Belfast, Lisburn and Castlereagh, Antrim and Newtownabbey and Ards and North Down.  Council areas can cross constituency lines; therefore, a breakdown by constituency may reveal investments both within and outside the BMA, depending on the location of the project.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will publish the terms of reference for the Delivery Unit.
answer_text: The Delivery Unit will work with departments and their delivery partners to promote effective collaboration, and ensure clarity of roles and responsibilities. Further consideration will be given to the need for a Terms of Reference, informed by the specific nature of the support the Unit will provide, as the scope of its work evolves.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the allocation of funding to Invest NI within the Budget 2025-26.
answer_text: All Departments are under significant financial pressures due to the continued underfunding of public services by the British Government.Funding for 2025-26 was allocated to Invest NI in the context of the overall funding available Departmental allocation. I recognise that Invest NI’s allocation presents challenges in delivering the ambitions set out in its Business Strategy. However, I am confident that Invest NI will utilise its allocation to maximise delivery in 2025/26.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail why women in the Western Health and Social Care Trust are facing waits of up to seven weeks for GP breast cancer referral appointments following the transition to a regional referral system.
answer_text: The current regional wait for patients waiting breast assessment is 8 weeks.A regional waiting list for breast cancer assessment became operational on 8 May 2025. This initiative is part of a broader review of Breast Services and is designed to ensure that all patients across Northern Ireland have access to the earliest available appointment, regardless of their home Trust.Most individuals will continue to be seen at their nearest hospital; however, where earlier appointments are available, patients will be offered an appointment at any of the acute hospitals. Following assessment, patients are referred back to their home Trust for treatment if required.With the introduction of a single regional list, waiting times are measured regionally rather than by individual Trusts.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether their Department will provide financial support for the repair of the Mute Meadows installation at Ebrington Square.
answer_text: The Department is currently liaising with Derry City and Strabane District Council on the repair of Mute Meadows and is supportive of the project.  We would refer you to the recently answered AQW 28427/22-27, as details remain the same at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to outline (a) the current timeline; and (b) projected costs for the development of the new health hub at the Fort George site, Derry; and (ii) what steps are being taken to ensure the project is not subject to further delays beyond the reported 2028-2031 timeframe.
answer_text: Construction of the Cityside health hub at the Fort George site remains contingent on business case approval and confirmation of affordability.  Subject to these conditions, construction could begin on site in the Spring of 2028 with an on-programme completion of Summer 2031.The current total project cost, excluding internal Department of Health costs is £141,691,439 based on the timeline above.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail what steps are being taken to address the lack of participation in the Dental Access Scheme by dental practices in Derry City.
answer_text: My Department has issued two expressions of interest to all dental practices who treat Health Service patients to join the Dental Access Scheme (DAS) on 28 May 2024 and 4 March 2025 respectively.  These expressions of interest have led to 36 dental practices participating in the DAS across Northern Ireland. There are 4 practices in the Western Local Commissioning Group (LCG) area, three in Limavady and one in Strabane. Unfortunately, there were no applications from the Derry/Londonderry city area despite the attractive fees paid through the scheme.Participation in the DAS is on a voluntary basis and my Department cannot compel practices to join the scheme.I am pleased to report that since the DAS commenced in August 2024,over 21,000 patients have been treated, of which 3,000 were seen in the Western LCG area. The most recent monitoring report shows that in recent weeks, activity has increased to such an extent that the expenditure has been exceeding the budget and as a result no further applications are being sought at the moment. This will be kept under review.  Should any approaches from dental practices in the Derry/Londonderry city area be received, they will be given due consideration by my officials.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of whether recent sentencing in respect of individuals regarding environmental crime at Mobuoy was proportionate to the offence.
answer_text: ORAL QUESTION No. 11 – AQO 2223/22-27  ANSWER I believe that environmental crime is a serious issue and that it is important that it is treated as such by the courts.  The financial cost borne by communities and government in respect of such crime is significant, and the impact on our natural environment can be devastating and, in extreme cases, irreversible. Sentencing decisions are determined by the independent judiciary based on the individual circumstances for each case, any relevant sentencing guidance that is available, and the sentence maxima for the offence in question as specified in legislation.  The sentencing exercise is a complex one and is unique to each case. It would be wholly inappropriate for me to comment on sentences imposed in individual cases, as this would impinge on the independence of the judiciary which I am bound to respect.  Furthermore, as there are aspects of this case, including financial redress that remain before the courts, it would be inappropriate to make further comment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the steps he is taking to support registered childminders within the childcare sector.
answer_text: In 2024-25 from the Early Learning and Childcare (ELC) ringfenced budget, I provided:-£303k to the Department for Communities (DfC) to support the Labour Market Partnerships (LMP) Childminding Academies.  This saw an expansion of Childminding Academies to 10 of the 11 local LMPs with an anticipated 146 newly trained childminders, this included academies providing culturally appropriate childminders in Irish Medium, Ukrainian, Bengali, Polish and Arabic.£30k to the Department of Health (DoH) to take forward an Approved Home Childcare (AHC) pilot scheme. £30k to enhance training support for the sector, including childminders.Funding for the above programmes will continue in 2025-26.   In addition, I expanded the Pathway Fund in 2025-26 by introducing a new funding stream specifically for Childminders and Approved Home Childcarers. To date, 659 successful applicants have received awards of up to £500 through this Scheme.Finally, £2m has been earmarked from ELC funding in 2025-26 for the childcare sector, including childminders, pending recommendations from the Department for the Economy (DfE) scoping study on business support requirements for the sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the reasons why a reduced requirement of £8.9 million was identified in relation to the Economic Regional Growth Fund.
answer_text: Departmental officials engaged with council representatives on the Regional Balance Fund Project Board to discuss budget allocation for Year 1. It was agreed that 30% allocation would be more suitable, to allow time for the necessary preparatory work of the LEPs being established and to identify projects.  It should be noted, however, there will be higher budget allocations for Year 2 and Year 3. Total Regional Balance Fund to support the LEPs remains £45m to 31 March 2028.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how the £8.9 million, allocated to the Economic Regional Growth Fund and subsequently returned to the Department, will be used.
answer_text: The internal reallocation of the £8.9 million Capital DEL has been deferred until after the outcome of DfE’s June Monitoring bids, to the Executive, are known.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 27332/22-27, to provide a timeline for the agreement of a targeted business support scheme for the childcare sector.
answer_text: The DfE led cross–departmental Steering Group appointed to oversee research into the need for a targeted business support scheme for the childcare sector met on 10th June to consider the final research report.I will shortly be writing to the Minister of Education to apprise him of the findings of the research and to provide advice on the implementation of the report recommendations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the total minor works funding provided to all schools for maintenance and repairs in the last 15 years, broken down by (a) constituency; (b) school; and (c) school type, up to 2024-25; and (ii) an overall total for each school across the entire period.
answer_text: As funding is not allocated to individual schools, capital minor works expenditure has been provided. Please note that all minor works projects are funded from the Department of Education’s (DE) capital budget and are not ‘maintenance or repairs’ works. The Education Authority (EA) has statutory responsibility for undertaking maintenance and repairs work in maintained and controlled schools.  DE allocates the EA resource funding each financial year to take this forward.In line with record retention policies, details of the amount spent on minor works for all schools, broken down by constituency, school and school type is only available for each of the last seven financial years and is set out in tables which I have deposited in the Assembly Library.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 27674/22-27, to detail the factors which have caused a delay in the Local Economic Partnerships bringing forward projects to her Department.
answer_text: The establishment of Local Economic Partnerships is a new approach, bringing together my Department, Invest NI, Councils and other key economic stakeholders in local areas. Delays have been due to the need to obtain approval of the business case which required a careful approach to provide the flexibility I intended, and to differences in the length of time Councils have taken to establish Partnerships. I have been clear from the start that I want genuine partnership. For this reason, I think it has been important to allow each Councils time to establish partnerships and consider what would work best in their area. I look forward to seeing the Partnerships bring forward to address economic issues that affect their areas.

answer_text: AQW 26932/22-27 was answered on 24 June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide a timeline for when her Department will have made a decision on the Translink business case for service enhancements on the North West rail corridor.
answer_text: My officials are currently reviewing the business case submitted by Translink to ensure that any costs submitted are accurate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the total funding provided to all schools for maintenance and repairs in the last 15 years, broken down by (a) constituency; (b) school; and (c) school type, up to 2024-25; and (ii) an overall total for each school across the entire period.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has statutory responsibility for undertaking repairs and maintenance work in maintained and controlled schools.  My Department allocates resource funding to EA each financial year.In line with record retention policies, details of the final outturn for schools’ repairs and maintenance expenditure is only available for each of the last seven financial years for maintained and controlled schools and is set out in tables, broken down by constituency, school and school type, which I have deposited in the Assembly Library.Please note that the 2024/25 maintenance figure is provisional outturn expenditure and may be subject to change. The EA have included expenditure for both Landlord maintenance (non-delegated earmarked funding) and Tenant maintenance (delegated from Common Funding Formula (CFF) funding). The figures exclude EA centre expenditure which includes expenditure for maintenance officers, depots, Education Other Than At School (EOTAS), central meals kitchens and corporate buildings which cannot be broken down to individual school or constituency. Voluntary Grammar and Grant Maintained Integrated Schools are responsible for their own maintenance works.

answer_text: My officials are working closely with officials in the Department of Enterprise, Tourism, and Employment on a memorandum of understanding between the two departments.	Once agreed, this will enhance the already strong working relationship between the two departments which is built on our partnership approach to InterTradeIreland; as well as the positive work taking place through the Shared Island Enterprise Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on Theme 2 of the PEACEPLUS Programme, Delivering Socio-Economic Regeneration and Transformation.
answer_text: The PEACEPLUS programme is managed and administered by the Special EU Programmes Body (SEUPB). DfE is accountable department in the North for three of the four Investment Areas (IAs) under Theme 2.IA 2.1 SME Development and Transition - Applications are under review and successful applicants will be informed by SEUPB in due course.IA 2.2 Innovation Challenge Fund - SEUPB recently announced a total of €65m funding awarded to eight collaborative innovation projects that will boost productivity and strengthen collaboration among SMEs and Research organisations across the island, driving skills development and expanding expertise in emerging fields such as photonics, digital manufacturing, agri-feeding solutions, medical technologies and AI. SEUPB announce €65m PEACEPLUS Innovation Challenge funding awardsIA 2.3 Programme Area Skills Development - €46m funding awarded to five applicants to improve equal access to inclusive and quality services in education, training and lifelong learning in the North and the border counties of Ireland. Currently three projects have formally launched and two are in initial start-up stage. SEUPB announce €46m PEACEPLUS Programme Area Skills funding awardsIA 2.4 Smart Towns & Villages - DAERA is the Accountable Department for this Investment Area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the development on an aviation Route Development Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Careers Action Plan.

This plan has been cleared by Minister Givan and myself, and has now also received Executive Approval. The Action Plan will now be published in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline total expenditure on agri-environment schemes, including allocated spend on this year's Farming with Nature Package, in each year from 2015.
answer_text: The table below sets out the total expenditure on agri-environment schemes in the last seven complete financial years.2017-20182018-20192019-20202020-20212021-20222022-20232023-2024TotalScheme Name£'000£'000£'000£'000£'000£'000£'000£'000Northern Ireland Countryside Management Scheme (Resource expenditure)         2,686        2,705            587                 -                 -                 -                    -            5,978 Environmental Farming Scheme (Resource expenditure)            283        2,622         3,381           4,664 5,127              5,377            7,759      29,213 Environmental Farming Scheme (Capital expenditure)               -          7,992       13,683         11,730         2,180         4,256             3,707        43,548 Due to the Department’s Records Retention Policy of seven years, financial information for the years before 2017-18 is not included. question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline the steps he is taking to mitigate damage to priority semi-natural grasslands arising from the Soil Nutrient Health Scheme.
answer_text: The Soil Nutrient Health Scheme (SNHS) includes several mitigations designed to protect priority habitats including semi-natural grasslands. During registration, the farmer is given the opportunity to identify any field(s) which are in priority habitats (full or partial coverage) and then sampling and reporting is tailored accordingly.  All registered fields which fall fully or partially within designated priority habitats (identified from NIEA datasets) are flagged automatically on the farmer’s SNHS report with a series of codes that result in either no nutrient recommendations given in the soil report e.g. it is a designated priority habitat or if adjacent to a designated priority habitat, the farmer is advised to contact DAERA before any planned agricultural intensification is carried out.Additional codes flag fields on the report that are within Special Protection Areas, are in the Environmental Farming Scheme (Higher) or could fall under Environmental Impact Assessment regulations and advise farmers accordingly.All participants in the SNHS have access to a specific CAFRE training package.  This training identifies the risks to sensitive environments of nutrient application and the impacts of overapplication on aquatic environments.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps his Department is taking to support mental health in newsrooms, including measures to encourage awareness, training, and the normalisation of conversations around mental well-being in journalism.
answer_text: It would primarily be the responsibility for individual employers to protect the mental health of their employees and to provide necessary training to staff and managers. The new Protect Life 2 Implementation Plan will contain specific actions in relation to strengthening awareness and response to suicide and self-harm and in relation to the use of media guidelines and monitoring the reporting of suicide and need to continually review guidelines.The implementation plan will recognise the importance of the provision of training programmes to meet the tiers of training as outlined within the PHA Wellbeing and Suicide Prevention Training Framework. It also includes a review of current training provision for suicide prevention and self-harm and improve collection of data and outcomes. The PHA will be pleased to assist newsrooms should advice around specific mental health support be required.The Health and Safety Executive Northern Ireland (HSENI) is the lead body responsible for the promotion and enforcement of health and safety at work standards in Northern Ireland. HSENI’s Mental Well-being at Work Advisory Service continues to offer webinars for employers and line managers on the HSE Management Standards, an assessment based approach to managing work-related stress. HSENI also publish a variety of online resources for line managers and employees.

answer_text: Following engagement with the Skills Council and work to ensure alignment with the draft Skills Action Plan and draft Careers Action Plan, the final draft of the Apprenticeship Action Plan is moving through the approvals process with an aim to publish shortly.

In parallel, my Department, via the Derry and Strabane City Deal is supporting the investment of £8.4m in a similar proposal to provide an Innovation Hub at Ulster University’s Magee campus which is strategically located to retain university spin-outs within the region.
