question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail what action his Department is taking to provide the public with more information about the fundamentals of owning an electric vehicle.
answer_text: My Department uses social media pages, its internet presence, and NI Direct to share information with the public on electric vehicles.My officials are also working closely with the Consumer Council and other key stakeholders, involved with the EV Infrastructure Task Force, to make sure useful information is available to the public in relation to EV ownership. The NI Direct page also provides links to further information for the public on EV Charge Points and infrastructure grants; on fuel consumption, CO2 emissions and vehicle tax bands; and the Office for Zero-Emission Vehicles’ guidance pages.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 17277/22-27, to detail the number of unsuccessful applications to the Energy and Resource Efficiency Programme from businesses in the last five years, broken down by location.
answer_text: Invest NI delivers a suite of Energy and Resource Efficiency (ERE) support to all eligible businesses, covering all council areas in the North of Ireland. The Resource Efficiency Capital Grant is the only element of Energy and Resource Efficiency support that is operated on a competitive basis. It is a competitive grant with finite funding. Applications are assessed against eligibility criteria, and those which do not meet the score threshold or will not deliver material efficiency will not be successful. Between 2020/2021 and 2024/2025, a total of 50 businesses have submitted unsuccessful applications to the Resource Efficiency Capital Grant. A breakdown of unsuccessful applications is provided in the table below.  20/2121/2222/2323/2424/25TotalCouncil AreaUnsuccessful for RECGAntrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council 000202Ards and North Down Borough Council 001113Armagh City, Banbridge and Craigavon Borough Council 1215110Belfast City Council 000000Causeway Coast and Glens Borough Council 000000Derry City and Strabane District Council 111216Fermanagh and Omagh District Council 210216Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council 020114Mid and East Antrim Borough Council 001012Mid Ulster District Council 0306413Newry, Mourne and Down District Council 110204Total5104211050

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail (i) the Barnett consequentials arising from increased government spending on childcare in England for each of the last five years; and (ii) any expected consequentials arising from the Autumn Budget 2024.
answer_text: The Executive received £76.4 million of Barnett consequentials arising from increased government spending on childcare in England in the last five years. 		The Executive received Barnett consequentials as a result of changes to Whitehall departments’ budgets for 2024-25 and 2025-26 in the Autumn Budget. These were provided at departmental level with no detail provided of the individual measures giving rise to any changes. 		Similarly, we cannot identify any Barnett consequentials received in previous Spending Reviews as these are based on departmental outcomes and are not broken down by individual measure.		The following table sets out the Childcare related consequential received outside Spending Reviews. These are all Resource DEL.	£million2021-222022-232023-242024-25Supplementary Estimates 2021-22Tax Free Childcare - RDEL Programme-   0.006  -  -  - Spring Budget 2023Address upfront childcare costs issue in UC -  -      1.397      1.796 Spring Budget 2023UC childcare maximum caps -  -      0.143               -   Spring Budget 20239-36 month 30 hours working parents  -  -               -     57.151 Spring Budget 2023Uplift existing entitlement for childcare -  -               -        9.459 Spring Budget 2023Wraparound Childcare -  -      0.131      6.339

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister (i) for an update on their Department’s work to develop digital solutions to support victims of domestic abuse to discreetly report their abuse to the PSNI; and (ii) whether any solutions with private companies will be taken forward as part of the development of the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Strategy.
answer_text: The Executive Office recently invited applications from private companies, professionals, not for profit organisations, social enterprises, and third sector to apply to the EVAWG Small Business Research Initiative (SBRI) Challenge Fund. SBRI provides innovative solutions to challenges faced by the public sector, leading to better public services and improved efficiency and effectiveness.Making our communities safe for women and girls will take innovation and new ideas in all sectors of our society. The title of the two complementary and reinforcing SBRI challenges are: Challenge 1 - Addressing Violence Against Women and Girls Through Data and Tech Innovations; andChallenge 2 - Making Spaces Safer for Women and Girls Through Design and Technology.The phase 1 challenges will run from November to the end of March 2025, after which consideration will be given to the further progress of solutions developed through the challenge.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on her Department’s work to develop digital solutions that can support victims of domestic abuse to discreetly report their abuse to the PSNI, including any engagement with private companies on this issue.
answer_text: Given the operational independence of the PSNI, my Department maintains a clear boundary when it comes to involvement in the procuring of digital solutions that the PSNI may ultimately use. While my department meets with various independent companies, it would not be appropriate to disclose information that could be commercially sensitive.  You may wish to note that the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls (EVAWG) Directorate within The Executive Office (TEO) has recently launched the Small Business Research Initiative (SBRI).  It invites suppliers to identify ways in which digital platforms can be used to improve the detection and reporting of violence against women and girls.  I am supportive of this initiative and consider that any outworkings could potentially benefit victims of domestic abuse.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the work being undertaken on the establishment of a mother and baby unit in all hospitals.
answer_text: As referred to in my answer to your earlier question on this topic (AQW 16606/22-27) the Belfast City Hospital site was identified as the most suitable location for a regional Mother and Baby Unit (MBU).An independent review was commissioned by my department to advise on the model and location for a MBU. The report which was completed in September 2023 incorporated the recommendations of the Royal College of Psychiatrists. The outcome of the review recommended a regional MBU for N Ireland and not a MBU in all hospitals. There are however community perinatal mental health teams in place within each trust that provide wrap around mental health care to women in the perinatal period.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 17063/22-27, to provide a timeline for the introduction of a ban on onshore petroleum licensing.
answer_text: I am currently seeking agreement from my Executive colleagues to introduce a legislative ban on all forms of onshore petroleum exploration and production. The timeline for introducing legislation will depend on this agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his engagement with Invest NI in relation to attracting further foreign investment.
answer_text: The attraction of further inward investment is a key element of my department’s economic development approach for the North.I engage regularly with Invest NI, both at home and overseas to advance opportunities for first-time FDI and expansions. That engagement is wide ranging and includes meetings with prospective and existing investors, partners and stakeholders to promote the North’s core investment proposition, together with highlighting our unique dual market access.In pursuit of attracting further investment, I have recently published my Regional Strategy to increase good, productive, low carbon investment across all regions of the North; each region developing bespoke propositions to further increase international attractiveness whilst capitalising on our regional strengths and capabilities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 16754/22-27, to detail the number of children attending special schools availing of a dental assessment by Community Dental Service staff in the last five years, broken down by location.
answer_text: The information requested is not routinely collected or requested by the Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 17066/22-27, to detail the number of businesses that have availed of the Energy and Resource Efficiency Programme for Businesses, broken down by location.
answer_text: Invest NI currently delivers a suite of Energy and Resource Efficiency Support available to all eligible businesses, covering all council areas, in the North of Ireland.  The table below represents the businesses by location that have availed of the Energy and Resource Efficiency Programme since its launch in April 2024.The Energy Efficiency Capital Grant, which launched in April 2024, has had a significant number of applications which are currently being processed.		Number of Businesses availing of individual support schemes 24/25	Council Area	Technical Consultancy & Sustainability Reports	Resource Matching through Industrial Symbiosis	Resource Efficiency Capital Grant	Energy Efficiency Capital Grant	Total 	Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council	3	2	1	1	7	Ards and North Down Borough Council	5	0	0	3	8	Armagh City, Banbridge and Craigavon Borough Council	17	0	4	6	27	Belfast City Council	7	7	2	4	20	Causeway Coast and Glens Borough Council	5	10	1	5	21	Derry City and Strabane District Council	5	1	1	1	8	Fermanagh and Omagh District Council	13	0	3	4	20	Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council	9	2	3	3	17	Mid and East Antrim Borough Council	8	0	1	2	11	Mid Ulster District Council	19	0	13	10	42	Newry, Mourne and Down District Council	8	0	2	5	15	Totals	99	22	31	44

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any recent engagement he has had with the Ulster University Magee Taskforce.
answer_text: I met with Professor Paul Bartholomew (Vice- Chancellor and member of Magee Expansion Taskforce) and Professor Duncan Morrow from the University of Ulster on 3 July 2024. Issues regarding Magee were discussed during this meeting and a further meeting with the Taskforce is scheduled in my diary for November.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment on whether his Department will meet the target of delivering at least 80 per cent of electricity consumption from renewable sources by 2030, as required by the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: One of my four critical objectives for the Economy is to reduce carbon emissions; generating sufficient renewable electricity is key to facilitate this change.		We must build on our previous success in renewables development and the Executive needs to take a collaborative, whole system approach to facilitate delivery of the 80% by 2030 target. 		Whilst my Department has legislative responsibility for the target, collaboration across departments and with wider stakeholders, including industry, will be essential to the successful delivery of this target.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 16607/22-27, whether he will engage with the British Government in relation to the implementation of an Essentials Guarantee, to protect people in receipt of Universal Credit from going without essentials.
answer_text: I currently have no plans to engage with the British Government in relation to the implementation of an Essentials Guarantee. My Department offers support for those in need of short-term financial help through the Discretionary Support Scheme, subject to meeting eligibility criteria. Further information is available at: https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/campaigns/finance-supportMy department also provides funding to Social Supermarkets, via local Councils, to assist those facing food insecurity.In addition, my department’s Make The Call service offers free and confidential advice on what benefits, supports and services an individual may be entitled to. Further information is available at: https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/contacts/make-call-service

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on any engagement he has had with his Executive colleagues since the publication of the Magee Expansion Taskforce Interim Report.
answer_text: Prior to the publication of the Magee Expansion Taskforce Interim Report, I met with Professor Paul Bartholomew and Professor Duncan Morrow from the University of Ulster.Officials from my Department also met with the Taskforce in October to better understand the asks contained in the interim report and outline my Department’s response.On 16 October I wrote to the Economy Minister endorsing the Magee Expansion Taskforce Interim Report and responded to the specific requests from the Economy Minister pledging my department’s full support in delivering this vital project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has undertaken a review of (i) active travel; and (ii) traffic management interventions to facilitate the expansion of the Ulster University Magee campus to at least 10,000 students.
answer_text: My  Department is currently preparing the North West Transport Plan which covers transport issues including active travel, public transport, the local road network and parking. This Transport Plan will complement Derry City and Strabane Council’s Local Development Plan.A public consultation on the Active Travel Delivery Plan will also be undertaken between 13 November 2024 and 28 February 2025. This will also provide a further opportunity for engagement with the Taskforce on prioritisation of active travel infrastructure improvements in the vicinity of Magee campus.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when his Department will introduce an energy and resource efficiency support programme for businesses to help support the transition to net-zero.
answer_text: My department launched an Energy & Resource Efficiency (ERE) Programme for Businesses in May this year, and I have committed £40m to the programme over the next five years. The ERE Programme includes a range of support in the form of Technical Consultancy, Resource Matching Through Industrial Symbiosis, a Resource Efficiency Capital Grant and an Energy Efficiency Capital Grant. The Energy Efficiency Capital Grant includes grant support for energy efficiency measures along with low carbon and renewable technologies, all aimed at reducing emissions and helping businesses take control of their energy use and lower their energy demand.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 16833/22-27, to detail any engagement he has had with the Minister of Education in relation to a market assessment of the childcare sector.
answer_text: I wrote to the Minister of Education on 1 August 2024 regarding a market assessment of the childcare sector.  Since that date, my officials have been working closely with their counterparts in the Department of Education to take this work forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement he has had with Invest NI in relation to attracting further foreign investment.
answer_text: The attraction of productivity-enhancing and regionally distributed inward investment is a key pillar of my department’s economic development approach for the North.I engage regularly with Invest NI, both at home and overseas, to advance opportunities for first-time FDI and expansions. This includes meeting with prospective and existing investors, partners and stakeholders to promote the North’s compelling investment proposition and highlighting our unique dual market access.For example, in recent months I have travelled to the US, Canada and Berlin, launched the Dublin Hub, welcomed incoming delegations such as the Irish Australian Chamber of Commerce, and met the executive leadership team of recent investor Symphony.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how he plans to scale-up the Northern Ireland Sustainable Energy Programme.
answer_text: The NI Sustainable Energy Programme (NISEP) is run by the Utility Regulator. In regard to scaling up NISEP, my Department has provided £1m of additional funding to the Programme in 2023/24, which helped almost 500 homes install energy efficiency measures such as loft and cavity wall insulation.  A further £2.5m of additional funding has been provided for 2024/25 which will help approximately 1000 more households. As we work to develop a new energy efficiency programme, we will continue to work with partners such as the Utility Regulator to assess the options for further scaling up existing energy efficiency schemes to ensure no break in funding for energy efficiency interventions for those most in need.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the steps his Department is taking to introduce a ban on onshore petroleum licensing.
answer_text: The Department consulted on a new onshore petroleum licensing policy earlier in 2024.  The majority of respondents supported my proposed policy position to introduce a temporary moratorium and eventual legislative ban on all forms of onshore petroleum exploration and production. I am currently seeking agreement to the proposed policy option from my Executive colleagues before I move to introduce the necessary legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department has considered a review of the financial assessment for those who are in need of a nursing home place.
answer_text: Arrangements for charging for personal social services provided in residential care homes and nursing homes, including third party top ups is detailed in Departmental Guidance ECCU 1/2010 ‘Care Management, Provision of Service and Charging Guidance:https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/dhssps/hsc-eccu-1-2010.pdf My Department’s Social Care Collaborative Forum provides a formal mechanism for officials and representatives of the Social Care Sector (statutory, voluntary/community and private sectors), to work together as partners to build shared values and deliver improvements that will support and sustain social care now and into the future.As part of its remit, the Forum will review proposals arising out of the consultation on the Reform of Adult Social Care which included future charging arrangements within the sector. Progress against all aspects of the Forum’s work will be reported on and where specific proposals are being made in respect of charging for care these will be subject to public consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of residential homes and nursing homes that (i) charge; and (ii) do not charge third-party contributions, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.

To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the impact a lack of an Early Learning and Childcare Strategy is having on his Department’s ability to increase the proportion of good jobs through investing in affordable childcare and fair pay for childcare workers, as set out in his Economic Vision.
answer_text: The Department of Education is leading on the development of an Early Learning and Childcare Strategy and I am committed to working with the Minister for Education and his officials as they take forward this vitally important Executive priority. I recognise the relationship between the provision of adequate childcare support and the ability for parents to participate in education and employment. To that end, £740k of the Skills Fund has been allocated this year to support pathways into the childcare sector, helping supply of workers into this critically important sector.The Department of Education has responsibility for the targeted business support scheme for childcare providers. However, as my Department has expertise in delivering business support, my officials are assisting DE with this work by leading on a market assessment of the childcare sector. This exercise will identify the key issues impacting the financial sustainability of childcare providers and develop options for a government intervention.   My ‘Good Jobs’ Employment Rights Consultation closed on 30 September.  This consultation sought views on a range of legislative and non-legislative ways to improve our framework of rights and responsibilities. Encouraging flexibility in the workplace and achieving a fair work life balance were key parts of the consultation. Flexibility which enables a good work-life balance can be beneficial to businesses by helping them recruit and retain good employees while ensuring that work and personal commitments of employees can be balanced, including those with childcare or unpaid caring responsibilities.. Finally, National Minimum Wage setting powers are not devolved however  I am working with the Living Wage Foundation and Advice NI to promote the Real Living Wage here.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of schools that avail of the RAISE programme, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: No school has availed of the RAISE programme to date as no funding has been allocated. Projects funded through RAISE will be delivered by a range of different partners through collaboration, to improve outcomes for children and young people.  Schools will, of course, play a key role but will not be the sole delivery partner.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he intends to reform the third-party contribution system for residential and nursing homes.
answer_text: Arrangements for charging for personal social services provided in residential care homes and nursing homes, including third party top ups is detailed in Departmental Guidance ECCU 1/2010 ‘Care Management, Provision of Service and Charging Guidance:https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/dhssps/hsc-eccu-1-2010.pdf My Department’s Social Care Collaborative Forum provides a formal mechanism for officials and representatives of the Social Care Sector (statutory, voluntary/community and private sectors), to work together as partners to build shared values and deliver improvements that will support and sustain social care now and into the future.As part of its remit, the Forum will review proposals arising out of the consultation on the Reform of Adult Social Care which included future charging arrangements within the sector. Progress against all aspects of the Forum’s work will be reported on and where specific proposals are being made in respect of charging for care these will be subject to public consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 15999/22-27, to detail any engagement he has had with the Minister for Infrastructure in relation to rail and bus transport links in the North West region to help deliver regional balance.
answer_text: Responsibility for bus and transport links in the North are the sole responsibility of Department for Infrastructure. My response to your recent question (AQW 15999/22-27) refers to ongoing discussions with the Department on Infrastructure relating to City of Derry Airport. The Department for Infrastructure also recently met with the Magee Expansion Task Force.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Climate Change Action Plan.
answer_text: A Departmental Climate Change Action Plan for 2024/25 was published on 17 December 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 16234/22-27, whether he has brought the Green Growth Strategy to the Executive for approval.
answer_text: I refer to my response to question AQW 16234/22-27 which provided background information and current status of the draft Green Growth Strategy. Work is ongoing within my Department and officials are continuing to work at pace to finalise this Strategy. It remains my intention to take the draft strategy to the Executive for approval at the earliest opportunity.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 16454/22-27, whether he will come to the Assembly Chamber to provide an update on cross-border study following his meeting with his counterpart in the south.
answer_text: I am content to update members on ongoing work and plans for the future to reduce barriers to cross-border student mobility once I have engaged with my Ministerial counterpart in the South.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps that can be taken to provide additional support to those with special educational needs who are in need of dental work.
answer_text: While many children with special educational needs can attend a general dental practice for routine care and treatment, I am aware that for some children and young people, this is not an option and they will require referral to specialised care, which may include treatment under general anaesthetic. Care pathways and waiting lists for dental general anaesthetics are very long, exacerbated by the COVID pandemic and increasing demand and capacity challenges. To ensure that the most urgent cases are treated as quickly as possible, community dental teams within Health and Social Care Trusts across Northern Ireland operate a strict triage and prioritisation process.   This will ensure that children with the most urgent problems are managed as quickly as possible.  I am cognisant of the impact of dental pain particularly for those who have communication or behavioural difficulties.My Department recognises the importance of collaboration between healthcare professionals to help those with special educational needs access care when they need it. Community Dental Service teams (CDS) across Northern Ireland accept referrals from a wide range of  healthcare professionals. Further advice is available from the dental leads within each of the HSC Trusts. Community dental teams provide comprehensive care to those who cannot access care in  high street dental practice.  In addition, children attending special schools are offered a dental assessment by CDS staff and signposting given if a treatment need is identified, or offered treatment within the CDS when most appropriate. My Department recently consulted on a range of recommendations to improve the oral health of all children in Northern Ireland. While additional financial resources in the current climate are challenging, my Department will continue to make the case for additional resources to improve oral health, including interventions such as supervised toothbrushing and fluoride application, which have a recognised evidence base. My Department has recently reinstated funding for an enhanced examination for unregistered children aged up to 10 years to encourage registration with a dentist, provide tailored oral health advice and the application of age appropriate fluoride varnish.Finally, oral health promotion and prevention is essential and there are a wide range of resources available to parents/carers and education staff  to support these children and motivate families to understand the importance of oral health.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work his Department has undertaken to develop a new career and skills portal.
answer_text: The development of a new Careers Portal is an ambitious and complex IT enabled system transformation project. Successful delivery will help every part of the careers system to work together and improve access to high quality careers information and services.A small project team was established in December 2023 with an initial focus on research and engagement to identify priority needs of the wide range of end users.The engagement work to date has included:Establishing a network with colleagues in the English, Irish, Scottish and Welsh governments who have worked on similar projects.Focus groups with careers advisers, careers educators and young people in the 14-19 age bracket from a range of educational backgrounds and geographical locations.11 interactive workshops in nine different locations with the Careers Service including careers advisers, operations managers, support staff and policy staff. In-depth interviews with a range of adults at different stages of their career journeys as well as parents/carers, including special educational needs representation. A cross-cutting in-person stakeholder event, facilitated by Dr Deirdre Hughes, which was attended by 31 key stakeholders representing education, government, business and the voluntary and community sector.An online Citizen Space survey which received 1,229 responses from a diverse range of users and stakeholders.The project has now moved into a Discovery phase, bringing the more empathetic user-focused engagement and research together with IT specialists to identify the best technical solutions. In conjunction with developers, 12 workshops are scheduled to take place over the next six weeks. These sessions will be attended by representatives of multiple groups including young people, teachers, careers professionals, parents and the voluntary and community sector, each representing a different user persona on a career journey. This co-design approach will be key to ensuring that the product taken through to development after the Discovery phase best meets the needs of users and stakeholders.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has considered a review of the vocational education provision to help develop a more skilled workforce.
answer_text: My Department delivers a wide range of vocational education, which spans from entry level to vocational education within the Higher Education sector and across the majority of sectors in our economy. 		Vocational education provision is reviewed regularily, through a range of approaches.  My Department is currently developing business cases for new apprenticeship provision and entry level vocational education and training. Recently, a consultation on how we best support disabled learners on our Entry Level and Level 1 provision concluded and the responses to this consultation will inform a new programme that will launch in September 2025. 		Employer engagement also plays a major role in the ongoing development of vocational education provision.  My Department consults with a wide range of employers and other stakeholders through Sectoral Partnerships to ensure our content and curriculum align with the changing needs of employers. 		My Department has also consulted with a wide range of stakeholders on what is needed from the qualifications which underpin the vocational education system. 	The responses received were positive and constructive, and this valuable feedback will ensure all vocational qualifications deliver the knowledge and skills needed by learners and employers. 		In the coming months, I will be publishing actions plans for for both Skills and Apprenticeships, which will set out the full range of actions to ensure our workforce has the skills it needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 16087/22-27, to detail the number of staff who will fill the new Invest NI Regional Office through (i) a mix of redeployment; (ii) internal recruitment; and (iii) external recruitment.
answer_text: Invest NI will fill additional staffing compliment in the regional offices as indicated in response to AQW 16087/22-27 on 10th October 2024. The posts, ranging from support staff to senior leadership will be filled by a mix of redeployment, internal recruitment, and external recruitment where skills gaps exist within the organisation. The exact mix of how posts will be filled will only be known at the conclusion on several recruitment exercises. It is anticipated that the majority of the recruitment exercises will conclude by the end of the financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department will undertake a review of each of its Skills Programmes.
answer_text: A review of Skills programmes is a recommendation in the recent NIAO report - Developing Skills for Northern Ireland’s Future. My officials are considering this as a part of the wider response to the NIAO, and subsequent PAC hearing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work his Department is undertaking to develop a Lifelong Learning Action Plan.
answer_text: I refer you to my recent answer to AQW 16217/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail what engagement he has had with the British Government regarding the implementation of an Essentials Guarantee, to protect people in receipt of Universal Credit from going without essentials.
answer_text: I have not had any engagement with the British Government on the implementation of an Essentials Guarantee in Universal Credit.The rates of social security benefits including Universal Credit are set by the UK Government each year and maintained in parity in Northern Ireland. Any deviation from parity, including higher rates of benefits, would result in increased costs to the Block Grant.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any engagement he has had with the PSNI in relation to flags being erected on street furniture, over the last 12 months.
answer_text: On receiving a complaint, or a request for removal of an attachment, my Department will always contact the PSNI to determine if removal is likely to increase the risk of local community tension.Finding a sustainable solution to the ongoing problem of illegally erected flags and emblems requires leadership and a united approach. I can assure you that I am committed to working with Executive colleagues and all stakeholders to deal with this issue comprehensively.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail whether his Department has considered restoring free school meal payments during holiday periods.
answer_text: I met with the Minister for Communities on 9 September to discuss the issue of child food poverty including how this can impact families both during and outside of school term time. We agreed that it was appropriate for this issue to be considered holistically as part of the development of the Executive’s Anti-Poverty Strategy. While the Department for Communities leads on the development of the Anti-Poverty Strategy, my Department will also contribute to its development, and will work to ensure that the needs of children in food poverty, including those experienced by children during school holidays, are fully considered. In light of this, there are no current plans for my Department to reintroduce a School Holiday Food Grant scheme

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 13660/22-27, what steps his Department has taken to find a sustainable solution to illegally erected flags and emblems.
answer_text: The issue of unauthorised flags on Departmental property has been ongoing for many years. My Department’s primary considerations is the safety of road users and where unauthorised flags or attachments pose a risk to road users, my Department will seek to remove that hazard. Where there is no such danger, my Department will liaise closely with other key stakeholders and seek to provide a solution.Finding a sustainable solution to the ongoing problem of illegally erected flags and emblems requires leadership and a united approach.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the business case for a mother and baby unit at Belfast City Hospital.
answer_text: Following the identification of the Belfast City Hospital site as the most suitable location for a Mother and Baby Unit in autumn 2023, the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust was invited to prepare a business case for the provision of the proposed regional unit.The business case for Mother and Baby Unit is now well-progressed and it is anticipated that it will be finalised and submitted to DoH for scrutiny in the near future. However, progression of this project will remain subject to the required capital and revenue funding being identified.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline what steps they have taken to ensure that their work representing the interests of victims and survivors has not been undermined as a result of issues around Michael McMonagle.
To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will introduce a social housing decarbonisation fund.
answer_text: One of the proposed long-term commitments of the draft Housing Supply Strategy is to ensure there is an emphasis on improving the energy efficiency of homes and ensuring a just transition to decarbonised solutions.The Northern Ireland Housing Executive have made available £57.5 million available for thermal and low carbon improvement programmes in the financial year 2024/25. My officials are also working on developing the NIHE revitalisation programme and a potential future retrofit funding model for registered Housing Associations. Given this level of investment alongside severe financial and budgetary constraints that all Departments are facing I have no plans to introduce a separate Social Housing Decarbonisation Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQW 14441/22-27, for an update on the application for businesses in Foyle Street, Derry, to benefit from a reduction in their rates bill due to the prolonged closure of the street to facilitate major works by NI Water.
answer_text: The degree to which public works may affect the Net Annual Value (NAV) of premises in a particular locality is based on the duration of the works and severity of impact on the businesses affected.  Land & Property Services is currently gathering information on how the businesses have been affected by the works. The District Valuer will then consider if a temporary allowance is warranted and decide if a wider review is required of similarly affected property on Foyle Street.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work his Department has undertaken in relation to the creation of both a national and regional strategy for the retrofitting of homes.
answer_text: As lead for the Residential Buildings Sector of the NI Climate Action Plan, DfC officials are liaising with colleagues across Government to develop a long-term residential decarbonisation strategy, to achieve Net Zero by 2050. This strategy will set out the long-term approach to retrofitting homes and will need to be evidence based. Therefore, my officials have commissioned work on the preparation of a residential decarbonisation model to inform the pathway to net zero for the sector and I am cognisant of the challenge this will be. In addition, a Residential Decarbonisation Coordination Group has been established, jointly chaired by DfC and Department for Economy officials, to monitor and report progress on an on-going basis.Current initiatives include a Housing Supply Strategy, with retrofitting being a key part of enabling decarbonisation and a just transition to low carbon homes. Also, a new Fuel Poverty Strategy and a replacement for the Affordable Warmth Scheme, which assists low-income owner-occupiers and households in the private rented sector with the costs of energy efficiency measures is under preparation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she has considered a review of the current rates system to ensure that businesses are provided automatic relief in circumstances of public realm or infrastructure works.
answer_text: Under the rating and valuation system each case is assessed by Land & Property Services on its own merits by considering the impact of works on the Net Annual Value (NAV) of the property. This is determined by established caselaw and a long-standing statutory framework that supports the stability of a taxbase used to fund both central and local government services. I have no plans to review the rates system in this regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the work his Department has undertaken in relation to removing the 1400 Republic of Ireland students, currently studying in Northern Ireland, from the maximum aggregate student number cap.
answer_text: I am committed to removing barriers to cross-border study and have instructed officials to consider options for addressing these. Simply removing Southern students from the MaSN cap and associated funding arrangements would mean those students having to pay substantially higher tuition fees. I am seeking a meeting with my counterpart in the South, Minister Patrick O’Donovan, so that we can identify actions that can be taken, separately and collectively, to create greater opportunities for people to study throughout Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how he plans to use the US Net Zero Advisory Board to boost the green economy.
answer_text: The US Net Zero Advisory Board was launched on 1st October 2024.  The Board, supported by Joe Kennedy III, will leverage the world-class expertise of the North’s strategic diaspora community. The purpose of this Board is to continue to enhance the North's position as a global leader in the green economy. The Board brings an immense amount of knowledge and expertise, from a US market perspective, but also an industry perspective. I see the role of the Board as supporting the advancement of the green economy here.  This includes raising international awareness of our Green Economy and Net Zero strengths and advising on proposition development initially targeted at the US. We anticipate that the Board will also facilitate partnerships and introductions, foster collaboration, and offer guidance on entering the US market.Board members are engaged in a voluntary capacity based on their connection to the North and their willingness to support the growth of the Green Economy here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the creation of a new renewable electricity support scheme.
answer_text: Action 17 in my Department’s recently published business plan has a target date of March 2025 to publish the final design of a renewable electricity support scheme for the north.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 16237/22-27, to detail the steps his Department is taking to be self-sufficient in affordable renewable energy.
answer_text: The Executive’s Energy Strategy aims to deliver self-sufficiency in affordable renewable energy for the North annually.  Details of the targeted milestones for this year can be viewed in the Energy Strategy Action Plan 2024 - Energy Strategy Action Plan 2024 | Department for the Economy (economy-ni.gov.uk)

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the actions he is taking to support (i) onshore; and (ii) offshore wind energy.
answer_text: I recognize the key role renewable generation, including wind energy, will have in our energy future and have made ‘delivering on decarbonisation’ one of the Department’s four priority objectives.	We are also collaborating across government to ensure we deliver on the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 target of at least 80% of electricity consumption to come from renewable sources by 2030 and my Department are leading on the establishment of a support scheme for renewable electricity which is one of the essential steps to meeting this target.		Action 17 in my Department’s recently published business plan has a target date of March 2025 to publish the final design of a renewable electricity support scheme for the north.		Offshore renewable energy will also have a key role to play in achieving net zero and we remain focused on delivering our offshore ambitions from 2030. Departmental officials have developed a draft Offshore Renewable Energy Action Plan (OREAP) to ensure that offshore renewable energy is delivered in an environmentally, socially, and economically sustainable way for all of society.  	The Department and our key stakeholders continue to work collaboratively towards delivery of the actions as outlined within the draft OREAP.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the implementation of the Northern Ireland Cancer Strategy 2022-2032.
answer_text: Significant progress has been made to deliver a wide range of key cancer strategy actions, for example:Two Rapid Diagnosis Centres (RDC) delivering a vague symptoms pathway for people with vague but worrying symptoms which may be cancer, for patients across the region.Haematology pathway reviews have been completed. Officials are currently implementing the recommendations to help improve service delivery and sustainability.We have invested in oncology and haematology stabilisation plans and Clinical Nurse Specialists, to ensure that the services are better staffed.A review of cancer support services for Adolescents and Young Adults has also been completed, with work progressing to implement the recommendations.On 25 September 2024, I announced plans to establish a regional Breast Cancer Services, with a regional waiting list for breast assessment to be established in the coming months. A directory of cancer support services has been developed to help signpost those impacted by a cancer diagnosis to support services in their area. Work to develop a Cancer Research Strategy is progressing, with plans to publish during the current financial year. Northern Ireland was the first part of the UK to commence work to implement an optimal care pathway for pancreatic cancer patients. There is undoubtedly more to be done, and additional investment is required, but the cancer strategy is already starting to deliver benefits for cancer patients in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the establishment of a regional task and finish group to help facilitate the introduction of Palforzia in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Following previous communications in May on the subject a regional Task and Finish Group is currently being convened to consider the range of options that are available to develop a service model for the delivery of this novel peanut allergy treatment in NI.   Paediatricians and specialist allergy nurses across all Trusts as well as service planners will be members of the group.  It is expected the group will commence its considerations in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 16085/22-27, to provide a timeline for when the formula for the funding allocation for the new Local Economic Partnerships will be decided.
answer_text: Departmental officials are engaging with key stakeholders over the coming weeks regarding funding allocation for the Local Economic Partnerships. Decisions on funding will be made as soon as practicable after this.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister why the target to achieve 80 per cent renewable electricity by 2030 was not in the draft Programme for Government.
answer_text: The PfG does not include everything that every department is working to deliver. The Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 sets a 2030 target of at least 80% of electricity consumption to come from renewable sources.  An Electricity and Renewable Generation statistical report is published by the Department for Economy each quarter, including a measure of the current performance against the 2030 target.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide a timeline for the review of Together: Building a United Community.
answer_text: A final draft of the Together: Building a United Community review is currently under development by officials. The recommendations of this review will inform the timeline for the development of a refreshed approach to Good Relations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to provide a timeline for the full implementation of all accepted recommendations under the Gillen Review.
answer_text: I remain committed to the full implementation of the Gillen Review, and I recently agreed to extend the implementation programme to the end of the current Assembly mandate (2027) when I expect the agreed recommendations to be completed. However, implementation is complex and cannot be achieved by my Department alone, so a collaborative and partnership approach is necessary to deliver the transformational change required. For example, some recommendations, such as those relating to Relationship and Sexuality Education, do not fall within the functions of my Department and are for the Department of Education to take forward; likewise, others sit within the remit of the Department of Health.Full implementation is also dependent upon the allocation of appropriate funding and resources and having the opportunity, in an already truncated mandate, to introduce the new legislation required to deliver some Gillen recommendations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs why the Green Growth Strategy has not been published.
answer_text: My Department has been leading the development of the Green Growth Strategy, on behalf of the Northern Ireland Executive, collaborating with other departments, local government and key stakeholders from across the business, voluntary and community sectors.The draft Green Growth Strategy is the Executive’s multi-decade strategy for climate, the environment & the economy.  Its aim is to ensure future government policy making here has climate and environmental action at its core.In October 2021, the then Executive agreed the text of a draft Green Growth Strategy. The strategy was issued for an eight-week public consultation, revised and updated to incorporate feedback from the consultation exercise and the impact of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) which received Royal Assent on 6 June 2022. While the updated strategy was agreed by the former DAERA Minister, it was not possible to secure approval in the absence of an Executive.Due to the extended period of time between the fall of the previous Executive and the establishment of the current Executive, it has been necessary to re-issue the draft Strategy to all Departments for review to ensure its accuracy and relevance. The Strategy has been updated to reflect feedback and is being finalised prior to consideration by the Executive.I intend to take the Strategy to the Executive this autumn for approval to publish.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has undertaken an assessment of the impact that having no Green Growth Strategy has had on Northern Ireland’s dependency on oil and transition to renewable energy sources.
answer_text: An assessment is not necessary because the transition away from fossil fuels towards affordable renewable energy is being delivered via the Executive’s Energy Strategy. DfE is committed to delivering at least 80% of electricity consumption from renewable sources by 2030 as the Climate Change (NI) Act 2022 requires.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the impact the lack of a Green Growth Strategy is having on his Department’s ability to grow the green economy.
answer_text: The Department for Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs has been leading the development of the Green Growth Strategy, on behalf of the Northern Ireland Executive. The Strategy will be submitted to the Executive for approval in the near future.I am very aware that the green economy is a key driver of growth and prosperity in the North and my Department is proactively supporting growth in this area via Invest NI.Invest NI is empowering businesses to fully optimise green economy opportunities and is supporting business decarbonisation in the drive towards net zero. Invest NI’s Draft Business Strategy 2024-2027 also outlines key strategic priorities, including a focus on developing and achieving sustainability, as well as driving investment in net zero initiatives and technologies that enhance energy and resource efficiency.		Invest NI also plays a crucial role in delivering my Department’s Low Carbon/Net Zero Sectoral Action Plan which outlines objectives to promote the green economy in the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his Department's assessment of (i) carbon capture technology; (ii) its state of development; and (iii) any carbon capture projects being considered and developed for use in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department’s primary approach to reduce carbon emissions is to be self-sufficient in affordable renewable energy. We have the resources including wind, biomethane and geothermal to do this, rather than continuing to burn fossil fuels, capturing the released Carbon Dioxide (CO2), then transporting and storing within expensive carbon capture infrastructure. 		This approach promotes the use of our natural resources but also reflects the scale and type of our economy, as well as limited access to carbon capture infrastructure.  The north of Ireland is dominated by Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs) with almost 80,000 registered businesses in operation, geographically dispersed across the country.	This is significantly different from Britain, where large industrial clusters exist with relatively close access to potential, large scale carbon capture infrastructure. I note the British Government’s recent announcement to provide funding for such clusters.		The Climate Change Committee (CCC) have advised that there are high volumes of potential CO2 storage under the seabed surrounding Britain in saline aquifers and in depleted oil and gas fields. However, the storage sites with best potential are mostly under the North Sea. The CCC have highlighted that the north of Ireland is not geologically optimal for CO2 storage.	Although the CCC points out the lack of potential for Ireland, I expect there will be a need for Britain to commission a site in the Irish Sea to meet the needs of the western side of Britain. Such a site could meet the needs of Scotland, NW England, Wales and Ireland. The depleted South Morecambe gas field in the Irish Sea has been identified as a candidate site.		The CCC also highlighted the economic issues facing the north in developing carbon storage solutions. Capturing, transporting and storing CO2 is expected to be more expensive for the north compared to Britain. This is because the larger industrial clusters in Britain will produce more CO2 going into each CO2 network, leading to lower unit costs due to economies of scale, and the transport distance from source to storage location will be much shorter.		Studies have also identified the potential for other candidate sites within the Irish Sea for geological storage of CO2 for Ireland. However further work needs to be carried out with the Irish Government, the devolved administrations in Scotland and Wales, and the authorities in NW England, together with large industrial emitters, to assess the technical and economic case for a shared carbon capture storage site in the Irish Sea.		My Department continues to engage with relevant stakeholders on the various elements required to develop feasible carbon capture solutions.	This includes research projects commissioned through Geological Survey Northern Ireland and engagement with colleagues in the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero to determine the legislative and regulatory frameworks around car

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) to detail how he is working (a) within his Department; and (b) across Departments, to reach Northern Ireland’s agreed climate targets; and (ii) for his assessment of progress made since the introduction of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: My Department is leading on development of Northern Ireland’s first Climate Action Plan (CAP), working together across all departments, to set out Northern Ireland’s approach to meeting the carbon budget for 2023 to 2027 and establishing a Net Zero pathway through a set of proposals and policies for emissions reduction. Collaboration is occurring at strategic and delivery levels, within DAERA and across departments through a Strategic Oversight Group (a cross-Departmental group comprising senior officials from all NI Departments), a Sector and Departmental Leads Group, an Evidence and Analysis group (a cross-departmental group which aims to support the provision of robust evidence, analyses and advice to inform CAP development) and supported by a core CAP team in DAERA. In addition to progressing the development of the Climate Action Plan, I have already introduced public body reporting legislation and I am currently engaging with Executive colleagues on setting Northern Ireland’s first three carbon budgets and appropriate emission reduction targets for 2030 and 2040.  I also hope to issue a consultation on the establishment of the Just Transition Commission in the coming weeks.I can assure you that I am fully committed to delivering on the climate targets within the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, and my officials are working closely with all departments to take this forward.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the publication of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, for a breakdown of how the £45 million for the Local Economic Partnerships will be allocated to local council areas.
answer_text: The formula for funding allocation for the new Local Economic Partnerships has not yet been decided. My officials will be engaging with local councils and other stakeholders on this over the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the number of staff that will be allocated to the Regional Balance Unit; and (ii) the objectives of the unit.
answer_text: Internal departmental restructuring of the Economic Policy Group is ongoing including the establishment of this unit. Due to ongoing consultation with staff I cannot confirm the number of full time equivalent staff that will work in the Unit. The objective of the Unit is to support delivery of my Sub-Regional Economic Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the establishment of an Enhance Investment Zone to harness £150 million of available funding focusing on green technologies and skills.
answer_text: The British government has yet to confirm its position on the Enhanced Investment Zone for the North. My officials have been advised by their counterparts in the Ministry of Housing Communities and Local Government that a position on the funding may be provided in the October budget, however it could also take to Spring 2025 budget before the position is known.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how Invest NI will enhance the presence and function in its regional offices by (i) allocating additional staff; and (ii) increasing leadership, as stated in the Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: Regional balance is a commitment in the draft Programme for Government.In line with this commitment, I recently announced a new approach to regional economic development, and Invest NI subsequently launched its Business Strategy for consultation. In this strategy the new Regional operating model is prioritised. It includes a commitment to move from a 4 office model to a 5 office model and to increasing the headcount of Regional Offices by an initial 40%. The posts, ranging from support staff to senior leadership will be filled by a mix of redeployment, internal recruitment, and external recruitment where skills gaps exist within the organisation.The Business Strategy also commits Invest NI to delivering 65% of its investments outside of the Belfast Metropolitan area by the end of the three-year period. This is the first time Invest NI has set corporate regional targets. My Department and Invest NI are firmly committed to delivering greater regional economic balance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how his Department will (i) allocate staff; and (ii) streamline points of contact for stakeholders, as stated in the Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: Internal departmental restructuring of the Economic Policy Group is ongoing including the establishment of the Regional Balance Unit.Formation of Local Economic Partnerships with advice and support from dedicated departmental and Invest NI officials will provide a voice for local economic stakeholders, both within my Department and across government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the publication of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, to detail the engagement he has had with the Minister for Infrastructure in relation to the transport links in the North West region to help deliver regional balance.
answer_text: The Sub-Regional Economic Plan notes that there are some macro level issues that the entire region faces, and which will require a joined-up approach across government. This includes a lack of transport links. I am acutely aware of how important the links provided by the City of Derry Airport are to help achieve regional balance in the North West.In my statement to the Assembly on 1 October launching the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, I confirmed that my Department is in the process of taking responsibility for the funding provided by Derry City and Strabane District Council to the City of Derry Airport. This has been in joint agreement with the Minister for InfrastructureTaking responsibility for the funding provided by the local Council to the City of Derry Airport aims to lift that burden from local ratepayers, and enable the Council to reinvest the £3m funding in initiatives to support regional balance. It will also ensure the future of the airport within the wider North West economic ecosystem.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the publication of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, to detail the engagements he has had with the Irish Government in relation to the establishment of a new Regional Balance Unit.
answer_text: My officials have been engaging with their counterparts in the Irish Government to discuss the development and implementation of my Sub-Regional Economic Plan. These discussions are ongoing and have covered a range of issues related to sub-regional economic development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the publication of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, to detail the steps his Department will take to ensure the Local Economic Partnership reflects the full breadth of local stakeholders.
answer_text: Membership guidance will be provided, and parameters will be set to ensure that the Local Economic Partnerships reflect key stakeholders in the locality.  Councils will be required to submit to the Department for approval, their proposals for the structure and composition for partnerships in their area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the publication of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, to detail the engagement he has had with the Minister for Infrastructure in relation to investment in water and sewerage infrastructure.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the constraints that infrastructure imposes on our economic resilience and capacity for growth.  These constraints are particularly important for work I will take forward under my Sub-Regional Economic Plan. Appropriate regional infrastructure is needed if we are to successfully address regional economic imbalance.  I have engaged with the Minister for Infrastructure and he is committed to working with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.  The reality is that the Department for Infrastructure has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the publication of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, to detail (i) where the new Invest NI Regional Office will be located; and (ii) the number of staff that will be allocated to this office.
answer_text: Invest NI is in the middle of commercial negotiations on the location of this additional regional office. The location will be announced once this process is complete.As part of the Regional Plan we are putting in place new staffing structures for each of the Regional Offices. This work is ongoing so we cannot confirm final numbers per office at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQW 11611/22-27, for an update on the timeline for full implementation of the McCloud Remedy.
answer_text: Under the remedy legislation, unfunded public service schemes should provide members who have already left service with their ‘Immediate Choice’ by 31 March 2025. Members will then have 12 months from receipt to make their choice of either pre-2015 or post-2015 scheme benefits. This would complete the implementation of the ‘retrospective’ element of the remedy.The legislation also requires that active members of unfunded schemes are provided with a ‘Deferred Choice’ about their ‘prospective’ remedy benefit entitlements prior to their retirement. In the Civil Service Pension Scheme, where my Department has direct responsibility, all active in-scope members now routinely receive that choice immediately before their chosen point of retirement, and as part of the normal pre-retirement process.  This process will continue until all individual NICS scheme members impacted by the remedy have retired and received their pension benefits. Each individual public service scheme is now responsible for full administrative implementation of the remedy for its members.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any evaluation undertaken on the level of methane emissions from the illegal Mobuoy waste dump.
answer_text: A Gas Risk Assessment for the Mobuoy site was completed in 2016, with a review of this Gas Risk Assessment carried out in 2022. Both Gas Risk Assessments are published and readily available on the NIEA website at - Landfill Gas Risk Assessment (https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/landfill-gas-risk-assessments)
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any mitigations in place to deal with the methane emissions from the illegal Mobuoy waste dump.
answer_text: Landfill gas modelling completed as part of the Updated Gas risk Assessment (September 2022) identified a number of potential  risks to human health from landfill gas. The modelled potential risks identified are mainly limited to areas within the site boundary. In some areas modelled potential risks may extend to a distance of up to 12m from the site boundary.  Since the nearest residential or commercial buildings are significantly further than 12m from the site boundary, the potential risk to off-site receptors is considered to be negligible. In the short term, modelled potential risks identified in the gas risk assessment are kept under review by routine landfill gas monitoring, carried out under NIEA’s Environmental Monitoring Programme.  The long-term mitigation of landfill gas emissions is a strategic objective of the draft Optimum Remediation Strategy.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, in light of the publication of the Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy and Performance Framework 2024 - 2031, for her assessment of the capacity among people working in front line and public facing roles to identify and respond effectively to domestic or sexual abuse.
answer_text: It is essential that those working in front line and public facing roles have an   appropriate awareness and understanding of domestic and sexual abuse and are equipped to respond effectively.  A significant programme of training was rolled out in advance of the introduction of the domestic abuse offence and continues to be built upon.  Details can be found in the second annual training statement Training Statement for the Domestic Abuse Offence | Department of Justice (justice-ni.gov.uk), published under the Domestic Abuse and Civil Proceedings Act (Northern Ireland) 2021 on 7th October 2024. I am clear that more work needs to be done in relation to training and that this needs to be targeted not just at those working in the justice system, but at frontline professionals across a range of sectors and disciplines.  The new Cross-Departmental Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy contains a Key Priority around building capacity across public facing roles.  Through the Strategy’s integrated Performance Framework, we will continue to measure the number and range of frontline staff completing training and evaluation of same.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the co-operation that will take place between the Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy and Performance Framework and the Tackling Paramilitarism, Criminality and Organised Crime Programme to ensure a focus on women who are subject to sexual and domestic abuse by paramilitary organisations.
answer_text: The Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy, which aims to address all forms of domestic and sexual abuse, is one of a range of strategic initiatives in place across the Executive to ensure a joined up, multi-agency approach to supporting women and girls affected by domestic and sexual abuse - including those affected specifically by paramilitary violence.  Other interventions include the Executive’s new Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls (EVAWG) which is designed to address the whole range of gender-based violence, abuse and harm that is disproportionately experienced by women and girls – again, including that perpetrated by paramilitaries. Both of these strategies involve working collaboratively with the Executive’s Programme on Paramilitarism and Organised Crime (EPPOC), for which I am lead Minister; EPPOC has in place a range of initiatives aimed at supporting women in areas most impacted by ongoing paramilitary activity.  At Ministerial and official level there is close and regular contact across these various initiatives to ensure that efforts are aligned. For example, reciprocal participation by officials in the various oversight and governance arrangements provides opportunities for discussion and collaboration in relation to the parallels between the strategies and planning for their delivery.  It is important that these initiatives are viewed as complementary efforts working together, not only to support those who continue to face harm, but also to ensure that we make every effort to prevent such harm occurring.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the Executive agreed to pause work on the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.
answer_text: In May 2024, the Executive agreed that operationalising the package of interim measures for early learning and childcare provision in the 2024/25 financial year would be the primary focus for the following 6 months.  Once these measures were up and running and information started to flow from the data collection programme, it was confirmed that work on the longer-term strategy would pick up pace.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on any work his Department has undertaken to address high insurance costs, including through detailing the current variation between those costs in Northern Ireland and the rest of the UK.
answer_text: Financial Services are a reserved matter and regulation of the insurance industry is the responsibility of the Financial Conduct Authority - any concerns or complaints should be raised with them.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how his Department plans to address regional imbalance through the Digital Skills Action Plan.
answer_text: When I set out my vision for the economy, one of key objectives was promoting regional balance. 	The Plan includes creating alternative pathways into the digital sector, giving more people the opportunity to access the training and skills needed, via the route that best suits their individual needs.	Access to digital courses is available throughout the region, and are delivered by our colleges, universities and online.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any changes CCEA plans to make to the curriculum to meet the strategic ambitions of the Digital Skills Action Plan.
answer_text: Ensuring that our children and young people are digitally literate and equipped to succeed in a digital world is a key priority and area of focus for my Department. In an era dominated by technological advancements, equipping students with digital proficiency is imperative for their future success.Working in collaboration with key stakeholders including CCEA and industry professionals, my Department is progressing a number of actions which aim to enhance and integrate digital skills across all areas of the curriculum at all key stages.  These focus on developing the digital curriculum framework, sharing of good practice, teacher training and professional development and resourcing schools.Earlier in the year I also announced a major £3 million investment in digital devices to support pupil learning. Around 8500 new devices have been rolled out to post-primary schools. This will deliver high quality digital infrastructure to schools supporting learning, teaching and administration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how he plans to advance education on technological skills following the publication of the Digital Skills Action Plan.
answer_text: Ensuring that our children and young people are digitally literate and equipped to succeed in a digital world is a key priority and area of focus for my Department. In an era dominated by technological advancements, equipping students with digital proficiency is imperative for their future success.Working in collaboration with key stakeholders including CCEA and industry professionals, my Department is progressing a number of actions which aim to enhance and integrate digital skills across all areas of the curriculum at all key stages.  These focus on developing the digital curriculum framework, sharing of good practice, teacher training and professional development and resourcing schools.Earlier in the year I also announced a major £3 million investment in digital devices to support pupil learning. Around 8500 new devices have been rolled out to post-primary schools. This will deliver high quality digital infrastructure to schools supporting learning, teaching and administration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has established a cross-departmental oversight group to oversee the list of asks published in the Magee Taskforce Interim Report, September 2024.
To ask the Minister for Communities when he plans to bring a draft anti-poverty strategy to the Executive for agreement.
To ask the Minister of Justice whether she will raise the legal threshold before the PSNI can request a rape survivors’ counselling notes.
answer_text: ORAL QUESTION No 2 - AQO 871/22-27ANSWERPSNI is responsible for ensuring it has the legal basis for seeking third party material including counselling records.Investigating officers are required by law to follow all reasonable lines of inquiry, which is fundamental to a fair, effective justice system. However, counselling notes should only be requested if identified as relevant to an issue in a case. I am informed that PSNI will never request counselling notes as a matter of course and that such requests must be balanced against the potential benefit to the investigation and the intrusion of collecting it. Authorisation must be obtained from an officer of at least the rank of Chief Inspector.In addition, I have been informed that PSNI is in the final stages of adopting the National Police Chiefs’ Council draft forms for requesting third party material, in conjunction with stakeholders. Therefore, I am satisfied there is no requirement to change the legal threshold on when rape survivors' counselling notes can be requested by PSNI.I do recognise the impact there can be on victims when their personal information is used without their knowledge or control, and I have asked officials to consult on the need for greater regulation of the use of victims’ private information within the court disclosure process and what legislative changes are required to address this.The Commissioner Designate for Victims of Crime is leading multi-agency work involving my Department, criminal justice organisations, representatives of the legal profession and the Information Commissioner’s Office, to identify ways in which victims’ personal information can be better protected within the wider third-party material disclosure process. This work should help to inform operational improvements as well as the development of legislative proposals.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the research report on Paramilitary–related Gendered Coercive Control.
answer_text: Professor Aisling Swaine’s research into Paramilitary-related Gendered Coercive Control is both timely and important.  It raises awareness of the lived experiences of women who have faced paramilitary-related gendered coercive control. The report and the testimonies of the victim-survivors makes for harrowing and sobering reading. These women have faced horrendous physical and sexual harm, including threats, “surveillance” and displacement, in relationships where they should have felt safe.The report highlights how much there still is to do to support women who have faced paramilitary violence and coercion. As both Justice Minister and the lead Minister of the Executive’s Programme on Paramilitarism and Organised Crime, I know how much work is being done to address paramilitary harm in the here and now whilst also trying to break that cycle of harm for future generations. I also know the devastating impacts threat, violence and harm have on individuals, families and entire communities. As this report also points to, Programme research has highlighted the shocking impacts on health, mental health and wellbeing that ongoing paramilitary activity has.This Government recognises that violence does not happen in a vacuum, and that violence against women and girls and paramilitary violence are linked.  Both the Executive’s Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Strategic Framework, and the draft PFG indicate how seriously we take these issues.
