It is imperative that we continue to work together to end the harms caused by these groups – particularly the often ‘hidden’ harms like those outlined by Professor Swaine.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) why there is no reference to spiking in the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls; and (ii) whether they have engaged with the Minister of Justice on this issue.
answer_text: One of the key objectives of the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls is for women and girls to be safe and feel safe everywhere. Sector specific work will be taken forward in order to achieve this objective. A Safer Socialising Steering Group, which contains representatives from a range of departments and organisations including the Department of Justice, Hospitality Ulster, Translink and the PSNI, has been established to develop an action plan to address VAWG issues in the context of socialising. Spiking has been identified as a future agenda item for discussion by this Group. The PSNI Action Plan, Tackling Violence Against Women and Girls, also contains actions in relation to spiking.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) how his Department plans to support existing childcare and early years programmes such as Sure Start and Toybox; and (ii) the total amount of funding that has been provided to those programmes in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Additional funding made available by the Executive this year for early learning and childcare has enabled me to provide an increased allocation to stabilise and expand a number of existing early years programmes such as Sure Start and Toybox.  The tables below provide the total amount of funding made available by my Department to Sure Start and Toybox for the period 2020/21 to 2024/25.Sure StartYearTotal DE Funding2024/25£33.67m2023/24£30.60m2022/23£30.68m2021/22£29.77m2020/21£27.4mToyboxYearTotal DE Funding2024/25£466k2023/24£356k2022/23£356k2021/22£356k2020/21£356k

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on her Department’s plans to increase the minimum age of criminal responsibility.
answer_text: I have been clear about my commitment to raising the minimum age of criminal responsibility to bring Northern Ireland more into line with international standards, including directives from the UN Committee on the Rights of the Child.  At 10 years of age, our current minimum is the lowest in Europe, along with England and Wales.  To change the law in this area, Executive support is required which previously has not been forthcoming.  To inform a further discussion, my Department carried out a public consultation in 2022 to seek views on increasing the minimum age of criminal responsibility.  Overwhelmingly, over 83% of respondents agreed that the age should be increased beyond 10 years, with the majority supporting an increase to 14 years.  My officials have developed several options for progressing this issue, based on views expressed during the consultation.  I have shared a draft paper with my Executive colleagues to seek their views on these proposals and I am awaiting their responses.  Any further steps I can take to effect legislative change to the minimum age will be dependent on  Executive approval.  In the meantime, we will continue to implement the successful policy to divert children of all ages – but in particular younger children – from the formal criminal justice system and deal with them through early intervention and community support.  This is in line with the actions set out in my Department’s Strategic Framework for Youth Justice.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail how paramilitary-related coercive control will be addressed through the implementation of the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls.
answer_text: The Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls (EVAWG) seeks to address the full spectrum of violence, harm and abuse faced by women and girls here, and recognises the need to work strategically and collaboratively across government and society with those who are dealing with other parts of the problem of violence in our society, such as paramilitary violence.  Our officials in the EVAWG Programme team work closely with their counterparts in the Executive Programme on Paramilitarism & Organised Crime (EPPOC) in the Department of Justice.  Representatives from EPPOC were co-design partners in the development of the EVAWG Strategic Framework.  Senior officials from both programmes are represented on the programmes’ governance structures, ensuring full visibility of all ongoing work.  Officials have collaborated on a range of issues to ensure alignment of overall effort, including developing a proof-of-concept shared data platform, and the recent EPPOC ‘Ending the Harm’ campaign which had a focus on violence against women.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the steps his Department is taking in relation to moss and weed removal on footpaths in Currynierin, Derry.
answer_text: The footpaths in Currynierin are programmed to receive a third weed and moss treatment by the end of September.  In meantime, an inspector will review the condition of the paths to determine if scraping and cleaning is also required.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister (i) whether the £3 million funding provided for a Change Fund represents the total amount of funding allocated to implement the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls; and (ii) to detail how the £3 million will be allocated.
answer_text: The Strategic Framework to End Violence against Women and Girls is supported by an initial two-year Delivery Plan over 2024/2026 with 14 key actions. The Change Fund is only the first action within this plan and has a £3m allocation. 	The Change Fund will support grassroots groups and community and voluntary sector organisations to do more to further build capacity and be better equipped to address the significant issue in their area. Further details on the Change Fund will be announced in the time ahead.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the targets that the Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan will set to ensure any support provided for entrepreneurs will be regionally balanced.
answer_text: The Sub-Regional Economic Plan provides a framework to address economic inequalities and establishes a new collaborative way of working between the Department, its arms-length bodies, and local stakeholders. The entrepreneurship programme Go Succeed, is led by local councils and delivered through Local Enterprise Agencies. I have tasked Invest NI with strengthening its regional presence, including a revitalised regional office network which will support local entrepreneurs and achieve higher levels of entrepreneurship.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they will commit to ensuring the provision of a stable source of state funding for Women’s Aid NI.
answer_text: We recognise the extremely valuable work undertaken by the community and voluntary sector, including Women’s Aid Northern Ireland, and acknowledge the challenges presented by the ongoing difficult budgetary situation. Women’s Aid were a key partner in the Co-design process to develop the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls (EVAWG) Strategic Framework, which launched on 16 September 2024. The Framework recognises the need to ensure services for victims deliver a consistent trauma informed response across a diverse range of needs, and that we will only end violence against women and girls through partnership working across government and society. The first EVAWG Delivery Plan, which has been approved by the Executive, has been developed to maximise the impact of available funding.  The Plan contains up to £3m across 2 years to support grassroots community and the Community Voluntary Sector experts in violence against women and girls, to work on prevention programmes and initiatives, over the next 2 years.  This does not displace existing policy remits or funding streams.The Minister of Health also recognises the important role played by the voluntary and community sector, and has indicated that 2024/25 funding will be a transitional arrangement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the changes he will make to the school curriculum by 2027 to meet the objectives of the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls.
answer_text: As the member may know the First Minister and deputy First Minister launched the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls and first Delivery Plan on 16 September.Addressing violence against women and girls is an issue for all areas of society and education has its role in supporting that.  I will want to take time to consider whether any changes that are identified are taken forward in guidance or whether legislation is needed.Our curriculum from foundation stage to key stage 4 is already designed to provide opportunities to educate young people to develop healthy relationships.  Pupils have the opportunity to explore their management of a range of feelings and emotions and those of others; initiating, developing and sustaining mutually satisfying relationships, explore the qualities of a loving, respectful relationship and develop coping strategies to deal with challenging relationship scenarios.Schools are supported to deliver the curriculum by the development, by the Council for the Curriculum, Examinations and Assessment, of teaching materials including materials in respect of Domestic and Sexual Violence and Abuse, Healthy, Positive Sexual Expression and Relationships and Consent all of which are relevant to the strategy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) why measures related to the flying of flags were not included in the draft Programme for Government; and (ii) whether they plan to publish their Department’s next steps in relation to the Commission on Flags, Identity, Culture and Tradition Final Report.
answer_text: We have agreed that the recommendations of the FICT Report will be taken forward as part of the current review of the NI Executive’s good relations strategy: Together: Building a United Community (T:BUC).The Programme for Government (PfG) cannot, by its nature, include everything that the Executive is doing, nor everything that is important within individual departments. However, the draft PfG does include a commitment to refreshing the T:BUC strategy, and FICT now falls within the scope of this work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he has instructed officials to pause work on the Early Education and Childcare Strategy in order to deliver the Childcare Subsidy Scheme.
answer_text: The Executive agreed in May 2024 that the immediate priority should be implementation of a package of early learning and childcare measures to ease pressures on parents and providers in 2024/25.  This not only included delivery of the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme, but also measures to stabilise and support the sector, expand the Pre-school Education Programme and build the evidence base through an extensive data collection programme.  Work on all of these is progressing and will inform the longer-term strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will instruct his officials to include measures related to the interest rates charged on student loans in the review of higher education.
answer_text: The scope of the review of higher education funding is currently being drafted.  However, at this stage, I do not envisage that tuition fee loan repayment terms and conditions will be within scope as the interest rate and repayment period are relatively favourable. In addition, the cost of developing a bespoke alternative is likely to be prohibitive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the programme for the new consultation on the future use of Fort George.
answer_text: My Department intends to undertake a Public Consultation on the revised Fort George Masterplan in either late 2024 or early 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the preparations that have been made by his Department and its arm's-length bodies, including NI Water, for the expansion of the University of Ulster Magee campus; and (ii) the capital plans his Department and its arm's-length bodies have in place to facilitate the expansion of the campus to at least 10,000 students.
answer_text: My Department is working with its arm’s length bodies and other stakeholders to identify potential impacts on local infrastructure that may be caused by the proposed expansion of University of Ulster Magee campus.  This includes the development of the North West Transport Plan that covers the Derry City and Strabane District Council area, which is currently part of an ongoing Public engagement exercise. In addition, public transport, active travel and traffic management interventions such as potential changes to current restrictions and traffic calming will be developed in conjunction with the evolving proposals for the University.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the amount of funding allocated for the new Challenge Fund Incentive Scheme; and (ii) why the fund is not referenced in the Delivering the Economic Vision: A three year forward look and 2024-25 Action Plan.
answer_text: Implementation of the Apprenticeship Inclusion Challenge Fund will require a budget of £830k over three years (£192k -2024/25, £498k - 2025/26 and £140k - 2026/27) including staff costs. As budget beyond this financial year has not yet been agreed, future costs will be prioritised from the available budget allocation. The Fund will be included within the three-year Apprenticeship Action Plan referenced within Delivering the Economic Vision: A three year forward look and 2024-25 Action Plan, and which will be published during this financial year. Engagement is ongoing with stakeholders and my Department will publish the Action Plan following this period of work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) any work to expand late night bus and rail services in the Derry City and Strabane District Council area; and (ii) how his Department plans to develop late night travel options from the North West Transport Hub.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:“Translink NI Railways are currently reviewing the pre-Christmas ‘Nightmover’ service provision for December 2024.  Subject to funding it is proposed to offer later services to/from the North West Transport Hub on Friday and Saturday evenings during December.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the extent to which other government departments have been involved in the development of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the amount of investment provided to the Northern Ireland Higher Education Innovation Fund in each of the last five years; and (ii) the purpose of that investment.
answer_text: Over the past 5 years the baseline Higher Education Innovation Fund has remained static at £3.96m per annum. Non-recurrent supplements of £1.8m in academic year 2021/22, £2m in academic year 2023/24 and £3.96m in the current academic year, were allocated to broaden the capacity of our universities to undertake a wider array of Knowledge Exchange activity.		The purpose of the Higher Education Innovation Fund is to support our Universities to engage with business, public and third sector organisations, community bodies and the wider public, to exchange knowledge, promote innovation and increase the economic and societal benefit from their work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the steps being taken to address flooding on Strand Road, Derry, including any current plans to improve drainage in the area.
answer_text: At present there are no plans to upgrade or improve drainage infrastructure on the Strand Road, Derry. The existing drainage infrastructure will be jetted and cleaned in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service Maintenance policy prior to the winter period to ensure it is working to full capacity.My Department is also leading on the development of a Living With Water in Derry Plan, to help protect against flooding, enhance the environment and support economic growth. The draft Plan is due to be published for consultation later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the steps being taken to reduce the waiting list for percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy insertion surgery, including in relation to children on this waiting list; and (ii) the number of children that have been waiting for this surgery in each of the last three years, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: The issue of access to percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) insertion for children and young people is an important issue as is the range of all surgical procedures that face children and young people in Northern Ireland.  This procedure is a complex procedure that requires specialised clinical and nursing input prior to, during and post procedure.  My officials within SPPG have now carried out some initial scoping on the question that you have raised with senior officials in BHSCT and have been informed of the following.(ii)There are 93 children currently on the waiting list.  Due to the introduction of the new ICT system, Encompass at BHSCT it is not possible to examine the data further at this stage.  It is hoped that when the Encompass system is fully operational across all Trusts in NI that the extraction and publication of data will be more streamlined.  Each case is constantly assessed by the specialist team at the RBHSC and dealt with as quickly as possible based on clinical priority and chronological order(i) For example, at the moment a paediatric surgeon is currently validating the percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) insertion waiting list so waiting times on the list may change dependent on any change to the patient’s condition or clinical priority. This validation process will identity which children are suitable for possible transfer to Alder Hey Hospital in Liverpool for the procedure.  Clinical criteria, agreed by the specialist team, will be in place to determine this.  BHSCT and Alder Hey are discussing the feasibility of this initiative.Belfast Trust are also hoping to provide a waiting list initiative in October, to carry out additional sessions on a Friday, with patients being nursed in RBHSC over the weekend. This will of course be dependent on theatres and beds availability and unscheduled care pressures.As well as this, discussions are underway to provide the Belfast Trust paediatric surgeon a gastrostomy surgery list in the Ulster Hospital.  Belfast Trust Gastrostomy Specialist Nurses will provide training to staff in South Eastern Trust to help facilitate this if it can arranged. There is also a review of surgical work performed in RBHSC underway and any work that can be redirected to District General Hospitals will be transferred.  This may free up capacity in RBHSC for Paediatric Surgeons to perform more gastrostomy surgery and other more complex surgery that can only be carried out in RBHSC.The Trust are also examining potential partnership working with the Private Sector.  This would mean using RBHSC capacity over the weekend to deliver Waiting List Initiatives in the Children’s Hospital and will be dependent on unscheduled care pressures. My Officials will continue to meet with BHSCT to discuss these potential solutions to examine their progress and success.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how his Department intends to work with Translink to promote the night-time economy in the Foyle constituency, including whether he will launch a review into current transport provision and its impact on the night-time economy in the Derry city region.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:“In October 2023 Translink enhanced timetables for the Foyle Metro network, following the successful roll-out of the new zero emissions electric bus fleet.  The enhancements included additional later evening services on Friday and Saturday nights.   Derry~Londonderry has become one of the first cities in the UK and Ireland to operate a fully zero emissions urban bus service and these latest service enhancements further reflect Translink’s efforts to make life better connected and support the local economy, environment and social prosperity.      Translink will continue to engage with stakeholders including Derry and Strabane Council and City Centre Initiative to promote existing services and review provision subject to available funding”.   For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.In addition to the operational response from Translink, my Department is not currently in a position to provide additional funding for these additional services.  As a result, the introduction of any services must be commercially viable.My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. The value of our infrastructure cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of funding provided to establish a new Skills Fund, broken down by (i) sector; and (ii) the amount of funding allocated.
answer_text: I have provided £11.5m to establish a Skills Fund this financial year, across four sectoral themes, supporting 22 separate initiatives, with allocations as follows: Productivity and Upskilling - £7.8mInclusion, Childcare and Adult Learning - £2.1mSupporting Vocational Education - £1.2mSupporting Skills Development - £0.4m

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for a breakdown of the £1.3 billion investment detailed in the draft Programme for Government that aims to stimulate regeneration and tourism, including what proportion of this funding relates to each City and Growth Deals; and (ii) for a timeline for the delivery of this investment.
answer_text: In addition to tourism and regeneration, the £1.3bn City & Growth Deal funding includes investment in innovation, digital, health, further & higher education and infrastructure.  You will be aware of the disgraceful decision by the British Government to pause its funding commitments to the Causeway Coast and Glens and Mid South West Growth Deals until the Spending Review.  I am in full support of the Finance Minister as she continues to impress upon the Chief Secretary to the Treasury that the British Government must honour the commitments and pledges made to City and Growth Deals.The investment by Deal, subject to satisfactory resolution of the Treasury’s pause, is as follows:DealUKGNI ExecutiveBelfast Region City Deal£350m£350mDerry City and Strabane£105m£105mCauseway Coast and Glens  £36m£36mMid South West  126m£126mComplementary Fund£100mTotals£1.334 bnWhile the Deals are intended to be delivered over a 10-15 year period, I have instructed officials to work more closely with project promoters to accelerate delivery of the £0.8bn for which my Department is accountable.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the adoption of Ivy Mead in the Foyle constituency to the public carriageway.
answer_text: My Department is aware of the adoption status of Ivy Meade Mews.  Whilst a number of phases within the development have been adopted by the Department, several remain unadopted due to outstanding issues with both roads and NI Water infrastructure issues.The existing roads bonds remain sufficient to allow completion of the roads infrastructure to an adoptable standard.  However, there are service strip infringements preventing adoption and officials in my department are working with the developer and local residents to find a resolution.  NI Water are also working with the developer to bring the related infrastructure to an adoptable standard.Ultimately, it is the responsibility of the developer to construct road and water infrastructure to the required standard to facilitate adoption.  My officials will continue to engage with the developer to complete the necessary works and undertake enforcement action should it be required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) whether it is still his policy to expand Ulster University’s Magee campus to at least 10,000 students; and (ii) the steps he will take to ensure that the wording of the Programme of Government is amended to reflect this commitment.
answer_text: As per my Department's Business Plan and the draft Programme for Government the target for Ulster University’s Magee campus is 10,000 students. I established the Magee taskforce to develop and oversee an action plan to deliver on that specific target. The Taskforce's first report was published on 11th September.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, further to the publication of Delivering the Economic Vision, a three-year forward look and 2024/25 Action Plan, whether it his intention to fully implement the Invest NI Review Action Plan by March 2025.
answer_text: In 2024/25, my department will work in partnership with Invest NI to implement the Invest NI Review Action Plan.  As per the published delivery dates, the Invest NI Review Action Plan will be fully implemented by December 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) when the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy will be published; and (ii) whether it will set a target for the reduction of childcare costs by end of the mandate.
answer_text: The package of early learning and childcare measures currently being implemented will be evaluated alongside evidence from administrative and survey data due to be collected in 2024/25.  This will inform the content and timeframe for the longer-term strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, further to the publication of Delivering the Economic Vision, a three-year forward look and 2024/25 Action Plan, whether the Enhanced Investment Zone will be fully delivered by March 2025.
answer_text: The new British government has yet to confirm its position on the Enhanced Investment Zone (EIZ) for the North. However, given the £150m package was agreed as part of the financial package to restore the Executive, it is hoped that there will not be any step back from that commitment.  DfE officials remain in contact with officials from Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (previously DLUHC) on the Enhanced Investment Zone programme.  At a recent meeting MHCLG advised that a position on the funding may be provided in the October budget, however it could also take to Spring 2025 budget before the position is known.The proposed Enhanced Investment Zone will not be fully delivered by March 2025 as funding is to be used over a five-year period once launched. We are working at pace to deliver an Investment Zone as soon as possible once the position is clarified.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) why the Anti-Poverty Strategy is not included in the draft Programme for Government; and (ii) to provide an update on its delivery and funding.
answer_text: The draft Programme for Government (PfG) sets out the Executive’s priorities for this Assembly mandate. The delivery of the Social Inclusion Strategies, which includes the Anti-Poverty Strategy, is referenced in the current draft of the PfG and you will note also that the issue of poverty is referenced at several points throughout the text. Addressing poverty in all its forms is a key priority for me. I am committed to working with all my Ministerial colleagues to ensure that the Anti-Poverty Strategy is taken forward in the most effective and expeditious way possible, taking account of departmental budget allocations and the need to develop a prioritised, sustainable and deliverable Strategy action plan.I am currently considering the next steps to be taken in relation to the Executive Anti-Poverty Strategy. Final decisions on the content and implementation of the Anti-Poverty Strategy will be subject to Executive agreement.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister how the draft Programme for Government will ensure that regional inequalities are fully addressed by each Department.
answer_text: We are committed to promoting regional balance and ensuring that everyone shares in the benefits of prosperity. Throughout the draft Programme for Government a commitment to regional balance can be seen by all Departments, for example, in the Grow a Globally Competitive and Sustainable Economy and the Building New Foundations sections of the document.The Wellbeing Dashboard provides geographic breakdowns of the inequalities that exist between council areas, Assembly constituency areas, between urban and rural areas, and areas of multiple deprivation. This will allow us to see where regional inequalities are in order for action to be taken so they can be effectively addressed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for his assessment of the Holywell Trust’s report, The North West Economic Region; (ii) how it will inform policy development in his Department; and (iii) whether he has considered working with Derry City and Strabane District Council and Donegal County Council to develop a public consultation on attitudes to possible options for closer cross-border partnerships and policy governance.
answer_text: Challenges detailed in the report underline ongoing disparities.  These are felt not just in the border areas but across the whole of the North.   Evidence has informed my forthcoming Sub-Regional Economic Plan – a singular framework that will give a local voice to create solutions tailored to the need of an area. I welcome more cross-border partnerships but now is not the time for consultation but for tackling those challenges that have been highlighted.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will increase funding for (i) the Transport Programme for People with Disabilities; and (ii) Shopmobility Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I have recently awarded an additional 4.2% funding in 2024/25 for the front line service provided by the Disability Action Transport Scheme and Shopmobility schemes above the levels provided in 2023/24.  I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to protect and support front line transport services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the planned timeline for the completion of a traffic assessment at Nixon’s Corner, Braehead Road, Derry.
answer_text: I can confirm that officials in my department will complete a review of the effectiveness of the existing traffic calming measures on the Braehead Road at Nixon’s Corner in the coming weeks. The review will inform any decision to introduce further interventions to encourage vehicles to drive at a more appropriate speed in this residential area.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) whether he will engage with the Housing Executive to progress the required refurbishment and repairs in the Triangle Estate in the Waterside area, Derry; and (ii) the expected completion date of those repairs.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Housing Executive can advise that there are currently 40 jobs issued to our contractor to complete including balcony walkway repairs, parapet wall repairs, underside concrete repairs, roof repairs, gutter repairs, step repair & rain penetration repairs.Repairs issued from February 2022 to September 2024:12no. jobs issued and completed in 202213no. jobs issued and completed in 202340no. jobs issued in 2024The Sub-contractor is currently on-site and we estimate a completion date of the end September 2024 for all works that have been issued this year.In regards to the improvement scheme that has been approved for the complex, there are a number of issues around decanting and leaseholder buybacks. Therefore, we have been considering a number of options for resolving these and implementing the scheme.  As a result, we have had to review our business case and this is currently underway.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to detail the steps he will take to fully fund the service to deliver and support the use of Palforzia by Health and Social Care Trusts; and (ii) whether he will engage with his Department's Strategic Planning and Performance Group to request the development of a business case for the required investment.
answer_text: Officers within the Department and its SPPG are committed to the introduction of an oral immunotherapy service for patients with allergies to nuts and other foodsThe introduction of this model of care is novel, very complex and without precedent in NI and it will be essential to introduce an effective and safe model of care.  Previous engagement has been held with clinical leads across NI to determine an agreed model of care for NI. However, a model of care was not agreed. To help facilitate the introduction of Palforzia in NI a regional task and finish group made up of key paediatric clinical and nursing leads who have expertise in the treatment of allergies will be convened in the coming weeks.  This will be chaired by a consultant with expertise in paediatric allergies. The goal will be to consider the available options and then agree a model of delivery.  Once this has been completed a full business case will be completed that will then consider the costs of introduction for this service. Given the complex clinical nature of this delivery model, as well as its novel nature, it is necessary to allow the clinicians time to agree a model that is safe and effective and these considerations may take a number of months.  The Task and Finish Group will be required to report their findings before the end of the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his work to support community pharmacies.
answer_text: The level of government funding for community pharmacies in NI compares favourably to other parts of the UK. Against the backdrop of the current severe budgetary pressures and the multiple demands for additional funding across all parts of health and social care, additional investment continues to be provided to the community pharmacy sector. Funding for community pharmacies in NI has increased in recent years. The year-start investment total for community pharmacy services was £104m in 2018/19. By 2023/24 it was £128m and in April of this year it stood at £147m. There has therefore been a £43m (41%) increase in six years, and 14% increase in the last year.The Department recognises the valuable role community pharmacy has within health and social care and I am happy to inform the Member that a Strategic Plan for Community Pharmacy 2030 was launched in May 2024. It is available at:  https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/publications/community-pharmacy-strategic-plan-2030. The Strategic Plan for Community Pharmacy aims to fully realise the potential of community pharmacy services to support better health outcomes from medicines and prevent illness. Implementation of the strategy will not only support elderly and vulnerable patients but the whole population.This important piece of work was developed collaboratively by the Department of Health and Community Pharmacy Northern Ireland (CPNI), the representative body for community pharmacy owners, and describes the vision and strategic priorities for community pharmacy up to 2030.The Department will continue to work with CPNI and the pharmacy profession to progress a phased approach to implementation of the Strategic Plan over the coming years subject to funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the number of students enrolled at each Ulster University campus in each of the last three academic years, including the 2024-25 academic year; and (ii) the courses that received an increase in student numbers as a result of the 350 additional student places announced in July 2024.
answer_text: The number of students enrolled at each Ulster University (UU) campus are published by UU at Ulster University Student numbers. Please find attached for your convenience, at Annex A, a copy of the figures for the most recent three academic years, up to 23/24.As the enrolment process for academic year 2024/25 has not yet fully completed, information regarding courses, including those that received an increase in student numbers because of the additional student places, has not yet been compiled.
