question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on the development of a Women’s Health Action Plan; and (ii) for his assessment of the merits of commissioning a comprehensive Women’s Health Strategy.
answer_text: Improving women’s health and wellbeing is a priority for my Department. Work to develop a women’s health action plan is currently in a research and data-gathering phase, with stakeholder engagement on an outline plan to follow in due course. The plan will highlight the wide range of policy and service development initiatives that are in progress within my Department and across the Health and Social Care system, as well as any additional initiatives that can be delivered within available resources over the next three years. By focusing on deliverable high priority short-to-medium term actions, alongside ongoing efforts to stabilise the Health and Social Care system during the remainder of this mandate, it is envisaged that the action plan will help improve services and pave the way for a longer-term women’s health strategy in the future, should the Executive decide to prioritise this.  The merits of a strategic, cross-sectoral approach to women’s health would be to facilitate greater focus on the long-term investment required to address the economic, educational and social determinants of ill-health, inequitable access to services and poor outcomes among women and girls in our society. This in turn would facilitate better management of the growing demand for health and social care services.The evidence base for a women’s health strategy will be informed by the large-scale public listening exercise which is planned and in development by Derry Well Women (DWW) in partnership with Queens University Belfast.  My Department has provided funding of £10k to DWW to ensure that women’s voices are heard and to help shape the how services are delivered in the future.  It is hoped that the first stage of this exercise – a women’s health survey – will be launched in September 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of (i) the progress of the recommendations of the June 2020 Criminal Justice Inspection Northern Ireland report on Child Sexual Exploitation in Northern Ireland; and (ii) whether appropriate data collation and monitoring is in place to assess progress, including by the Child Protection Senior Officials Group.
answer_text: The Criminal Justice Inspection Northern Ireland (CJINI) report, published in June 2020, was an examination of how the justice sector is responding to Child Sexual Exploitation (CSE) and is therefore a matter for the Minister of Justice.Alongside the CJINI inspection, the Safeguarding Board for Northern Ireland (SBNI) commissioned a review of the impact of work undertaken to address CSE, following publication and implementation of the Marshall Inquiry report into CSE in 2015. The SBNI-commissioned review was undertaken by Marcella Leonard; it reported in July 2020. The CJINI inspection and the Leonard review made a number of recommendations and, implementation of those which overlapped, was reported to the cross-departmental Child Protection Senior Officials Group (CPSOG). The Leonard review report was positive on progress made to tackle CSE in Northern Ireland. It found that SBNI member agencies were responding more effectively to CSE, that there was an increased awareness of CSE as a form of abuse, a determination to tackle CSE and evidence of good preventative work.  The report also found CSE leads in Health and Social Care Trusts (HSCTs) and PSNI and joint training to have been effective, that there were examples of good relationships with young people and effective inter-agency working between PSNI and HSCTs, including building intelligence to disrupt perpetrators.   Recommendations from the Leonard review as well as overlapping CJINI report recommendations have now been implemented.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 10493/22-27, when a decision will be made on the proposal for research into the prevalence of non-recent child sexual abuse.
answer_text: We have no current plans for research into the prevalence of non-recent child sexual abuse. This will be kept under review as part of our consideration of the overall research needs of the Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 11361/22-27.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 10864/22-27, how they will ensure that alleviating regional economic imbalance is addressed by all government departments, including through the (i) Programme for Government; and (ii) Legislative Programme.
answer_text: We are committed to ensuring that everyone has the opportunity to thrive, regardless of where they live. The Programme for Government is an Executive strategy and all Ministers and their departments play their role in shaping and agreeing it, before supporting its delivery. Should any of the commitments require legislation, this will be a matter for the Executive to agree, based on proposals from individual Ministers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) whether he has received an update from officials on the review of higher education funding; and (ii) when officials last met to discuss this review.
answer_text: Officials have updated me on preparatory work undertaken and options for a review of higher education funding.  I have agreed to proceed with a review, the scope and approach of which is now being further developed.  Officials last met to discuss the review on Wednesday 10th July 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the engagement she has had with (i) Executive colleagues; and (ii) HM Treasury in relation to the ring-fencing of Barnett consequentials on early education and childcare.
answer_text: Allocations received through Barnett consequentials are unhypothecated meaning that the Executive can determine allocations for specific priorities and programmes regardless of the nature of the comparable spending that gave rise to the Barnett allocation. This is an important principle of devolution therefore, I have not had any engagement with Treasury in relation to the ring-fencing of Barnett consequentials on early education and childcare.		However, the Executive demonstrated its commitment to the Childcare Strategy when it set aside £25 million in the Budget. The Executive has now agreed proposals from the Education Minister which will utilise that funding. 		It will be for the Executive to determine the use of any further additional Barnett consequentials it receives in line with local priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) how much funding has been provided to Altram in each of the last five years; (ii) the steps he is taking to support the Irish-medium Early Years sector; and (iii) his Department’s assessment of whether this sector is fully funded.
answer_text: (i) The table below provides details of the funding which Altram has received from my Department in each of the last five years for early years support:Financial yearIrish-medium Pre-school Support ServiceIrish Medium Pre-school Immersion Education Course developmentTotal2024/25 £158,000 (interim budget allocation)£58,000£216,0002023/24£158,000£58,000£216,0002022/23£175,000£40,000£215,0002021/22£150,000-£150,0002020/21£150,000-£150,000Total funding£947,000(ii) My Department has supported the Irish Medium Early Years sector by funding Altram to develop and pilot an Irish-Medium Pre-school Immersion Education course for Northern Ireland and to provide the Irish-Medium Pre-school Support Service.  A number of Irish medium pre-school settings also receive funding via the Getting Ready to Learn programme and the Extended Services Funding programme, both of which are funded by my Department. (iii) The budget outcome for Education falls far short of what the overall Education sector requires.  Given the size of the funding shortfall faced, I am working with my Department’s officials to determine the options available and what difficult decisions are required to offer the education sector the best chance to operate within budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps he is taking to support Health and Social Care Trusts in the recruitment and retention of Medical Technical Officers.
answer_text: Medical Technical Officers are employed by HSC Trusts across a very wide range of specialist areas.  While their recruitment and retention is primarily a matter for HSC Trusts as direct employers, my Department’s Health and Social Care Workforce Strategy 2026: Delivering for Our People, provides the strategic context within which the workforce across the health and social care system is being developed.  The Strategy’s extant Action Plan is a comprehensive and ambitious work programme that includes the development of initiatives to enhance attraction, recruitment and retention across all staff groups.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the action is he undertaking, with the Minister for Communities, on the creation of household energy efficiency schemes; (ii) the dates on which he has undertaken engagement with the Minister for Communities on this issue; and (iii) the outcome of those engagements.
answer_text: The Department for Communities (DfC) is leading on the Residential Buildings Sector of the Climate Action Plan, part of this work considers how future Energy Efficiency Schemes can employ affordable options to decarbonise, including improved insulation and low carbon heating solutions. This has involved my officials working closely with DfC officials on developing an energy efficiency scheme, low carbon heat scheme, the replacement of the current Affordable Warmth Scheme and a new Fuel Poverty Strategy. The aim of the new DfC fuel poverty energy efficiency scheme is to assist those low-income owner-occupiers and households in the private rented sector, with the costs of energy efficiency measures. The new scheme will align with the Energy and Green Growth Strategies, Climate Change Act and support the principles of the new Fuel Poverty Strategy. A Residential Decarbonisation Coordination Group, jointly chaired by Department for Communities and Department for Economy officials, has been established to ensure effective cross Government collaboration on a long-term policy approach for residential decarbonisation across the North of Ireland in line with the challenging targets contained within the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022. The group will monitor and report progress on an on-going basis.My department is also currently preparing a consultation on evidence-based options for a domestic energy efficiency programme in line with Action 7 of the Energy Strategy Action Plan for 2024. The outcome of this consultation will help to inform options regarding the design, rules, and guidance of future support.  In the interim, the department is working with partners such as the Utility Regulator to assess the options for further scaling of up existing energy efficiency programmes.  I have recently responded to a request from the Minister for Communities welcoming a meeting on areas relating to housing that lie with my department including heat decarbonisation and skills.  Energy Strategy - Path to Net Zero Energy - 2024 Action Plan Report (economy-ni.gov.uk)

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance (i) for an update on her plans to incorporate gender budgeting processes in her Department; and (ii) to detail any steps that were taken to incorporate gender budgeting in the development and agreement of Budget 2024-25.
answer_text: My department is committed to ensuring that it fulfils its Section 75 duties in relation to the Budget, including duties in relation to gender. To inform the Executive’s consideration of Budget 2024-25 proposals to the Executive, it was provided with departments’ assessment of potential equality impacts of each bid and of living within their existing baselines. Now that the Budget has been agreed, it is for individual departments to carry out equality impact screening and assessments in line with their Equality Schemes. Once completed I will bring this information to the Executive as part of the in year monitoring process. This will allow the Executive to consider if any adjustment should to made to departmental budgets as a result.  The 2024-25 Budget was set under a very restricted and pressurised time period. In future years it would be my intention, where possible, to issue a draft Budget, followed by a period of consultation, before a final Budget is agreed. I met with the both the NI Women’s Budget Group and the Women’s Policy Group NI in May 2024 to discuss gender budgeting, among other things. I have asked my officials to further engage with these, and other, relevant groups to discuss what more can be done to further best practice in relation to equality impacts and gender budgeting, within the parameters of the public expenditure regime and the wider Budget process.The development of the Executive’s Social Inclusion Strategies, including the Gender Equality Strategy, will be an important step towards considering gender impacts. This work on this is being led by the Department for Communities and I understand this may include consideration of gender budgeting and gender budgeting pilots.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how he intends to increase (i) the number of Higher Level Apprenticeships; and (ii) the proportion of those apprenticeships in places of higher deprivation.
answer_text: Higher Level Apprenticeships (HLAs) are demand led, with employers creating opportunities in line with their business needs.  Delivery of HLAs began in September 2017 and the numbers using this alternative route to access third level education have grown significantly since then, exceeding original forecasts.My officials are currently assessing a large number of proposals for brand new HLA qualifications for commencement in September.  Once approved, these will enable more employers, across more sectors, to utilise apprenticeships to attract new talent to their businesses, as well as offer existing employees the means to gain new skills where they are moving to a new job role.My Department leads Northern Ireland Apprenticeship Week, which in 2025 will run from 3 to 7 February. This week of events and activities across the North showcases how HLAs can transform the future of apprentices and businesses, helping them release their potential.I am committed to the promotion of regional balance, and believe that everyone, no matter where they live, should have the same opportunity to earn as they learn.  Higher Level Apprenticeships are delivered by all six Further Education Colleges, CAFRE and the three local universities, enabling employers and their apprentices to access the programme, no matter where in the North they are located.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the implementation of the recommendations from the Independent Review of Children’s Social Care Services in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The report of the Independent Review of Children’s Social Care Services was published in June 2023 and a public consultation on the Review recommendations ran for 12 weeks between September and December 2023. I am currently considering advice developed by my officials based on analysis of the responses to the public consultation. Subject to the final outcome to consultation and other factors, including the availability of budget and the views of other Ministers where relevant, the intention would be to implement recommendations as quickly as possible. A number of the recommendations will be taken forward through the Children's Social Care Services Reform Programme as appropriate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of clinical psychologists employed by each Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: At 31 March 2024, there were 472 (422.3 WTE) people graded as clinical psychologists and employed by the Health and Social Care (HSC) Trusts. question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the representations he has made to his Executive colleagues in relation to the Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan; and (ii) the outcome of those representations.
answer_text: The Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan is the first stage of tackling the persistent economic issues which fall under the control of my department. There is a need for greater cross-government action, and I will be asking Executive colleagues to task their officials to work with the Department to support its delivery.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) to detail the number of his departmental officials that are involved in the drafting of the Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan; (ii) whether the objectives and actions contained in this plan will be limited to those taken forward by Invest NI; and (iii) how he will ensure the objectives and actions of the action plan will inform the work of other Departments.
answer_text: The Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan will set out a series of actions by DfE and its delivery partners. Officials across the Department and its Arm Length Bodies have been involved. This is just the first stage – there will be a need for greater cross-government action to tackle persistent economic issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) any occasions, since the restoration of the Executive, that he has written to the Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities, or any Minister in that Department, to seek a full devolution of the levelling up agenda; and (ii) the outcome of those engagements.
answer_text: The Finance Minister leads engagement on behalf of the Executive with the British Government on Levelling Up policy and delivery. I will continue to support her efforts in that regard.Minister Archibald wrote to SoS Michael Gove (DLUHC) on this subject in May 2024; I understand that no response has yet been received.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to detail his Department’s plans for the use of social prescribing across Health and Social Care Trusts; (ii) for his assessment of the value of social prescribing link workers; and (iii) what steps he will take to increase the number of social prescribing link workers.
answer_text: My Department has promoted the use of social prescribing in various initiatives over a number of years.  In 2019 the SPRING Social Prescribing (SPRING) project was announced as a way to engage community-centred alternative interventions which can be more effective than the traditional treatments to improve the health and well-being of marginalised groups and vulnerable individuals. Instead of prescribing medicine and referring to hospitals, people can access a range of activities including arts, group learning, counselling sessions, cookery, befriending schemes, healthy lifestyle support, sports and yoga.The Public Health Agency currently has work underway to develop digital tools and support for mental health promotion and early intervention which will deliver on Actions 1 and 2 of the Mental Health Strategy.  As the first step PHA has appointed a research and advisory company to take forward a ‘Digital Discovery’ exercise to review global best practice, review the landscape in NI and hear from partners and stakeholders and develop a report with actionable insights. The report is due in the early summer 2024.  This initiative will help to raise awareness of and promote the benefits of social prescribing initiatives.My assessment is that social prescribing link workers are valuable as resource finders in local communities. They have local knowledge of the location, purpose and capacity of the service provision in their area; they have direct contact with patients and can support them in creating personalised solutions; they can obtain first-hand feedback on the effectiveness of the services received and can scale and schedule the referrals to providers accordingly.Financial constraints mean that the Department of Health cannot support any new initiatives in the current year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) to list the interventions his Department has taken in response to the 2021 study on Artificial Intelligence, commissioned by the Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, which found that all government districts, other than Belfast, Lisburn and Castlereagh, Derry and Strabane and Causeway Coast and Glens, were predicted to experience net job losses as a result of Artificial Intelligence; and (ii) to detail any measures that have been taken in response to the estimation that Armagh, Banbridge and Craigavon will experience the highest numbe
answer_text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is one of several innovative technologies that has widespread application across the economy, some of which we do not yet fully understand. In both its development and its industrial application, it will create opportunities, benefits, risks and challenges to be managed across the board. As with other technological developments in recent decades, I expect the adoption of AI will be productivity-enhancing overall but will also create some disruption across labour markets as the nature of jobs change, and the workforce becomes more productive – and is more concentrated in higher productivity activities. A key role for my department is to encourage the adoption of productivity-enhancing technologies such as AI, data analytics, innovative software and robotics across the economy in a way that creates opportunity for all. That is why, as we build innovative and AI-focussed industrial capability through our City and Growth Deals investments, and the recently launched AI Collaboration Centre, we are also supporting individuals to position themselves with the mix of skills and qualifications – academic, vocational and technical – to benefit from the technologies that are shaping economic opportunity in the coming decade and beyond. The development of a sub-regional economic action plan seeks to provide strategic coherence and a robust framework to place the north in the best position to adopt the benefits, and effectively manage the disruptions associated with AI and other technologies.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for his assessment of the return on investment on arts and culture interventions; and (ii) what direction he has provided to the Arts Council NI in relation to regional balance since taking office.
answer_text: Financial investment through arts and culture interventions is intended to encourage a range of social and economic returns including in terms of the sustainability of the sectors and the improvement of the quality of life of people here.  A number of tailored interventions are delivered by my department, directly or through its Arm’s Length Bodies, and a great many more are offered by a wide range of other statutory and non-statutory bodies and individuals.  Each intervention will include an evaluation mechanism and a range of data is also available, including through sector-commissioned research and from the Northern Ireland Statistics and Research Agency.  Assessment of the value, in economic and other terms, of investment will be a consideration in work underway to develop a new strategic approach to culture, arts and heritage.  The Arts Council of Northern Ireland is an arm’s length body of my department and is the primary delivery body for support to the arts. It operates a range of revenue and capital support programmes which are offered on a region-wide basis.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the organisations that have received financial support from the Arts Council in each of the last ten years, broken down by (i) the amount of funding awarded to each organisation; and (ii) the local council area the organisations are based.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Arts Council of Northern Ireland.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive.  Due to the volume of information required to provide an answer, I have arranged for this to be placed in the Assembly Library.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Arts Council directly. Making direct contact with the Arts Council will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) how he will ensure the new Culture, Arts and Heritage Strategy considers the value of funding specific arts and culture interventions in areas of highest deprivation; and (ii) what measures will be used to assess the areas where funding should be directed.
answer_text: Equality of access and opportunity, including for people and communities in areas of highest deprivation, are principles at the heart of work underway to develop a new strategic approach to culture, arts and heritage.  Potential interventions, including assessment measures, will be considered in that context.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 10017/22-27, to detail the dates, since the restoration of the Assembly, on which he has held formal meetings with the (i) Minister of Health; (ii) Minister of Justice; (iii) Minister of Education; and (iv) Minister for Communities in relation to regional balance, where this was the initially intended purpose of the engagement.
answer_text: The Regional Balance objective of my Economic Vision is currently being pursued at official-to-official level. As such, I have not yet conducted meetings with other Ministers specifically on the topic of regional balance. Further to my response to AQW 10017/22-27, regional balance remains a core priority and a frequent topic of all my engagements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the number of the 35 strategic actions, formulated in response to the Independent Review of Invest NI, that have been met; and (ii) the interventions that have been completed.
answer_text: The Invest NI Review Action Plan sets out the pathway for the transformation of the agency. To date, eight of the 35 Strategic Actions within the plan have been delivered. The completed interventions are: 	Strategic Action 1: Appointment of Kieran Donoghue as permanent CEO	Strategic Action 3: Board Effectiveness Review (annual)	Strategic Action 4: New Partnership Agreement	Strategic Action 5: Appointment of John Healy OBE as Board Chair	Strategic Action 10: Rationalisation of programmes and interventions	Strategic Action 22: New process to secure additional funding	Strategic Action 23: Additional business support to secure funding	Strategic Action 33: New Communications and Engagement Strategy

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for a breakdown of the purpose of the (a) capital; and (b) resource funding allocated by his Department for the expansion of the Ulster University Magee Campus in 2024-25; and (ii) to detail the quantum of funding that exists for the implementation of any recommendations from the Ulster University Magee Taskforce.
answer_text: The draft 2024-25 budget for my Department includes an initial £4.1m resource and £14.7m of capital allocations for Magee expansion activities. My officials are currently engaging with UU to develop a detailed plan on the allocation of that funding. The funding will be made available in financial year 2024/25 to take forward opportunities to expand the Magee campus by developing the physical environment, ensuring full utilisation of that environment, increasing student numbers and supporting the work of the Taskforce.  If there is scope for additional spending it will be prioritised by my Department.The Taskforce will publish its action plan before the end of this year and the funding required to implement the recommendations will be prioritised as part of future year budgets.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the steps he is taking to (i) ensure children and young people can access Palforzia for severe nut allergies; and (ii) support young people and their families who have been diagnosed with a severe nut allergy.
answer_text: Officers within the Department and its SPPG remain committed to the introduction of a local service to provide Palforzia to patients within Northern Ireland.  Work is ongoing to agree the most appropriate service delivery model for Northern Ireland.   The Child Health Programme Board which provides strategic direction regarding child health issues across NI is meeting in June   to consider a number of key issues including the Palforzia service model.  It is intended that the meeting will prioritise the establishment of a working group to develop and recommend the NI service model with its report to be available by late summer 2024. In the interim the regime is available on case by case basis for eligible patients in line with the Department’s endorsed guidance.Paediatricians and specialist allergy nurses across all Trusts work closely together to ensure that children and young people get the best care possible for treatment for their allergy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail (i) her engagements with (a) the Lady Chief Justice; and (b) the Commissioner for Victims of Crime in relation to the issue of family courts since the restoration of the Assembly in February 2024; and (ii) the outcome of those engagements.
answer_text: I met with the Lady Chief Justice on 14 February. Matters relating specifically to the family courts did not arise during our discussions. I am, however, aware that, like me, the Lady Chief Justice is keen that consideration is given to how the openness of family courts could be increased, whilst continuing to protect the privacy of the families and children involved. My Department has recently commenced policy scoping work in relation to this issue. I met with the Commissioner Designate for Victims of Crime on 13 February. Ms Hanna informed me that her office is planning research relating to children's experiences of the family courts. I will consider the findings of that research when it is completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of (i) the progress made in tackling child sexual exploitation since the publication of the 2020 report from the Criminal Justice Inspection Northern Ireland on Child Sexual Exploitation in Northern Ireland; and (ii) whether  appropriate data collation and monitoring is in place to assess progress.
answer_text: Please refer to the response for AQW 6315/22-27 for an update on the progress made in tackling child sexual exploitation since the 2020 inspection report.  Progress on the 2020 recommendations is monitored by the cross-Departmental Child Protection Senior Officials Group (CPSOG). The CPSOG is chaired by Department of Health and other core Departments include Justice, Education, Finance and Communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his Department’s engagement with the UK government on the UK Shared Prosperity Fund since taking office, including on how many occasions his officials have met with their counterparts in the UK government on this scheme.
answer_text: The Finance Minister leads in negotiations on replacement EU funding with the British Government and I will continue to support her efforts in that regard.Further to the Finance Minister’s update on discussions with HM Treasury on the status of funding from the SPF beyond March 2025 (Official Report for AQO 236/22-27), Minister Archibald wrote to SoS Michael Gove (DLUHC) pressing for urgent engagement on post-2025 funding, stressing that the timeline for the continuation or replacement of the SPF be prioritised in order to prevent another funding cliff-edge for the Voluntary and Community Sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the data collection exercise on the evaluation of 2024/25 early learning and childcare measures will include an analysis of regional disparities in childcare provision.
answer_text: My Written Ministerial Statement on 23 May 2024 set out my plans for 2024/25 support measures.  Work to confirm the operational detail, including in relation to the data collection exercise, is ongoing and will be communicated in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) how the targeted business support scheme for childcare providers will be regulated; and (ii) whether it will contain provisions to raise pay and qualifications for workers, along with improving the sustainability of providers.
answer_text: My Written Ministerial Statement on 23 May 2024 set out my plans for 2024/25 support measures.  Work to confirm the operational detail regarding the targeted business support scheme is ongoing and will be communicated in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the steps he will take to ensure that women in Northern Ireland will be included as part of a redress scheme for those harmed by pelvic mesh and the medicine valproate; and (ii) the engagement he has undertaken with the UK government on this issue.
answer_text: The Hughes Report by the Patient Safety Commissioner in England, published on 7 February 2024, made recommendations for the government in England to consider. There have been no final decisions taken by the government in England in relation to redress, and no set timeframe for such decisions. While the Hughes Report is specific to England, I recognise that women across the UK including in Northern Ireland have been affected by these issues. My officials continue to engage in discussions with counterparts in England, and in Scotland and Wales, to understand the emerging considerations there. Options regarding next steps for Northern Ireland will also take account of these discussions and of related DHSC timeframes which remain under consideration.On 7 May 2024 my predecessor Robin Swann wrote to Lord Markum, Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department of Health and Social Care, to seek his assurance that the UK Government will consult with the Devolved Administrations in an open and timely manner regarding the detail of the proposals under development. Work in this area continues to be an important priority for me and for my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline his plans to meet the growing demand for integrated education places.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how the Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan will be aligned with Place10X.
answer_text: Place10X has been integrated into my Regional Balance priority. The evidence gathered, stakeholder views received, and analysis published in December 2023 are informing the development of the Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to his statement on Early Leaning and Childcare Measures 2024/25, what steps will be taken to assist parents who are not in employment with the costs of early education.
answer_text: The Pre-School Education Programme (PSEP) is a universal pre-school education programme and is provided free at the point of delivery for every child in their target pre-school year whose parents want it.  My statement of 23 May included a commitment to standardise all PSEP places to 22.5 hours per week over time.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the establishment of a Northern Ireland Climate Commissioner.
answer_text: Development of the Regulations required under Section 50 of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 is progressing well. These Regulations will be a first step in establishing the NI Climate Commissioner.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) their plans for the development of a Bill of Rights in this Assembly mandate; and (ii) any engagement their office has had with the Irish government in relation to the development of a Bill of Rights.
answer_text: There is no agreed position.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the immediate support measures he intends to put in place to support registered childminders, pending the publication of the draft Early Education and Childcare Strategy.
answer_text: My Written Ministerial Statement on 23 May 2024 sets out my plans for 2024/25 support measures.  Implementing these will be informed by further stakeholder engagement, including with those representing childminders.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will instruct her officials to submit any papers to the Department of Education’s Task and Finish group on Early Education and Childcare.
answer_text: The Group is led by DE and to date my Department has not been commissioned to provide any papers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what considerations are being given for the continued safeguarding of children and families when they are moved to dispersal accommodation.
answer_text: Regionally agreed safeguarding processes, policies and procedures exist, which are intended to provide the greatest level of protection to all of the population of Northern Ireland, regardless of their immigration status or where they live. If a family or child, who are known to social services, move between Health and Social Care Trust areas, the established process ensures the receiving Trust is informed of the movement of the family or child into their area. An Information Sharing Agreement is in place between MEARS (the accommodation provider for the Home Office) and all 5 HSC Trusts. Trusts are informed on a weekly basis of all movements into and out of their respective areas from MEARS-provided accommodation.  The Department of Health is continuing to work in partnership with the Home Office and MEARS to ensure that the safeguarding practice of MEARS and any underpinning policies and procedures are robust and in keeping with expected standards of safeguarding practice, policy and procedure in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice when she will answer AQW 7146/22-27.
answer_text: I understand that as a result of budget difficulties and the need to implement saving measures the Department of Health advised Women’s Aid that it was not possible to provide core grant funding for the full 12-month period in 2023/24.  My Department fully recognises the importance of the meaningful work carried out by Women’s Aid Federation Northern Ireland.  However, in light of budget constraints affecting the Department of Justice and in order to be able to deliver my Department’s statutory functions, I am not in a position to allocate any funding to cover the shortfall in the funding previously provided by the Department of Health.At the time of writing the 2024-25 budget has yet to be agreed and set by the Executive.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 10770/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 10770/22-27 was answered on 21 May 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) whether he will provide funding to the Men’s Action Network; (ii) whether he will meet with Men’s Action Network to discuss their work; and (iii) what actions he has taken in relation to men’s health since the restoration of the Assembly in February 2024.
answer_text: The 2024/25 Health Budget has resulted in all sectors within health and social care experiencing severe financial pressures and I appreciate this has been particularly challenging for those such as Men’s Action Network (MAN) operating in the VCS sector. I regret that the ongoing financial challenges facing my Department mean that I cannot commit to any additional funding opportunities for the Network at this time.PHA have just made a small grants fund available and this may provide an opportunity for MAN to apply for emotional health and wellbeing related funding.The Public Health Agency advertise all funding opportunities on its website and through eTendersNI (which is an online portal used by all Government Departments, agencies and arms-length bodies). PHA would advise organisations wanting to avail of future funding opportunities to register on this platform.Regarding meeting MAN, I am giving active consideration to how best to engage with the many VCS organisations that add such great value to Health and Social Care in Northern Ireland.Development of a new Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy is in its final stages and it is hoped that it will be published in June 2024.The new strategy provides a high-level overarching framework based around five pillars - Partnership; Prevention; Children and Young People; Support and Provision; and Justice – and it will be implemented by way of annual action plans.The strategy recognises that anyone can be a victim of domestic and/or sexual abuse regardless of age, ethnicity, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation or any form of disability. As such it adopts an inclusive approach.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister (i) for an update on the implementation of the recommendations from the report entitled, An inspection of contingency asylum accommodation for families with children in Northern Ireland; (ii) to detail any engagements they have had with the Home Office in relation to this report; (iii) what information in relation to this implementation has been shared with other agencies; and (iv) who is monitoring this implementation.
answer_text: The recommendations from the ICIBI Report are for Home Office (HO) to deliver.  HO responded to the Report following its publication on 29 February 2024, accepting 2 recommendations fully and partially accepting the remaining 3. We recognise that some of the issues raised in the Report had been progressed prior to the publication and the number of families with children living in contingency accommodation has significantly reduced.  Our officials continue to liaise directly with HO, and via the Strategic Planning Group (Refugees and Asylum), on implementation of the Report’s recommendations, and have put in place regular check-in meetings to be assured on progress and enable local representation of issues. Through discussion to date, HO have advised that there is a multifaceted programme in place with partners for delivery of this work and it is understood the aim is to complete implementation within 12 months from publication of the Report. Safeguarding is now a standing agenda item at the Tactical Delivery Group led by TEO. An ongoing action of this group relates to engagement with HO on the implementation of the report recommendations and updates are shared with members accordingly.Given its remit for safeguarding, the Department of Health is continuing to work in partnership with the Home Office and their provider Mears to ensure that the relevant safeguarding policies and procedures are robust and in keeping with expected standards of safeguarding practice, policy and procedure here.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current number of consultants, including in cardiology and emergency medicine, broken down by (i) Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) area of specialism.
answer_text: (i) The whole-time equivalent number of consultants employed by Health & Social Care organisations at 31 March 2024 is provided in the table below.HSC TrustWhole-time equivalent ConsultantsBelfast858.9Northern263.3South Eastern319.7Southern280.7Western246.9Other HSC organisation43.4Total2,012.8(ii) The number of consultants broken down by all areas of specialism is not currently published.  My Department has been working on a project to collate, organise and present this information, but there is no indication yet of when any findings will be publicly available.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will work with the Regulation and Quality Improvement Authority in relation to legislative or policy changes required to ensure that misleading information is not provided through pregnancy care centres.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current number of junior doctors, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The term junior doctor usually refers to post graduate doctors who have entered the workforce to continue postgraduate clinical training, including foundation training, core training and specialty training.   In Northern Ireland, these doctors are employed by the Northern Ireland Medical & Dental Training Agency (NIMDTA) under the single lead employer initiative.  At 31 March 2024, there were 1,705.3 whole-time equivalent (WTE) NIMDTA-employed junior doctors assigned to a regional HSC Trust, with a further 10.0 WTE doctors assigned to another organisation and 223.9 WTE GP trainees on hospital placements. A breakdown of the 1,705.3 WTE junior doctors assigned to an HSC Trust is provided in the table below.NIMDTA-Employed Junior Doctors by Assigned Regional HSC Trust (WTE), at 31 March 2024Assigned HSC TrustWTEBelfast739.1Northern238.7South Eastern256.8Southern247.1Western223.6Total1,705.3Source: Information & Analysis Directorate, DoH.This information is published by my Department on a quarterly basis in the Northern Ireland HSC Quarterly Workforce Statistics bulletin, which is available at the link below:https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/topics/dhssps-statistics-and-research/workforce-statistics

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the number of occasions her Department has submitted papers to the Task and Finish Group on Early Education and Childcare; and (ii) the topics of those papers.
answer_text: The Group is led by DE and to date my Department has not been commissioned to provide any papers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what representations he has made to Executive colleagues to replace the funding lost by Women’s Aid NI through Core Grant funding allocations in 2023-24.
answer_text: In the absence of additional funding, I will be unable to support important initiatives such as the Core Grant Scheme. I have made my position clear to Executive colleagues.In relation to the loss of Core Grant funding experienced by the Women’s Aid Federation specifically, I have provided the Justice Minister with information about the Core Grant Scheme reduction, and discussions have taken place at official level between my Department and the Executive Office.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 6315/22-27, (i) how her Department has taken forward a cross-departmental strategic approach to tackle child sexual abuse and exploitation, which is linked to any existing and future strategic plans for child protection; and (ii) for her assessment of whether the child sexual exploitation problem profile is informed by robust data collection, including detailing where any gaps in data exist.
answer_text: Further to AQW 6315/22-27, I have responded to your two queries below:My Department is taking forward a cross-departmental strategic approach to tackle child sexual abuse and exploitation, linked to existing and future strategic plans for child protection, through the Child Protection Senior Officials Group (CPSOG).  The terms of reference for CPSOG were refreshed to better position it to provide the cross departmental strategic approach in line with the recommendation of the 2020 CJINI inspection on Child Sexual Exploitation. As a cross-departmental strategic forum, CPSOG provides direction on existing or emerging child protection issues that require cross-departmental consideration and co-ordination. The CJINI recommendation to develop a child sexual exploitation problem profile was led by police with input from partners. The PSNI is operationally independent from me as Justice Minister and is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  I am committed to upholding the independence of those structures and would suggest that this aspect of your question is addressed to the Chief Constable.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when his Department will clean the three tonnes maximum gross weight road signs at the Irish Street and Altnagelvin roundabout, Derry.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.  As a result, my Department has had to introduce a limited service maintenance policy meaning that only the highest priority road defects are repaired. For safety reasons, the maintenance of traffic signs is prioritised to concentrate on regulatory signs and markings, and junctions. Maintenance, including cleaning, of other signs/bollards and road markings, is only carried out if budget is available or where a specific safety issue has been identified. These signs will be cleaned as soon as resources permit.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what steps her Department has taken in relation to the future of the BT site in Enniskillen since 9 April 2024.
answer_text: My Department and officials from Invest NI have undertaken a comprehensive programme of work and engagement with senior management in BT and EE based in both the North of Ireland and Britain. My department and officials have also engaged with a wide range of stakeholders on this matter including the MP for the Fermanagh and South Tyrone constituency, Members of the NI Assembly, Members and officials from Fermanagh and Omagh District Council, the Communication Workers Union (CWU) and ICTU trade union representatives and concerned members of the public.In addition to a series of high-level meetings taking place with BT senior management, on 16 April 2024 Minister Murphy wrote to BT Group to reiterate his support for the Enniskillen site and its workforce.On 17 April 2024 Invest CEO and executives met in person with BT and EE key decision makers to discuss the progress of the fact-finding review by BT and to reiterate and explore areas where Invest NI might support the company to retain a presence in the Enniskillen area. Invest NI also outlined the results of its search for alternative property in Enniskillen should alternative premises be needed.On 18 April 2024 Minister Murphy, officials from DfE and Invest NI again met with senior management from BT and EE. Minister Murphy again reiterated the importance of the BT jobs in Enniskillen to the local economy, his priority to seek to ensure regional balance in the economy and to impress upon BT the need to recognise the long-term service, loyalty, commitment and value of the local workforce. On 14 May 2024 BT EE announced its decision that the EE Customer Care centre would close on 31 October 2024 following a further period of statutory consultation with staff and their representatives. Invest NI was in contact with company management on that day.I am also aware of ongoing dialogue between Invest NI and Fermanagh and Omagh District Council to help secure alternative employment for those workers impacted by this announcement and to seek to secure new investment into the Fermanagh area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when gullies at Cuthbert Street, Derry, were last successfully cleansed in their entirety.
answer_text: Cuthbert Street is a narrow cul-de-sac in the Waterside area of the city and there are five gullies located along the street. The last time the Department’s contractor was able to gain access and clean these gullies was 25 November 2021. During subsequent visits in April 2022, Oct 2022 and July 2023 the contractor was unable to gain access due to parked cars. Unfortunately, this is a common problem at many locations throughout the city no matter what time the contractor visits sites.Giving the on-going difficulties with access, my officials will liaise with the local community to see if an arrangement could be put in place with residents to move their cars or have spaces coned off to allow the contractor to carry out the necessary works.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of patients being charged for access to their medical records.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 9366/22-27, when the follow up attempt to cleanse gullies at Cuthbert Street, Derry, has been scheduled for, following the unsuccessful attempt on 5 March 2024.
answer_text: Giving the on-going difficulties with access at Cuthbert Street, my officials will liaise with the local community to see if an arrangement could be put in place with residents to move their cars or have spaces coned off to allow the contractor to carry out the necessary works.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the steps taken by her Department in relation to the Independent Review of Invest NI since the return of the Executive; (ii) the number of Invest NII staff working on green technology; and (iii) any proposed increase to this team in relation to (a) the number of staff; and (b) the associated budget.
answer_text: Steps taken by the Department in relation to the Independent Review of Invest NI.Implementation of the 35 strategic actions formulated in response to the Independent Review of Invest NI remains a key priority for my Department and the agency.Progress on delivery is being overseen by a jointly constituted Implementation Oversight Group. My officials are also working through a series of deep dives to assess progress against the more complex elements of the Plan, providing support as required. Minister Murphy has already met Chief Executive Officer, Kieran Donoghue and Chair of the Invest NI Board, John Healy OBE to seek their commitment to the profound change called for by the Lyon’s Review.  I am continuing this engagement, and a further progress meeting is scheduled for the coming week.  The number of Invest NI staff working on green technology, and (iii) any proposed increase to this team in relation to (a) the number of staff, and (b) the associated budget.I am committed to supporting businesses to transition to a greener and more sustainable economy through decarbonisation. This commitment will be reflected in Invest NI’s new three-year Business Strategy.Strategic Action 29 in the Invest NI Review Action Plan also commits Invest NI to establishing a dedicated Green Economy Division that will provide targeted investment to promote green economic growth across the North.  On 31 March 2024, Invest NI had 24.1 Full Time Equivalent (FTE) staff dedicated to the green economy. However, as my priorities are operationalised within the agency, the number of staff directly working in this area, and the associated budget, will increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for an update on the development of the Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan; (ii) whether this plan will be aligned to existing district council Local Development Plans; (iii) how her Department’s Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan will interact and align with Invest NI’s sub-regional economic plans; and (iv) in what areas and council districts Invest NI’s sub-regional economic plans will be aligned to existing district council Local Development Plans.
answer_text: The Sub-Regional Economic Action Plan is on target to be published in September 2024, with DfE and Invest NI officials currently co-designing interventions with councils and local partners. This singular plan will set an overarching framework that connects local economic priorities with DfE and partner ALBs’ policies and programmes to tackle disparities, exploit opportunities, and deliver greater regional balance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on whether his Department plans to extend the footway and cycleway from Drumahoe towards Altnagelvin on the right-hand side of the road.
answer_text: My Department is currently developing an Active Travel Delivery Plan that includes proposed active travel network plans for our largest towns and cities, including Derry and Strabane.I can confirm that the proposal to extend a path to Altnagelvin will be given consideration in the preparation of this plan, following the construction of the well used path between Drumahoe and the park in 2023.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of inward investment visits that have taken place in each month of the last five years, broken down by (i) constituency; and (ii) local council area.
answer_text: Tables 1-5 show the number of visits hosted by Invest NI by potential foreign direct investors which took place in each Parliamentary Constituency Area in each of the years between 2019-2020 and 2023-2024.Tables 6-10 show the number of visits hosted by Invest NI by potential foreign direct investors which took place in each District Council Area in each of the years between 2019-2020 and 2023-2024.Table 1 -
