question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of patients who have received a uMRD test following Venetoclax treatment, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust, for each of the last fifteen years.
To ask the Minister of Justice whether she will commission a full review of the family court system.
answer_text: Ensuring the court system is fair, accessible and responsive and the right supports are in place was a key focus of the Modernisation Programme I launched in 2021 and I remain committed to improving the experience of parties to family proceedings, particularly those who are more vulnerable. 		My Department is considering potential next steps in the Modernisation Programme. Decisions will be informed by a number of ongoing workstreams including:	research soon to be commissioned by Geraldine Hanna, Victim of Crime Commissioner Designate, on experiences of children whose families have been involved in family proceedings	a Foundational Review of Civil Legal Aid initiated by my Department to examine the scope of current legal support, how citizens benefit from the resource and opportunities to better ensure access to justice	a forthcoming consultation on independent research on how to ensure victims of domestic abuse do not experience further abuse as a consequence of family proceedings		I look forward to the outcome of these workstreams and will consider further options at that point.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) whether it is still the policy of his Department to undertake a full review of the higher education funding system, including the support provided to students such as (a) maintenance loans; (b) grants; (c) other student support products; and (d) the support provided to the sector, such as the level and mix of teaching grant and fees; and (ii) what deadline he has given his departmental officials to update him in relation to this work.
answer_text: I am committed to working with the higher education sector to embed sustainable funding arrangements that enable the sector to thrive and to create more opportunities for our students.My assessment is that students already take on a significant level of debt in order to access higher education and that adding to this debt is not the right approach. 	I have asked my officials to update me on any work to date on a review of Higher Education Funding, as well as proposals to take it forward. I will be engaging with my officials to consider the current support available and determine whether this can be improved. No deadlines have been set at this point.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the implementation of the Domestic Abuse (Safe Leave) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, including any plans in place to roll-out (i) training; (ii) policy development; and (iii) pathways to support organisations in relation to the Act.
answer_text: The Domestic Abuse (Safe Leave) Act (NI) 2022 requires my Department to make regulations providing at least 10 days paid leave in each leave year for workers that are victims of domestic abuse. Work to operationalise this Act is ongoing and I will consult on proposals before the summer.There is a statutory requirement for my Department to issue guidance about the implementation or application of a provision of regulations made under the Act.  The content of guidance will be determined following the consultation exercise. This guidance should be a source of support to individual businesses and employers which may help them develop workplace policies which suit the needs of their businesses and workers. Workplace guidance on how to support employees affected by domestic violence and abuse is already available.  The Safe at home Safe at work.pdf (lra.org.uk) guide and the Developing a Workplace policy on Domestic and Sexual Abuse - a guide for employers produced by the Departments of Health and Justice are two examples of guidance for employers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) to detail the standards set by his Department for repair work carried out by utility companies, including NI Water; and (ii) whether NI Water is expected to reinstall original paving stones following any access or repair work.
answer_text: The Street Works (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (the Order) forms the legislative framework for street works by statutory undertakers such as NI Water. The Order requires the undertaker, when reinstating a street, to comply with such requirements as may be prescribed in relation to the specification of materials and the standards of workmanship to be observed. These requirements have been prescribed in the Specification for the Reinstatement of Openings in Roads (2nd Edition), a code of practice approved by the Department under Article 31 of the Order. Details of this can be found at:www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/drd/nirauc-code-of-practice-specification-for-the-reinstatement-of-openings-in-roads-2nd-edition.pdf Permanent reinstatements are normally carried out using the same materials and in some cases it may be possible to reuse some or all of the original materials. Interim reinstatements may be carried out using different materials prior to sourcing replacment materials and completing a permanent reinstatement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the housing challenges facing students in rented accommodation.
answer_text: I am acutely aware that cost-of-living increases have placed financial difficulties on students, including those who live in rented accommodation. I will be engaging with my officials to consider the current support available and determine whether this can be improved.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 8943/22-27, to detail the number of officials in his Department who are specifically tasked to lead work on regional economic balance, broken down by (i) group within the Department; and (ii) grade.
answer_text: All officials at all grades are expected to take regional balance into account in all aspects of their work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what engagement he has had with the Minister for the Economy on exploring pay parity between school staff and the further education sector.
answer_text: I refer to my previous response to AQW 8725/22-27, that the terms & conditions of employment for school staff and further education staff are not comparable. I have therefore had no engagement with the Minister for the Economy on exploring pay parity between school staff and the further education sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how he will ensure that seriously ill children and their families can access the required palliative care.
answer_text: The issue of access to paediatric palliative care for seriously ill children and young people in Northern Ireland is extremely important to me and to the work that is being done within my Department.The Department published the Paediatric Palliative and End of Life Care Strategy in 2016.  This strategy was completed after considerable consultation with all relevant stakeholders across the sector in NI and beyond to ensure it was as comprehensive as possible. The Strategy includes 23 ambitious objectives that cover the full range of issues within paediatric palliative care, from diagnosis of a palliative or life limited condition through to bereavement support for families of those children who have passed away.Subsequent to the publication of the strategy a number of specific innovations were introduced in NI.  This include the appointment of a paediatric palliative care lead in each Trust, the appointment of a Regional Paediatric Palliative Care consultant who works at the RBHSC but has input across the region, and the appointment of Paediatric Palliative and Life Limited Nurse who works for the NI Children’s Hospice but is based at the RBHSC. In 2019 a Regional Paediatric Palliative Care Network (RPPCN) was created and this is chaired by a Paediatric Palliative Clinical Care lead.  The network is made up of key clinical and nursing leads from each Trust as well as the NI Children’s Hospice to deliver on the strategy and to identify areas of best practice and share clinical opinion on specific cases to ensure continuous improvement. To date the network has developed ante-natal care pathways, rapid discharge plans, advanced care plans and has also agreed pathways regarding pain relief, amongst others.  Currently the network is also working on other key issues including the provision of 24/7 end of life care as well as examining how to best develop closer ties with colleagues in the Republic of Ireland. The network works closely together to ensure that irrespective of where a child lives in NI they can access high quality paediatric palliative care.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 7847/22-27, (i) for his assessment of whether the low uptake of modern languages is a barrier to matriculation in universities in the Republic of Ireland for pupils from Northern Ireland; and (ii) what plans he has to address this.
answer_text: As advised in my answer to AQW 7847-22-27, changes to entry requirements to Universities in the Republic of Ireland are already in place for the University of Galway and are currently going through approval process in other universities in the Republic of Ireland. These changes enable Northern Ireland students to use their best 3 A-levels, along with a 4th A-level, an Extended Project or an AS subject. This will mean that applicants can attain a score of 600 points with 3 A-levels and 1 AS, and 625 points if one of the A-levels is Maths. There is no requirement for one of those qualifications to be a Modern Language qualification.There have been numerous academic reports on the modern languages’ curriculum and qualifications in Northern Ireland over the last decade and more. These have highlighted issues in regard to modern languages learning and the uptake of languages as a qualification at key stage 4 and post-16.  This has resulted in a steady decline in the number of pupils studying modern languages in Northern Ireland. This decline is mirrored across the UK. Stakeholders are generally in agreement about the main challenges. The Education and Training Inspectorate published a ‘Modern Languages at Key Stage 3 Report’ in September 2023 to disseminate effective practice to ‘empower improvement’ in the teaching of modern languages and to stimulate further discussion and debate.The Department and the Council for Curriculum Examinations and Assessment are working closely to identify options and develop policy proposals to increase uptake of modern languages at both primary and post-primary level, as well as increase GCSE and A-level entries in modern languages. Officials have engaged on modern languages through the Four Nations Fora (England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland) to scope curriculum policy implications. Within this forum, the application of current policy interventions and other language initiatives are discussed along with collaboration opportunities and signposting to resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 8405/22-27, (i) how many of his departmental policies and strategies include addressing regional economic imbalances as an objective, other than the proposed Sub-regional Economic Plan; and (ii) whether he will list those policies and strategies, excluding the proposed Sub-regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: My departments policies and strategies are being aligned to reflect my four key priorities, which include regional balance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) whether he will ensure that graduate entry medical students can access student loans in the same way as students in Britain; and (ii) whether he will introduce a postgraduate maintenance loan.
answer_text: Whilst my Department has responsibility for the provision of Higher Education student support for students from here in the main, policy responsibility including student financial support entitlement for medical students rests with the Department of Health (DoH).  Any changes to the funding of Graduate Entry Medical students would therefore be a matter for DoH to determine, however, I will be engaging with the Minister for Health on this issue and my Department is ready to facilitate any change in policy as required.  I have also asked my officials to update me on any work to date on a review of Higher Education Funding, which includes student support packages. I will be engaging with my officials to consider the current support available and determine whether this can be improved while taking account of the financial context for my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for his Department’s assessment of the condition of (a) Strand Road; (b) Fannad Drive; (c) Park Avenue; (d) Rosemount Avenue; (e) Fountain Hill; and (f) Iniscarn Road, Derry; and (ii) whether they will be prioritised as part of future resurfacing schemes.
answer_text: Resurfacing schemes are funded through my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme; regrettably, for some time the available investment has not kept pace with what is needed to maintain our roads, including those referred to above, in a reasonable condition.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention. I am very aware of the long-term underfunding from which our road network has suffered.  The programme for 2024-25 will be developed and published once future budgets have been agreed. In the meantime, officials in my Department will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 8190/22-27, whether he will confirm that the additional funding announced by Derry City and Strabane Dictrict Council and its project partners to its City Deal will be enough to ensure that the Derry and Strabane City Deal is delivered in full, as originally envisaged.
answer_text: My Department continues to work with Derry City and Strabane District Council and its partners to drive delivery of the City Deal. Affordability of all the approved projects is constantly kept under review.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on (i) the findings of the Review of the Northern Ireland Postgraduate Tuition Fee Loan; and (ii) the implementation of those findings.
answer_text: Following the Review of the Northern Ireland Postgraduate Tuition Fee Loan, my Department undertook a public consultation on various options from 26 April 2021 to 25 June 2021. The consultation document, along with the Departmental response, can be found online at the following web address: https://www.economy-ni.gov.uk/consultations/review-northern-ireland-postgraduate-tuition-fee-loan.As stated in the response, the former Minister decided to proceed with Options 1 and 4 of the consultation. My Department therefore currently provides a loan of up to £6,500 to eligible students from here undertaking eligible postgraduate course including postgraduate certificates, postgraduate diplomas, taught Master’s or research Master’s at local Higher Education Institutions. From academic year 2024/25 this will be extended to postgraduate students from this jurisdiction studying in the south of Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will develop an urgent plan of interventions to ensure dental practices can continue to provide NHS care.
answer_text: On 27 March I announced a £9.2m investment package for General Dental Services to include:£1m for newly registered child patients through the reestablishment of the Enhanced Child Examination Scheme. This scheme provides children aged 0-10 who have not been registered with a dentist with an examination, individualised oral health advice and age-specific fluoride application to teeth to assist with preventing dental decay.£4.3m to fund a 30% enhancement to fees paid to dentists for health service fillings, extractions and root canal treatment for 2024/25. This will support public access to priority treatments.£3.9m for the treatment of high priority unregistered patients through a Dental Access Scheme;This investment package is in addition to the 6% pay uplift for dentists I have announced for 2023/24, in line with the recommendations from the DDRB (Doctors’ and Dentists’ Review Body). The uplift comes with a 7.3% uplift to non-staff expenses for 2023/24, with a 3% recurrent award in future years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) for his assessment of the results of the survey released by Melted Parents NI on 22 March 2024; (ii) whether he will provide any financial support to families to assist them with increased childcare costs; and (iii) whether he will meet with the group to discuss these findings.
answer_text: My officials, in conjunction with colleagues in other departments, are actively considering what measures would be possible and appropriate to ease the childcare pressures which parents are facing.  A thorough assessment of all relevant evidence is a core component of this work.  Over the next few weeks, I have meetings arranged with a wide range of stakeholders, including parents and my Assembly Private Secretary, Diane Forsythe, MLA met with representatives of Melted Parents NI on 26th March 2024.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they will allocate funding to Women’s Aid Northern Ireland which is commensurate to the funding lost by the organization from the Core Grant.
answer_text: The funding for Women’s Aid NI from Department of Health core grant is yet to be finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Housing Executive improvement scheme for the Triangle area, Clooney Terrace, in Foyle.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:The Housing Executive has encountered a number of difficulties when trying to deliver this project regarding a number of leaseholders resisting proportionate maintenance/ improvement costs and the buy-back options offered.  NIHE are currently revisiting the business case and re-assessing potential future options, to include redevelopment, for the Triangle area, Clooney Terrace in Foyle. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the affordability of the business case, received from College Chief Executive Officers and Governing Body Chairs, for a pay award to further education lecturers.
answer_text: As a pay settlement has not yet been reached between Further Education Colleges, as employers, and Trade Unions, my department has not yet received a business case. Affordability will be considered in line with the Department of Finance’s Public Sector Pay Policy Guidance 2023-24.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps he will take to reopen the midwifery-led unit in Altnagelvin Hospital.
answer_text: The Western Health and Social Care Trust has a rolling advert to recruit midwives.The unit will open as soon as safe midwifery staffing can be achieved.The Department of Health, Queens University and the Chief Nursing Officer have approved and supported an increase in the numbers of Student midwives undertaking a shortened course in the Western Trust.The Trust has facilitated numerous career events to share midwifery career pathway with students, facilitates work experience in maternity settings and assist students prepare for interview.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on when work will commence on the redevelopment of Meenan Square.

To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to his comments at the Committee for the Economy meeting on 20 March 2024, why it is his understanding that he is unable to legislate for other Departments, given that Clause 4 of the Parental Bereavement (Leave and Pay) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 confers duties on the Department for Communities and Clause 1 of the Rural Needs Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 confers duties on all Departments.
answer_text: Ministers cannot unilaterally legislate for other departments. The most expedient way to deliver regional balance is through an agreed Programme for Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will review his Department’s gully cleaning protocol to ensure that residents in areas with a high proportion of on-street parking are given suitable notice in advance of any gully cleaning.
answer_text: If vehicles are parked and gullies cannot be cleaned during a programmed cyclical clean, these are generally noted by the Department’s contractors and further attempts to clean them are made at least once soon after.  If further attempts to clean the gullies are still unsuccessful then, depending on the circumstances, plans may be made to enter the area at weekends or evenings, as resources permit. These operations are normally preceded with a letter drop to residents advising them of programmed weekend gully emptying. Alternatively, in the case of individual gullies, they may be left to the next cyclical clean unless staff are aware of a specific issue.My Department has been operating in a very difficult financial position with years of underfunding. That has had a significant impact on the ability to deliver our essential maintenance functions. Constrained budgets have also impacted on my Department’s ability to fill staff vacancies resulting in understaffing across many of its functions. For these reasons, I do not plan to change the current arrangements for gully cleaning at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of staff employed by his Department, broken down by the Assembly constituency where they are based.
answer_text: In order to answer the above question accurately, the Department requested the information from the Department of Finance.  The number of staff employed by the Department for the Economy, broken down by the Assembly constituency where they are based, is shown in the following table.Assembly Area 2008TotalBelfast East11Belfast South1,160East Antrim5East Londonderry4Fermanagh & South Tyrone10Foyle14Lagan Valley4Mid Ulster10Newry & Armagh18North Antrim7North Down8South Antrim4South Down4Strangford3Upper Bann11West Tyrone7Total1,280

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when a business case for the proposed Mother and Baby Unit at the Belfast City Hospital site will be finalised.
answer_text: A business case for the proposed Mother and Baby Unit at the Belfast City Hospital site is currently being prepared. The timescale for approval of this business case remains subject to the identification of capital and revenue funding to deliver the unit in the context of an overall challenging financial position.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether a Health and Social Care Trust can regrade a role to a lower banding and subsequently reduce the salary of an affected employee.
answer_text: HSC employees could for reasons related to organisational change, capability, disciplinary or ill health be regraded to a lower band.  If an employee is to be regraded, this will be managed in line with the appropriate HR policy and the employee, and their representative, involved in discussions.  In the case of organisational change, protection of salary will be applied in accordance with the appropriate Circular/local policy.  If a post is regraded following a job evaluation that has been initiated by the organisation protection of earnings will be applied for a period as specified in the relevant policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how he will ensure a regional balance in the distribution of funding to support arts and culture.
answer_text: The Arts Council of Northern Ireland is an arm’s length body of my department and is the primary delivery body for supporting arts and culture. The Arts Council operates a range of revenue or capital support programmes which are offered on a region-wide basis. 		The issue of geographical reach and balance across Northern Ireland, in terms of access and funding, was raised in the report of the Culture, Arts and Heritage Taskforce and will be an important aspect of the work being undertaken by my department to develop a new Culture, Arts and Heritage Strategy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 5604/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 5604/22-27 was answered on 4 April 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on funding for the City of Derry Airport.
answer_text: As Minister for Infrastructure, my powers relating to NI’s three main airports are limited to the Airports (NI) Order 1994.  These are powers to control noise, control land in the interests of the safe and efficient use of airports, make byelaws, provide for airport constabularies and airport consultative committees and to give grants to assist capital expenditure.In April 2021 Derry City and Strabane District Council submitted the original Business Case requesting support for City of Derry Airport.  My officials had requested updated information in the form of an addendum to the original Business Case which was received by my Department on 21 March 2024 and is currently being considered by officials. I remain committed to working with Executive colleagues to listen to the case brought forward by Derry City and Strabane District Council for support for City of Derry Airport, and to working together with the rest of the Executive to identify ways to support our airports.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has plans to work with the Northern Ireland Statistics and Research Agency to collate data on the number of people with post-16 qualifications who (i) stayed in; or (ii) returned to the towns they grew up in, similar to those statistics recently introduced by the Office for National Statistics.
answer_text: My Department is working with the Northern Ireland Statistics and Research Agency and the Department of Education to develop a Longitudinal Education Outcomes (LEO) database. The creation of the LEO database will make possible the production of similar statistics on a local basis in the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what steps he will take to support childcare providers in advance of the increase in the National Minimum Wage in April.
answer_text: Minimum wage rates are a universal government intervention that apply to all employers across the United Kingdom.  While the rise in April 2024 will be good news for employees, it will undoubtedly increase the financial pressures on employers in several sectors, including childcare.  My officials, in conjunction with colleagues in other Departments are considering what short-term measures might be appropriate to ease the range of cost pressures which childcare settings and parents are facing.  My ability to provide any interim support will be dependent on the associated funds I have bid for, being made available by the Executive in my 2024/25 budget allocation.  Over the coming days, I plan to meet providers myself, to hear from them directly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) whether he will allocate the relevant funding for the Education Authority Pay and Grading Review; and (ii) what further steps he will take to support education support service workers.
answer_text: My officials are currently working with colleagues in the Department of Finance to seek business case approval for the Pay and Grading Review. Given the significant cost associated with the review, my Department will need additional funding from the Executive in order to implement its recommendations. I will therefore be bidding for this funding as part of the Executive’s Budget 2024-25 process.A separate review of the employment model that applies to Classroom Assistants is also underway. The Education Authority has been engaging with trade unions on this issue and has established a multi-disciplinary working group to progress this work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of (i) derelict; and (ii) unoccupied homes, broken down by (a) Assembly constituency; and (b) local government district.
answer_text: This information is not held in LPS.	Properties are not recorded by Assembly Constituency within the LPS rating system, however the table below sets out the number of Housing Stock* properties recorded as vacant within the rating system, by District Council in each of the last five years. question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what steps he has taken in relation to affordable childcare since taking up post.
answer_text: I recognise that there is a need for high quality affordable childcare and early education to enable parents to work and to give children the best start in life.The Early Years and Childcare Strategy is being developed by the Department of Education and is an Executive priority.  My department will support the development of this strategy.A senior DfE official has been appointed as a member of the Northern Ireland Childcare Affordability Scheme Task and Finish Group to develop and successfully implement the strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will fund the Skill Up initiative at the end of March 2024.
answer_text: The Skill Up programme is currently being evaluated and I will consider any new initiative based on the evidence collated.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether any of his departmental officials are specifically tasked to lead work on regional economic balance.
answer_text: Yes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how much funding his Department has provided to assist with the cost of student accommodation in the Foyle constituency in each of the last ten years.
answer_text: None, as my Department does not have responsibility for the funding of student accommodation in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) whether he will ensure pay parity for further education lecturers with school teachers; and (ii) whether he will consider working with the Minister of Education to establish a formal link in pay between further education lecturers and school teachers.
answer_text: Further Education lecturers’ pay, as well as the terms and conditions of employment, are matters for the Further Education colleges as employers. However, I am committed to ensuring that the pay provided to FE lecturers fully recognises the range of teaching and learning which they deliver to our students and their hard work and dedication in doing so. 		I regard the issue of closing the pay gap with teachers as a priority issue. I have therefore asked for my Officials to explore ways to ensure this pay gap is addressed as quickly as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the merits of a single department overseeing all aspects of education, including further and higher education.
answer_text: My Department is currently considering the report of the Independent Review of Education, which I understand includes amongst its recommendations that there should be a single Department overseeing all aspects of education, including further and higher education. Given the implications of this recommendation the merits, or otherwise, of such a move would need to be agreed by the Executive.  In the meantime, my officials are considering this recommendation, as well as the other recommendations in the report which are relevant to my Department. This process includes discussion with key education stakeholders including other relevant Departments and Arm’s-Length Bodies.  This will enable me to determine a way forward for those recommendations relevant to my Department, including in conjunction with the Minister of Education where appropriate. Partnership working and collaboration between the two departments is critical to support all our learners, and the Education Minister and I will be engaging on areas of mutual responsibility and interest.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the Coleraine Enterprise Zone is now closed, or will be closed, in order to facilitate the enhanced Investment Zone.
answer_text: The Enterprise Zone designation at Coleraine expired on 16 March 2024.

answer_text: Conventional Capital Grant Funding and Financial Transactions Capital loan funding have been provided by the Department for the Economy and the former Department for Employment and Learning over the last ten years to Ulster University to support the delivery of infrastructure projects. question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide an update on a redress scheme for victims and survivors of historical institutional abuse.
answer_text: As of 30 June 2024, the Redress Board has received 4,554 applications, of which 12 remain non-compliant or incomplete.  Redress Board panels have considered 4,978 applications making award determinations totalling £90,593,250, with £88,118,425 having been paid directly into the applicant or applicant’s solicitor’s account.  As of 30 June 2024, 56 compliant applications remained to be considered by a panel. Electronic versions of each of the four Board Annual Reports are available via the Assembly Library. The Redress Scheme is due to close to applications on 2 April 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of pay parity between school teachers and further education lecturers.
answer_text: The Department of Education (DE) only has policy responsibility for teachers’ pay in grant-aided schools. Teachers’ Pay is negotiated and agreed at the forum known as the Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC) which includes employers, sectoral interests, DE and the five main teachers’ unions in Northern Ireland.Policy responsibility for lecturers’ pay falls within the remit of the Department for the Economy.An assessment of parity between teachers and lecturers is not possible, firstly, as both are not within the remit of my Department and secondly, they have totally different terms and conditions, different working days / hours as well as totally different pay scales.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she will introduce legislation to abolish the use of good character references in trials involving sexual offences.
answer_text: I am aware of the concerns regarding the use of good character references in sexual offences trials; however, given the constraints of this shortened mandate and the significant resourcing challenges facing my Department, I am not in a position to advance policy or legislation in this area in the current mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department will expand ante-natal programmes as part of Sure Start.
answer_text: ANSWERAnte-natal support is already available to all parents living in the catchment areas of my department's Sure Start programme.The nature of provision in each of the 38 Sure Start areas is determined by local Sure Start management, in agreement with the Department of Health (which administers the Sure Start programme on behalf of the Department of Education), to meet the individual needs of each area.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to detail the total monetary level of discount claw-back from community pharmacy contractors, in each of the last five years; and (ii) for his assessment of whether the totality of this discount claw-back is allocated to pharmacies in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: (i) The total monetary level of discount claw-back from community pharmacy contractors for the last five years was:Prescription Financial YearDiscount Clawback2019/2020£29,750,924.242020/2021£29,997,209.852021/2022£28,342,857.522022/2023£29,261,244.662023/2024*£17,529,862.56*Total£134,882,098.83* 23/24 relates to April 23 to January 24 period and discount was suspended for the November and December payments.(ii) The discount claw-back is not allocated to, but deducted from community pharmacy.  The purpose of claw-back is to adjust   reimbursement in recognition that pharmacies are receiving additional profit through their efficient procurement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether the Social Sector Size Criteria-Bedroom tax will remain at 14 per cent for one extra bedroom and 25 per cent for two extra bedrooms for all social housing sector claimants.
answer_text: There are no current plans to change the Social Sector reduction percentages from 14 per cent for one extra bedroom and 25 per cent for two extra bedrooms. In Northern Ireland, these reductions are fully mitigated as part of the Executive approved Welfare Supplementary Payments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for a breakdown of figures provided to his Department by Ulster University in order to meet the capital investment costs of a campus that can accommodate 7,500 learners.
answer_text: Ulster University estimates that the capital investment required to accommodate 7,500 students at the Magee campus could be in the region of £385m.  This figure includes previously announced projects where funding is already committed as well as estimated costs to develop teaching accommodation, additional facilities for sports, student support services and student accommodation.  A breakdown of the investment already committed for the expansion of Magee, supplied by Ulster University, was provided to the Economy Committee in response to a written question, Ref EC2024:015 on 23 February 2024.No further breakdown is available from the University at this stage. As is the case for any major infrastructure project, costs will be influenced by a range of factors including procurement timescales, construction costs, inflation and the specific nature of the teaching and support facilities required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the list of departmental (i) policies; and (ii) strategies that include addressing regional economic imbalances as an objective.
answer_text: Addressing Regional Balance is one of my four key priorities. Interventions to address this will be included in the Sub-regional Economic Plan I will publish in the autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) whether he has requested Ulster University to provide estimations of resource and capital investment necessary to reach (a) 7,500 students; (b) 10,000 students; (c) 15,000 students; and (d) 20,000 students at the Magee campus; and (ii) the outcome of that request.
answer_text: I have established a Taskforce to expand Magee. No limits have been set but the initial target is to expand students numbers to 10,000 in line with New Decade, New Approach.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they will extend the funding for the North West Strategic Growth Partnership beyond 31 March 2024.
answer_text: The current North West Development Fund, which supports the operation of the North West Strategic Growth Partnership, is scheduled to continue until March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on his work to prevent job losses at the BT site in Enniskillen, including a list of all engagements he has had in relation to this matter.
answer_text: There have been ongoing discussions between my department and officials within Invest NI, both with BT and also with local stakeholders including Fermanagh and Omagh District Council and the Unions, since the announcement of the review of the BT Enniskillen Site on the 08 March 2024.Invest NI executives met with BT on Tuesday 13 February 2024 regarding the announcement of a review of the future of its EE customer contact centre in Enniskillen. At the meeting, BT confirmed that it is undertaking what it describes as a major modernisation programme across its global business. This includes the introduction of new technologies and innovation, and the consolidation of buildings within its estate. The company confirmed that it had engaged with staff at the Enniskillen location, who have been offered what BT describes as a voluntary paid leavers package. BT again stressed that no decision has yet been taken on the future of the site, which is pending the outcome of the review. On Friday 23 February Invest NI met with representatives from Fermanagh and Omagh District Council to discuss the potential impact on Enniskillen and to discuss options being considered.On Tuesday 27 February, Invest NI met with GB based representatives from EE, along with local senior management from BT, to discuss options for the staff and for EE in Enniskillen. On Wednesday 28 February I wrote to the Chief Executive of the BT Group to emphasise the importance of regional balance and to urge the business to retain the employment in the Enniskillen area. Also on Wednesday 28 February, Invest NI met with representatives from the Communication Workers Union, including staff from the EE Enniskillen site.On Thursday 29 February my Special Adviser, along with Invest NI, met with representatives from the Communication Workers Union and the Irish Congress of Trade Unions. Also, on Thursday 29 February, BT confirmed that the deadline for BT staff to express their interest in the Voluntary Leavers Package had been extended from the 08 March 2024 to the 28 March 2024. My officials at Invest NI are continuing to engage with the business to influence the best possible outcome for the staff concerned. BT has continued to state that no decisions have been taken, pending the outcome of the review of the EE site in Enniskillen.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for the number of apprenticeships currently offered, broken down by (i) level of apprenticeship; (ii) local council area; and (iii) gender.
answer_text: As demand-led programmes, ApprenticeshipsNI and Higher-Level Apprenticeships apprentices are recruited and retained in line with business needs. The latest published statistics are available using the following links. ApprenticeshipsNI statistics from August 2013 to July 2022 | Department for the Economy (economy-ni.gov.uk)	Higher Level Apprenticeship activity | Department for the Economy (economy-ni.gov.uk)

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for a breakdown of Student Finance NI funding allocated to students currently studying in (i) England; (ii) Scotland; (iii) Wales; (iv) Northern Ireland; and (v) the Republic of Ireland.
answer_text: In the academic year 2022/23, the following funding was allocated by the Department. North of IrelandSouth of IrelandEnglandScotlandWalesTotal (£m)Tuition Fee Loans£112.8£1.1£80.5£18.0£3.2£215.6Maintenance Loans£73.1£2.3£32.2£8.9£1.4£117.9Maintenance & Targeted Support Grants£36.2£0.7£13.0£2.4£0.5£52.8Total (£m)£222.1£4.1£125.7£29.3£5.1£386.3

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the review of further and higher education will include proposals to increase the student loan provided to postgraduate students.
answer_text: I recognise the role of Higher Education in a thriving, regionally balanced economy; however, it is clear that there are significant financial pressures facing both the institutions and their current and prospective students.  I have asked my officials to update me on any work to date on a review of Higher Education Funding, as well as proposals to take it forward.  Any review would be likely to consider support available to students.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for the number of employers offering apprenticeships, broken down by (i) level of apprenticeship; (ii) local council area; and (iii) gender.
answer_text: The number of employers offering apprenticeships is not held by my Department.Information on the number of apprentices by level of apprenticeship, local council area and gender can be found on my Department’s website at: https://www.economy-ni.gov.uk/topics/statistics-and-economic-research/higher-and-further-education-and-training-statistics.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the Supporting Newcomer Pupils policy, consulted on in 2019.
answer_text: ANSWER	The Department commenced a review of its Supporting Newcomer Pupils policy in October 2017 in response to the growing number and increasing diversity of newcomer pupils arriving in Northern Ireland.  In 2019, the Department held a public consultation on its review of the policy however, work was temporarily suspended in March 2020 due to the outbreak of the COVID-19 pandemic.  In 2022, work recommenced with policy proposals prepared for consideration of an incoming Minister.		Officials will now build on the previous consultation and revisit policy proposals to account for the current context, with a view to presenting proposals for my consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) what support is available to those diagnosed with an eating disorder and their families, with particular reference to support for families as patients turn 18 years old; and (ii) whether there are plans to expand this support further.
answer_text: (i) There are currently four adult Eating Disorder Specialist Teams across the five Health and Social Care Trusts.  The Belfast Trust and South Eastern Trust is represented by one team.  These teams have developed community based psychological therapy services using evidence-based treatments as per National Institute for Clinical Excellence (NICE) guidance.  They work to the Regional Care Pathway for the Treatment of Eating Disorders (2016). The care pathway outlines what people can expect if they are referred for treatment and provides summary of clinical advice derived from evidence-based practice. It also includes information to assist people with self-care and accessing other supports before, during and after treatment.The Regional Eating Disorder Network Group (REDNG) ensures local developments are coherent and standardised as far as possible. Representation on the REDNG is lifespan. It includes individuals from Strategic Planning and Performance Group, Public Health Agency, Health and Social Care Trusts including, Eating Disorder Service Clinical Leads, Team Leads and Clinical Service Managers, Psychology nominee (nominated on behalf of psychology services in all Trusts), experts by experience encompassing the voluntary sector, service users and carers, Trust service managers and Department of Health policy representatives. The role of the REDNG is to promote the uniformity of approaches to patients across Trusts and share best practice and learning from service developments.  It provides a consistent and equitable approach across NI to the planning and commissioning of high quality, effective and accessible treatment for service users. Standards are based upon those laid out by the Royal College of Psychiatrists and NICE (National Institute for Clinical Excellence).  REDNG currently fulfil that function, aiming to: •	improve outcomes for those who require Eating Disorder Services•	promote collaborative working between the Strategic Planning and Performance Group, Trusts, Department of Health Policy, Statutory Services and the Community & Voluntary Sector•	involve service users and carers in service developments•	ensure workforce development and sustainability•	address any teaching and training needs•	establish/promote gold standard clinical practice  Currently a clinical lead and a 0.5wte Network Manager are being recruited for the REDNG in order to improve eating disorder identification and treatment.  (ii)  Action 29 of the Mental Health Strategy 2021-2031 commits to ensure there are specialist interventions available to those who need it. 29d commits to enhance the regional eating disorder service. The Mental Health Strategy 2021-2031 notes provision of services for people with an eating disorder, recognising the benefits of REDNG, whilst acknowledging outcomes for these patients could be improved.  In particular, evidence suggests that early intervention is key.  This means supporting services to offer specialist treatment to all t

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has assessed the use of legislation in other jurisdictions to address regional economic imbalances.
answer_text: My immediate focus is on action to promote regional balance, rather than a long process to research, develop, and enact legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of whether the recent funding increase to the Derry and Strabane City Deal will ensure it is not scaled back due to inflation.
answer_text: I welcome the recent announcement by Derry City and Strabane District Council that its project partners have committed additional funding to its City Deal. This increase in investment will enhance the benefits to the region and support delivery of my aim of ensuring regional balance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for his assessment of the pay challenges facing NHS interpreters; and (ii) what steps he will take to address those challenges.
answer_text: Face-to-face spoken language interpreters are independent self-employed contractors who are not employed by the Health and Social Care (HSC) service but are paid for their service through the Business Services Organisation (BSO) at an agreed rate.HSC Interpreters must be registered with BSO and adhere to the HSC Terms of Engagement.  This stipulates an hourly rate of pay as well as a preparation payment.  (i)	The Department is aware that pay has not increased for a number of years and the impact this is having on the valuable service that interpreters provide. You will, however, be aware of the significant financial constraints within the wider Health Service.  There have been a number of correspondences to my office regarding this issue.(ii)	I have asked the Strategic Planning and Performance Group to review this matter.I will endeavour to do the best I can to come to a suitable resolution.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to detail (i) the MLAs it will fund to travel to Washington DC for St Patrick’s Day celebrations; (ii) the capacity they are travelling in; and (iii) the number of staff that will be accompanying them.
answer_text: The Assembly Commission has made arrangements for two MLAs to travel to Washington DC for St Patrick’s Day engagements as officeholders:The Chairperson of the Executive Office Committee, Paula Bradshaw MLA.The Speaker, Edwin Poots MLA.Each officeholder will be accompanied by an official from their respective offices.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will ensure the reinstatement of the Western Health and Social Care Trust's MS helpline at Altnagelvin Hospital.
answer_text: The Western Health & Social Care Trust Multiple Sclerosis (MS) Helpline was restricted in late December 2023 due to an unforeseen administrative staffing shortage.  The message service on the helpline was altered to reflect this and to ensure that patients with MS receiving disease modifying treatments had the ability to contact the MS nursing team with any issues. The Western Health & Social Care Trust and the MS nursing team recognise the importance of the helpline to patients with MS and intend to fully re-instate this service as soon as possible.  Recruitment is ongoing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) whether the alleviation of regional economic imbalances will be an objective of the Anti-Poverty Strategy; and (ii) when that strategy will be published.
answer_text: I am committed to delivering long-term, sustainable solutions to poverty in all its forms for our communities right across Northern Ireland.I am currently considering the next steps to be taken in relation to the Anti-Poverty Strategy.  Final content of the Strategy will be subject to Executive agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether an equality assessment has been undertaken by his Department in relation to the location of the Investment Zone.
answer_text: In developing the investment zone proposition I will ensure equality considerations are appropriately taken in to account.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) to detail the funding pressures on apprenticeship programs as a result of the loss of European Social Funding; and (ii) how he intends to meet those pressures in his budget.
answer_text: European Social Funding (ESF) previously met up to 60% of costs for the ApprenticeshipsNI programme.  Therefore, the loss of ESF funding has a created a significant pressure for my Department’s budget. The Executive continues to press the British government to fully replace lost EU funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of how perpetrators of non-fatal strangulation can be held to account when those incidents occurred more than six months ago.
answer_text: There is a general time limit of six months from the commission of an offence which can only be tried summarily for a complaint to be made to allow its prosecution. This time limit does not apply to offences such as non-fatal strangulation which can be tried either summarily or on indictment.Non-fatal strangulation occurring before 26 June 2023, when the new offence came into operation, would typically continue to be dealt with under existing offences against the person legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what steps he (i) has taken; and (ii) plans to take to assess the impact of the loss of European Social Funding on the community and voluntary sector since March 2023.
answer_text: While my Department did provide match funding to a small number of European Social Fund (ESF) projects in 2022/23, the Department for the Economy (DfE) were the Managing Authority for ESF in Northern Ireland (NI). Therefore, my Department has not assessed the impact of the funding ending.The Department of Finance (DoF), supported by all NI Departments, lead on the loss of EU Funding and the introduction of funding streams under the UK Government’s Levelling Up Agenda, including the Shared Prosperity Fund (SPF).The SPF NI Investment Plan was designed and developed by Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities (DLUHC) alongside the Northern Ireland Partnership Group, which included members of the community and voluntary sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what steps he will take to remove barriers in the school system to cross-border student mobility, particularly in relation to careers guidance provided in schools.
answer_text: The current barriers to cross-border student mobility are due to the entry requirements and maximum allowable student numbers for universities in the Republic of Ireland.Council for the Curriculum, Examinations and Assessment (CCEA) Regulation, which is part of the UK and Ireland Qualifications Frameworks and Policy Group, meets annually with colleagues from the Irish Universities Association (IUA) to ensure that qualifications taken and offered in Northern Ireland are understood by universities in the Republic of Ireland with the aim of informing entry criteria decisions thus promoting the portability of these qualifications.  CCEA Regulation also collaborates with the Central Applications Office (CAO), the higher education admissions authority in the Republic of Ireland.  This collaborative work with their counterparts in the Republic of Ireland has contributed to the Irish government’s recent decision, announced in December 2023, to remove the requirement for Northern Ireland applicants to have four A levels in order to score maximum points under the Irish application system.  The changes have already been adopted by the University of Galway and the Technological Universities group and are currently going through approval process in other universities in the Republic of IrelandInformation on the changes was shared with the Education Authority for circulation to all post-primary schools and careers teachers to ensure they are up to date with the recent changes to the Irish points system for university admissions when supporting students with their university applications.  This correspondence also included further information for students on how to access course information on the Central Applications Office website (www.cao.ie).

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the implementation of the Period Products (Free Provision) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, including a timeline on the introduction of free period products to all public buildings.
answer_text: The Executive Office will ensure free period products are available to anyone who needs them from 13 May 2024.   TEO also plans to specify, by regulations, its public service bodies which will be under the duty, provided in section 2 of the Period Products (Free Provision) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, to make products available free of charge to persons while they are in their premises.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for a list of organisations that lost European Social Funding in March 2023, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: Details of ESF Priority 1 & 2 projects for whom ESF funding ceased on 31 March 2023 can be accessed on a constituency-by-constituency basis on-line as follows: 65 ESF Call 3 Projects by constituency and council area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how his Department's policies and strategies are informed by any barriers to investment that are identified by Invest NI.
answer_text: Infrastructure investment can have a positive difference to people and their wellbeing and can contribute to addressing regional imbalance and driving economic growth. Investment in infrastructure can also increasing employability and productivity by reducing production costs, creating more opportunities and driving innovation.My Department’s policies and strategies relating to investment are informed through an evidence-based approach, taking on board stakeholder engagement, and the available budget.  My Department engages with other Civil Service Department’s and agencies, including the Department for the Economy, Invest NI and the Strategic Investment Board (SIB) regarding a range of infrastructure issues to maximise our opportunities across the North and I look forward to working with Ministerial colleagues on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the work of his Department in linking the Wild Atlantic Way with the Causeway Coastal Route in order to promote the tourism offering of the North West City Region.
answer_text: Tourism NI, Failte Ireland and Tourism Ireland are working in partnership to deliver a €7.6m programme to improve synergies and experiences for visitors to the Causeway Coastal Route (CCR) and Wild Atlantic Way (WAW).This programme will deliver increased connectivity and improved visitor experience across the WAW and the CCR, providing an opportunity to bring together two of the most internationally renowned experiences that the island of Ireland has to offer. By demonstrating to the international visitor, the connectivity of the two routes and showcasing the Northwest of the island as a gateway to these two iconic experiences, it will create a dynamic proposition encouraging international visitors to explore more widely, stay longer, and spend more.One strand of the programme will be to undertake an audit of the CCR to assess the current visitor experience and develop appropriate discovery points along the route in line with the current WAW experience. This will include concepting of signage along the route. This work will be carried out in 2024 and all key stakeholders will be engaged before recommendations are implemented. A second strand of the programme aims to launch a small capital grants scheme for public and private businesses on both sides of the border. This will allow businesses aligned with the brands to apply for grant funding to improve interpretation, visitor experience etc. The scheme is being developed in partnership with the three agencies with the aim to launch by summer 2024.A programme of consumer research is currently live in four overseas market (Britain, USA, France & Germany). Findings from the research will inform a market campaign to be launched later in 2024 in one overseas market. The initial phase of the marketing campaign will be to highlight the accessibility of the two routes on one visit for international holidaymakers, building on the appeal of these two iconic tourism products.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what request he has made, or plans to make, to HM Treasury to locate the Investment Zone in the North West City Region, in order to address regional inequalities.
answer_text: My Economic Vision includes a clear commitment to regional balance and to the North West in particular. Any proposal I put to the Treasury in relation to the Investment Zone policy will be in line with this Vision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will initiate a formal review of (i) the pay of further education lecturers; (ii) their terms and conditions; and (iii) the parity between those lecturers and school teachers.
answer_text: Further Education lecturers’ pay, as well as terms and conditions of employment, are matters for the Further Education colleges as employers. I am committed to improving pay for Further Education lecturers.I have asked my officials for an update on the work already progressed in respect of this area under the Review of Further Education Delivery Model and for options available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in light of the Career or Care report released by the Carer Poverty Commission, (i) for his assessment of the challenges faced by unpaid carers in navigating the social security system; and (ii) any steps he will take in relation to those challenges.
answer_text: I agree that access to social security benefits for all those who are entitled, including those within our society who move in and out of employment due to fulfilling caring responsibilities, is vitally important.Information in respect of all social security benefits is widely available on NIDirect at https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/information-and-services/benefits-and-money. This includes advice, videos, contact details and support available.My Department’s Make the Call Service provides free and confidential needs assessments for individuals to check for entitlement to social security benefits, as well as additional supports and services. Further details can be found at https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/contacts/make-call-service or by calling Freephone 0800 232 1271.Help may be available to anyone in need of short-term financial assistance via my Department’s Discretionary Support Scheme, which includes the Universal Credit Contingency Fund non repayable grant for those awaiting their first Universal Credit payment. Further details are available via https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/campaigns/finance-support or by calling Freephone 0800 587 2750.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the suitability of the financial funding model for adult hospices.
answer_text: As part of existing arrangements for the delivery of contracted services, my Department has ongoing engagement with hospices. These arrangements provide hospices with opportunity to highlight any in-year service pressures should there be any threat to service sustainability.  In 2022/23 for example, in addition to core funding for agreed contracted adult hospice services, non-recurrent funding of £0.324million was made available in recognition of financial pressures facing hospices.Mt Department has proposed review of specialist palliative care for adults to consider how adult specialist palliative care services, including hospice services, might be planned, commissioned and funded to meet the current and future needs of our population. However, in the context of the ongoing and challenging financial pressures, it has not at this time been possible to take this review forward. While a budget for 2024/25 has not yet been set it is expected that the overall financial position for all NI departments will remain very challenging.  My expectation therefore is that we will not be able to do all that we want or need to do and the main focus will be on preserving and protecting existing services, with all the limitations that they currently have.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how he will improve support for unpaid carers to access further and higher education through financial support, such as bursaries and increased availability of part time and online courses.
answer_text: Access to Further and Higher education is key for addressing equality of opportunity and creating pathways to good jobs. This is particularly important for those who experience additional barriers to participation, such as people with caring responsibilities. To that end, a range of financial support is currently available including FE Grants, Hardship Fund, Care to Learn (NI) childcare scheme, Childcare Grant, Adult Dependants Grant and Parental Learning Allowance. However, I am very mindful of the current cost of living pressures, and I will be engaging with my officials to consider the current support available and determine whether this can be improved.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will instruct officials to conduct a desk-based review of existing departmental strategies with the view to determining whether they appropriately prioritise regional economic balance.
answer_text: I have asked officials to reconsider all work across the Department to identify how it could better deliver on my priorities including driving regional balance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) how he will work with employers on the challenges facing unpaid carers; and (ii) whether he will introduce tailored employability programs to support and retrain carers who wish to return to employment after a caring role has ended or reduced.
answer_text: Following a co-design and consultation process, I am committed to bringing forward an employment rights Bill within the current Assembly mandate to ensure the employment law framework remains fit for purpose and operates effectively for both businesses and workers. Carer’s Leave is an issue that will be considered in that context.The existing right to request flexible working also offers carers the opportunity to request working arrangements which allow them to balance their caring responsibilities with work.  I will be working with the Labour Relations Agency and other stakeholders to encourage employers to accommodate flexible working, and my proposed Bill also presents a potential opportunity for strengthening this right.My Department currently has several programmes available to all qualifying members of the public who wish to retrain. A current Careers Service bulletin on the Health and Social Care sector contains detailed information for those interested in a career in this field including entry routes and qualifications. This is available on the Departmental website. Each of these programmes and details on careers information may also be found on NI Direct.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) how barriers to investment identified by Invest NI, which fall under the policy remit of departments other than the Department for the Economy, are effectively reported to those departments; and (ii) what steps his Department is taking to ensure action is taken by other departments to address those barriers.
answer_text: My Department and Invest NI take a proactive approach to economic development and work collaboratively with other departments on cross cutting issues such as skills and labour supply.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will answer AQW 5912/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 5912/22-27 was answered on 29 February 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for his assessment of the challenges facing student renters; and (ii) what steps he will take in relation to those challenges.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the impact of cost-of-living increases facing students, including those who live in rented accommodation.This year, my Department is forecast to provide eligible students attending higher education institutions with maintenance loans and grants totalling £192m to help with accommodation and other living costs. My Department is also delivering a further £3.8m to support those students studying locally who are most in need.	I will be engaging with my officials to consider the current support available and determine whether this can be improved.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what steps she will take in this mandate to address the issue of the use of parental alienation in courts.
answer_text: The presence and impact of damaging parental behaviours in individual court cases is and must be a matter for the judge concerned, as informed by all available evidence.  The concept of parental alienation is not recognised in law in Northern Ireland and any policy change would be a matter for the Minister of Health but our Departments have been working collaboratively to improve the experience of families involved in proceedings under the Private Law Early Resolution Action Plan. I will continue to work with Minister Swann to scope and support any future actions and will continue to try and identify areas where action might be taken. 		I am aware, for example, that the Commissioner Designate for Victims of Crime plans to commission research to capture the experiences of children whose families have been involved in family proceedings. Work is expected to progress in the next financial year and I will carefully consider the findings when the research completes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for her assessment of the appropriateness of the current rules on the use of Financial Transactions Capital, given the significant underspend in recent years.
answer_text: The rules on the use of Financial Transactions Capital (FTC) are set by the Treasury. FTC can only be used to provide loans to or equity investment in the private sector.  The FTC budget is ring-fenced for this purpose and cannot be used for anything else.  In this regard, it is more challenging for departments to identify suitable projects that advance the Executive’s policy aspirations and fulfil the criteria of loan or equity investment.  I encouraged all departments to make bids for the use of FTC when responding to the current Budget process and would ask that all Ministers examine options for utilising this funding source. The Executive is required to repay 80% of the FTC allocations received from the Treasury. My department has agreed with the Treasury that any underspends may be used to offset these repayments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for a breakdown of the organisations affected by the loss of European Social Funding, broken down by (i) Assembly constituency; and (ii) amount of funding lost.
answer_text: Details of ESF Priority 1 & 2 projects for whom ESF funding ceased on 31 March 2023 can be accessed on a constituency-by-constituency basis on-line as follows: 65 ESF Call 3 Projects by constituency and council area.The total amount of funding awarded (EU and Programme match was £26.8m for the 2022-23 period).I understand that 18 bidders secured £57.5m from the British Government under Shared Prosperity (SPF) Economic Inactivity funding for 2023-25 (£26.75m per annum). Emergence of new, consortia or amended groupings bidding for SPF means that a direct comparison with previous ESF funding is not possible.It should also be noted that ESF funding of c£12m pa for Level 2 & 3 apprenticeships has not been replaced, leaving my Department with a funding pressure for the NI Apprenticeships Programme.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister how they will ensure regional balance in their Department's policies, strategies and plans.
answer_text: We are committed to delivering real change and prosperity for all our communities. That means tackling the barriers to inclusion and prosperity and addressing the crisis in public services, wherever people live and work, to ensure that all our communities, all parts of our society can realise their potential. That focus will underpin how we deliver our programmes and interventions in The Executive Office, including in our continued support for the North West Strategic Growth Partnership. However, we also recognise that delivering real change and enabling all parts of our place to realise their full potential will require a focus on outcomes as much as programmes and action across all Executive Departments. Whilst we are currently in discussion about what our Executive’s priorities will be, ensuring that all areas are supported to achieve economic growth and prosperity is a key priority.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he has considered introducing a non-taxable Child Payment.
answer_text: I am aware of requests for my Department to introduce a Child Payment similar to the scheme implemented in Scotland.  This matter along with other recommendations that have been made for a future mitigation package in Northern Ireland will need to be assessed in the climate of financial constraint and the requirement to ensure that budgets remain sustainable over the coming years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for his assessment of the recent changes to North South Student Mobility, introduced by the Republic of Ireland’s Minister for Further and Higher Education, Research, Innovation and Science; and (ii) what further steps he intends to take on this issue in order to increase cross-border student mobility.
answer_text: I have been encouraged by the recent steps taken by the Government in the south to improve cross-border student mobility. I will seek to build on the recent positive engagement I have had with Minister Harris to identify further actions that can be taken, separately and collectively, to open up greater opportunities for our young people to study throughout Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance how she will ensure that a regional balance is achieved in the distribution of public finance.
answer_text: ANSWERDelivery of a regional balance in the allocation of public funding will require the support of all Ministers as it is they who ultimately determine how to spend the resources allocated to them by the Executive through the Budget process.In taking recommendations to the Executive on the allocation of resources across departments, I can take account of the regional impact of various proposals.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how he intends to work with the Minister for Communities to ensure a joined-up approach between Departments on the (i) Early Learning and Childcare Strategy; and (ii) Anti-Poverty Strategy.
answer_text: Significant work has been done to identify and cost a range of high-level options for early learning and childcare services in Northern Ireland, informed by published research, OECD reports and case studies from other jurisdictions, including within the European Union.  This will support the development of a bespoke model for Northern Ireland and I intend to engage widely as we take this work forward.  The Department for Communities is represented on the cross-Departmental Early Learning and Childcare Strategy Programme Board and has also been invited to participate in the Task and Finish Group which my Department is setting up to develop both immediate and longer-term childcare proposals.The Minister for Communities leads on the Anti-Poverty Strategy, however poverty in Northern Ireland is an issue that cannot be addressed by any one department. To date, my Department has played an active role in cross-departmental working groups, contributing to the development of the Anti-Poverty Strategy and will continue to do so going forward.  There are a number of policies within my Department that contribute to the aim of addressing poverty in Northern Ireland, such as Free School Meals and Uniform Grants. I am keen to engage further on cross-cutting issues where the Anti-Poverty Strategy could provide a vehicle through which we can ensure that all children receive the support they need to thrive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) how the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy will be informed by comparison exercises with early education and childcare systems in European Union countries; and (ii) what engagements he will have with governments in the European Union to inform this strategy.
answer_text: Significant work has been done to identify and cost a range of high-level options for early learning and childcare services in Northern Ireland, informed by published research, OECD reports and case studies from other jurisdictions, including within the European Union.  This will support the development of a bespoke model for Northern Ireland and I intend to engage widely as we take this work forward.  The Department for Communities is represented on the cross-Departmental Early Learning and Childcare Strategy Programme Board and has also been invited to participate in the Task and Finish Group which my Department is setting up to develop both immediate and longer-term childcare proposals.The Minister for Communities leads on the Anti-Poverty Strategy, however poverty in Northern Ireland is an issue that cannot be addressed by any one department. To date, my Department has played an active role in cross-departmental working groups, contributing to the development of the Anti-Poverty Strategy and will continue to do so going forward.  There are a number of policies within my Department that contribute to the aim of addressing poverty in Northern Ireland, such as Free School Meals and Uniform Grants. I am keen to engage further on cross-cutting issues where the Anti-Poverty Strategy could provide a vehicle through which we can ensure that all children receive the support they need to thrive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice (i) for her assessment of the provisions of Section 12 of the Justice (Sexual Offences and Trafficking Victims) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 which provides for suspect anonymity 25 years after the date of the suspect’s death; and (ii) for a timeframe after which she will consider reviewing this legislation.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer I gave to AQW 5477/22-27. I have nothing further to add to that response at this time.
