My Department is also raising awareness of domestic abuse and the domestic abuse offence through a ‘Still Abuse’ advertising campaign, which will have further bursts later this year.  In addition, an eLearning package was developed with experts in the sector, which has been made available across the public sector.  Together, these will assist to increase knowledge and ensure earlier identification of abuse.    The Health and Justice Departments are also working collaboratively to develop a new seven year strategy to further progress work in this area, to take effect from April 2023. The new strategy will be consulted upon later this year and will have outcomes related to holding individuals to account for their abusive behaviour, supporting the

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the future of educational provision at Ballougry Primary School in the Foyle constituency.
answer_text: Development Proposal (DP) 691 was published by the Education Authority (EA) on 27 September 2022.  It proposes that “Ballougry Primary School will discontinue with effect from 31 August 2023, or as soon as possible thereafter”.  This started a statutory two-month objection period which will end on 28 November 2022, during which time interested parties are able to submit their expressions of support or opposition to the Department of Education (Area Planning Policy Team,  Rathgael House, Balloo Road, Bangor Co Down, BT19 7PR) or e-mailed to dps@education-ni.gov.uk. At the end of the statutory objection period, Departmental officials will gather all the relevant information and evidence and will present these in a detailed submission to me for my consideration. Unfortunately, I am unable to offer a specific date for a decision.  I appreciate the importance of a timely decision regarding the proposals and I am acutely aware of the need for certainty for the interested parties.  I will not delay a decision any longer than necessary.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what assessment his Department has made of the impact that a vacancy in the ministerial office, post 28th October 2022, will have on students.
answer_text: This question was asked in the previous mandate and to the previous Minister for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) what steps he is taking to support social work students during the cost of living crisis; and (ii) what discussions his Department has had with the Department for the Economy in relation to support for social work students.
answer_text: I recognise the additional hardship faced by students undertaking clinical courses due to the cost of living crisis. My Department provides a Social Work Student Incentive Scheme for all NI Degree in Social Work students who are domicile and studying full-time in Northern Ireland. Social Work students can access a non-means tested payment of £4,000 for each year of their study.  In addition, they are awarded £500 per year to cover expenses whilst on Practice Learning placements.  Unfortunately, due to budgetary constraints, my Department is unable to provide more assistance to students than it does currently.The DOH Student Incentive Scheme is in addition to any student finance support, which can be applied for separately. I understand that the Minister for the Economy has recently announced a substantial increase in the Student Loan Scheme, which is available to NI domicile students.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the introduction of electronic prescribing in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Departments Strategic Planning and Performance Group leads on a programme  to replace the current written prescription system with an electronic prescribing system. However, my Department continues to operate under significant budgetary and financial constraints including in our Capital budget.  I remain hopeful that implementation of this project will be able to proceed once capital funding has been prioritised for this programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what consideration has been given to developing gender pay gap legislation.
answer_text: Section 19 of the Employment Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 sets out the requirement for employers to publish information relating to the pay of employees for the purpose of showing whether there are differences in the pay of male and female employees, i.e. the Gender Pay Gap.  The way forward on how this legislation is to be enacted is being considered in the context of the development of the Gender Equality Strategy.  I will be engaging with my officials in the near future to understand the work that has been done on this matter and I will announce a way forward in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what flexibility is afforded to school principals on the maximum number of places in A-level science classes.
answer_text: For A-Level science classes, there is no flexibility afforded to school principals, the maximum number is limited to 20 pupils.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the progress of the replacement of Waterside Youth Club, Derry.
answer_text: Following approval of the Business Case Addendum, works commenced on site in February 2023 and the scheme is due to complete in June 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) what consideration his Department has given to the request by NUS-USI for free travel for students; and (ii) what assessment his Department has made of the response to the cost of living crisis from other governments across Europe to address the costs of public transport.
answer_text: I fully recognise the difficulties facing all our citizens, including students, in light of the current cost of living crisis.However with the continued DUP boycott of the Executive and the absence of an agreed 3 year budget my department is facing significant budgetary pressures further impacted by a decade of austerity budgets from Westminster, the unprecedented increases in energy costs and rising inflation. Whilst my officials have assessed what could be done, all options require financial support which is currently not possible given the resource position outlined.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what consideration her Department has given to a rent freeze, given the current cost of living crisis and following the recent intervention by the Scottish government.
answer_text: The powers to control rents set out in Section 7 of the Private Tenancies Act (NI) 2022 (adding 5C to the 2006 Order) expired on 27 October 2023. I currently have no plans to introduce new rent controls in the private rented sector.This is because research carried out by my Department concluded that the best ways of relieving pressure on affordability for renters was by ensuring the benefit system properly takes account of the cost of housing and by increasing housing supply. Northern Ireland Housing Executive rent levels are considered by taking into account inflation, projected running costs, spending requirements and tenant affordability. Rent pays for all of the essential services that tenants need, this includes customer and neighbourhood services as well as maintenance of homes and improvements. Affordability for tenants is of utmost importance in the decision-making process.Rent increases in Housing Association homes are an issue for each Housing Association Board. My Department has in the past called upon Housing Associations to be mindful of the financial pressures their tenants are facing, when considering rent increases. My Department last wrote to all Housing Associations in March 2022 with this request.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what engagement he has had with his UK counterparts regarding the £400 energy payment; and (ii) how those renters, particularly students, who have their energy costs included in their monthly rent will receive this support.
answer_text: I met with the Chancellor of the Exchequer and relevant partners to discuss the £400 Energy Bill Support Scheme on Wednesday 10 August and again on Monday 15 August, where everyone agreed that we need a solution which was simple and ensured people in Northern Ireland received the payment in line with elsewhere in the UK. The Department of Business, Energy & Industrial Strategy (BEIS) is working with the local Utility Regulator and Energy Suppliers on the urgent delivery of this and other support. As part of this work BEIS has confirmed that it is the intention that if students pay for their energy as part of their rent, then the landlord will be required to pass through the relevant energy support that they receive to the student as appropriate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what consideration her Department has given to ensuring that all schools issue a letter to parents informing them of the application portal for Free School Meals.
answer_text: In recognising the importance of pupils’ free school meals (FSM) entitlement being confirmed before the start of the academic year, the Education Authority (EA) has undertaken extensive communications explaining to families that in order to ensure they were able access FSM from the start of the academic year, they should apply before 31 July. This was communicated via social media and the EA website. In early July the EA also sent direct emails to approximately 30,000 parents who had applied last year and were yet to register and apply this year, encouraging them to do so and signposting them to the EA website.In support of this, the EA sent letters to school principals on 1 June asking them to promote awareness of FSM within their school community and to encourage all potential applicants to apply before 31 July. In late September the EA provided a promotional graphic to all schools asking to them to consider sending it to parents to encourage them to submit applications as soon as possible, particularly if the number of pupils registered for FSM at their school was lower at this point than in previous years.   In a letter from the EA to all schools on 28 September in relation to the use of FSM entitlement numbers within the school census, principals were also asked to encourage any parents they were aware of who had not submitted a FSM application to submit an online application as soon as possible or to contact the EA if they required technical assistance to make their application.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many patient beds were available in each year from 2010 up to and including 2022, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: Information on the number of available beds per year can be found in the Department’s annual publication “Hospital Statistics: Inpatient and Day Case Activity”. This can be found at the following link: http://www.health-ni.gov.uk/articles/inpatient-and-day-case-activity.Please find overleaf a table with the number of available beds per year from 2010/11 to 2021/22, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust. Average available beds1 per year from 2010/11 to 2021/22, broken down by Health and Social Care TrustYearBelfast HSCTNorthern HSCTSouth Eastern HSCTSouthern HSCTWestern HSCTTotal22010/112,504.21,158.2969.61,059.91,040.56,732.42011/122,411.71,061.3951.41,006.11,008.06,438.52012/132,311.01,035.4976.9963.6993.16,280.02013/142,255.11,039.3954.7920.5981.96,151.52014/152,203.41,000.6957.8888.7983.86,034.32015/162,149.6986.3929.6896.5925.15,887.22016/172,128.31,015.2950.0904.9911.55,909.92017/182,087.41,020.6959.0914.0912.25,893.22018/192,061.71,022.4946.3901.1899.15,830.42019/201,983.81,023.3949.5918.8904.45,779.82020/212,037.8978.8867.8934.2854.05,672.62021/222,064.21,004.8944.3915.5875.45,804.2Source: Quarterly KH03a returns from each Health and Social Care Trust1Average available beds during the year is only measured in wards that are open overnight, measured at midnight. Beds reserved for day case admission or regular day admissions are not included.2Total available bed figures are averaged from the sum of every Health and Social Care Trust for the year and so may not add exactly to the individual sum across the year using the averaged figures.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) whether his Department has plans to ensure that women who underwent Essure procedures have access to their removal and are informed of this option; and (ii) whether his Department has put in place plans to reduce the waiting lists for Essure removals.
answer_text: Patients who require treatment or surgery associated with Essure procedures are currently included within the general Gynaecology lists with the appropriate priority coding per clinical team, this group of patients is not identified separately on waiting lists.  At present, women who have cancer or suspected cancer are prioritised for surgery and there is therefore a delay in other Gynaecology cases.  Regrettably, demand within gynaecology exceeds the current capacity.There are currently no plans in place to specifically deal with those patients who are waiting on the gynaecology waiting list to have their Essure coil removed as the focus remains on addressing red flags and cancersA key priority for all Trusts is to maximise all elective capacity to ensure that as many patients as possible are seen and treated in an environment that is safe for both patients and staff. As Minister, I will continue to do the best I can to deliver for the public with the resources I have available.Amid ongoing budget uncertainty, I am on record as warning that funding pressures in health may be significant by the second half of 2022/23. The financial situation will undoubtedly be constrained whatever the final budget settlement.My ability to plan strategically is being significantly impaired by the ongoing budgetary uncertainty.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) what consideration his Department has given to the need to mandate hospitality venues to provide spiking testing kits, given the impact on public health; and (ii) whether he will undertake to work with his Ministerial colleagues to mandate hospitality venues to provide spiking testing kits.
answer_text: The spiking of alcohol drinks in hospitality venues is a criminal act, and any incidents where drink spiking is suspected to have occurred should be reported to the police in the first instance, so that investigations can be undertaken and for appropriate supports to be put in place for the person affected.As the Department for Communities has responsibility for the licensing of hospitality venues and the Department of Justice leads on community safety, I would be happy to have conversations with my Ministerial colleagues, as appropriate, to ensure that people can enjoy a safe night out.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 3970/22-27, what consideration her Department has given to the need for legislation to ensure members of the judiciary receive compulsory training on domestic abuse.
answer_text: As you will be aware, the judiciary are independent and the issue of judicial independence as separate from Government is sacrosanct.  Judicial guidance and training is a matter for the Lady Chief Justice, delivered through the Judicial Studies Board.  In light of this, it would not be appropriate to consider legislation in this area.I am always keen to explore any opportunity to increase knowledge and awareness around domestic abuse more generally for all those involved in the criminal justice system.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what consideration his Department has given to the reintroduction of the Apprenticeship Incentive Scheme.
answer_text: There are no plans to reintroduce the Apprenticeship Incentive Scheme which was introduced as a short term measure to address the impact of the Covid 19 pandemic on apprenticeships.  My Department will continue to support fund the off the job element of an apprentice’s training and will keep the need for any further incentive scheme under review.  I remain committed to widening apprenticeship participation and continuing to support employers, apprentices and training providers to maintain and grow the apprenticeship landscape.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what discussions he has had with the UK or ROI governments regarding the capital investment necessary for the expansion of student numbers in the North-West; and (ii) to outline the outcome of those discussions.
answer_text: My officials highlighted to the Northern Ireland Office at the time the New Decade New Approach agreement was negotiated the very significant costs involved in further expansion of student numbers in the North West region of Northern Ireland. My officials are in ongoing discussion with their counterparts in the Republic of Ireland’s Department of Further and Higher Education, Research, Innovation and Science on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline the limit of Ministerial responsibility regarding the movement of money between Units of Business within Departments, in the absence of an Executive.
answer_text: Department of Finance In-year Monitoring of Public Expenditure Guidance on the movement of funding states that Ministers have flexibility to move funding across units of business within the same category of spend.Exceptions to this would be where funding is ear marked and has been provided for a specific purpose.I would expect the same principle to apply in the absence of an Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when he will answer AQW 3622/22-27.
answer_text: This question was asked in the previous mandate and to the previous Minister for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 4020/22-27, whether his Department has engaged with GP practices to update them in relation to their role in identifying patients who need to receive the Nuvaxovid Covid 19 and referring them to their local Trust to administer the vaccine at an allergy clinic.
answer_text: GP advisors in the Strategic Planning and Performance Group (previously the Health and Social Care Board) advised GPs of the process for referring patients who need to receive a non-mRNA COVID-19 vaccine to a Trust allergy clinic.  This advice was issued during earlier stages of the COVID-19 vaccination programme and while the brand of non-mRNA vaccine has now changed the process remains the same.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what steps are being taken by his Department to advance cross-border collaboration in higher and further education.
answer_text: I fully recognise the benefits of bilateral and multilateral research and innovation collaboration in general, not just with the Republic of Ireland.  As such I am keen to explore opportunities that will allow our many world-class scientists and innovators to collaborate with partners wherever they may be located in the world, in order to drive societal and economic benefit.	In Higher Education, one such existing example is the long standing US-Ireland R&D Partnership which gives researchers in Northern Ireland institutions unique access to research collaboration opportunities with United States as well as Republic of Ireland based institutions.  The Department provides up to £2 million each year to support the Northern Ireland universities’ participation in the Partnership.My officials have had a number of discussions with their counterparts in DFHEIRS about how potential investment and policies may be beneficial to Higher Education in both countries.The Department does not currently have any formal arrangements to advance cross-border collaboration in Further Education (FE), however the FE colleges are engaged in a number of programmes and exchanges with institutions in the Republic of Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what assessment her Department has made of the need for members of the judiciary to receive any training or guidance on the impact of unconscious bias on decisions.
answer_text: The judiciary are an important part of the justice system. They are independent, including with respect to training, and I am bound to respect that independence.In his report to my Department on Law and Practice in Relation to Serious Sexual Offences, Sir John Gillen recommended that ‘the Judiciary should become more open to multiple stakeholder engagement in training and to the potential offered from training by outside agencies’. Sir John Gillen also stated that if ‘judicial training is to be effective, training has to be regularly given, refreshed and coordinated across all stakeholders in the criminal justice system’. The Gillen report was accepted by my department.The Lady Chief Justice has statutory responsibility for the provision of training for the independent judiciary.  She has recently restated publicly her commitment to ensuring the judiciary receive appropriate training, and I welcome her ongoing commitment. Whilst delivery of such training is a matter for the Judicial Studies Board, which is independent of the Department of Justice, we are committed to sharing training and awareness-raising resources developed by and for our justice partners with the Board where appropriate and helpful.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the benefits of further transparency in the proceedings of family courts.
answer_text: I am aware of the view that family courts should be more open to the media and it is, of course, important to promote public understanding of, and confidence in, judicial decision-making. To that end, I welcome that important (anonymised) judgments of the High Court, which hears the most complex family law cases, are published. However, family courts deal with the most sensitive and personal of issues, and I am mindful that there are also strongly held views against allowing greater media presence in, and reporting of, family law cases. Therefore, any move towards greater openness in the family courts would have to be very carefully considered and balanced with the paramount importance of protecting the privacy and safety of the families involved, particularly children.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what consideration his Department has made of the Cost of Survival campaign asks by the National Union of Students and Union of Students in Ireland.
answer_text: I have recently set out that I will be using the Carnegie Framework as the basis for assessing Good Jobs.  This Framework identifies seven dimensions of job quality and my Department will work to progress all seven dimensions with a particular focus on addressing issues around job security, guaranteed hours and low wages. An assessment of progress will be included in the Department’s forthcoming business plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether (i) Essure procedures were discontinued in August 2017, in the Belfast Trust; and (ii) other Health and Social Care Trusts continued these procedures beyond August 2017.

To ask the Minister of Health what engagement he is undertaking with the Health and Social Care Trusts to ensure the restoration of (i) day services; (ii) respite; and (iii) transport provision, to pre-pandemic levels.
To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 3425/22-27, in relation to the speech and language therapist workforce.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 2716/22-27, for an update on the progress of flood alleviation schemes in the Foyle constituency, including the flood alleviation proposals for Drumahoe and Eglinton.
answer_text: As you are aware my Department is developing flood alleviation proposals for Drumahoe and Eglinton and these projects have now progressed to detailed design. However, the development of these proposals are complex and have required re-evaluation to determine if a viable solution can be found. As previously advised this re-evaluation continues and will include a review of the recent flooding to ensure that any proposals would be effective. It is hoped this re-evaluation will be completed by the end of 2022. Subject to the identification of a viable proposal the detailed design will be further developed, in conjunction with the environmental impact assessment, and I have asked my officials to progress this work as quickly as possible. A further flood alleviation scheme is currently proposed for the Derry City area, mainly focusing on tidal flood risk.  It is intended to progress this project to design as promptly as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what steps her Department has taken to support the development of breakfast clubs in more schools.
answer_text: In 2022/23, my Department made funding of £9.1m available to schools for the Extended Schools (ES) programme, which has been in place since 2006. The programme currently enables around 480 schools serving the most disadvantaged communities to provide a range of interventions and additional learning opportunities outside of the traditional school day. Provision is determined by individual schools, in response to the particular needs of their pupils, parents, families and the wider community. Many schools choose to offer breakfast and after-school clubs to pupils. In the 2020/21 academic year 149 schools used Extended schools funding for a breakfast club. In view of the on-going financial crisis faced by many families, I would welcome an increase in funding to DE's baseline for programmes like Extended Schools to provide additional interventions such as breakfast clubs. I appreciate however, that there are many other issues facing the NI Block and in light of the advised 2022/23 indicative baseline, the Department of Education is currently facing inescapable or pre-committed pressures in excess of £500m.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what engagement he has had with his UK counterparts to ensure that local businesses who had been in receipt of EU funding will be able to have this funding gap met via the Shared Prosperity Fund.
answer_text: This question was asked in the previous mandate and to the previous Minister for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for an update on how businesses in Northern Ireland will be able to access the Energy Bill Relief Scheme; (ii) when this support will be rolled out locally; and (iii) whether he has engaged with his UK counterparts to expedite the timeline for delivery.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the pressure businesses are facing, primarily as a result of soaring wholesale energy costs associated with Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. As we are seeing across Europe and beyond, the magnitude of these global inflationary pressures means that it can only be tackled meaningfully by significant interventions at a national government level. 		As part of the support measures announced on 21st September, the UK Government (UKG) confirmed that a similar scheme to the GB Energy Bill Relief Scheme, which provides help to businesses and other non-domestic customers, will be established in Northern Ireland.  This is a discount being applied to all non-domestic consumers for electricity and natural gas for 6 months from 1st October – 31st March 2023 – this will be reviewed after the six months with plans to support the most vulnerable businesses.	The Department for Business, Energy & Industrial Strategy (BEIS) is delivering the support scheme, engaging with the Northern Ireland Utility Regulator and local energy supply companies. More details can be found on the UKG website.	I continue to press the Government to ensure that these support measures reach Northern Ireland businesses as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice how many reports of domestic abuse, involving controlling and coercive behavior, have been recorded by the police, each month since February 2022, in each constituency.
answer_text: The recording of information on domestic abuse offences reported to the police is a matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the Chief Constable and the role of the Northern Ireland Policing Board.The Police Service of Northern Ireland maintain their own statistics on offences reported to them. You may, therefore, wish to direct any question relating to the number of domestic abuse offences involving controlling and coercive behaviour recorded by the police, to the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what support is available to Northern Ireland Housing Executive clients to ensure that their access to medication they are being prescribed is not interrupted when they are housed in temporary accommodation, when that temporary accomodation is a significant distance from their registered GP.
answer_text: I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive of the Northern Ireland Housing Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Any placement of temporary accommodation will be provided with consideration to the needs of the applicant and the wider supply that is available. While such consideration will include factors such as access to GPs it should be noted that the Housing Executive can meet its homelessness duty by keeping an applicant in accommodation that would not be suitable to occupy on a long-term basis, but it would not be unreasonable for the applicant to occupy for a short period. This is while the Housing Executive continues to look for a permanent housing solution, which can be a Housing Executive or Housing Association property, within their area of choice.The Housing Executive will also work to avail of any additional necessary support from other agencies to support clients who are placed in temporary accommodation.If an applicant does not agree with the Housing Executive’s decision as to the suitability of the accommodation offered, they have rights within the Housing (Northern Ireland) 1988 Order (as amended) to request a review as to the suitability of accommodation offered to them.”

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for an update on the repair and refurbishment of Triangle's housing stock; and (ii) the date for the commencement of this work.
answer_text: I would refer you to the answer I provided for AQW 9478/ 22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what action he is taking (i) to establish a dedicated Mother and Baby Unit; and (ii) to ensure health professionals are trained in postpartum psychosis and severe perinatal mental illness.

To ask the Minister for Communities (i) how often inspections are carried out to monitor the quality and safety of non-standard temporary accommodation being used by the Northern Ireland Housing Executive; and (ii) what checks are in place to ensure that staff, working in non-standard temporary accommodation, are fulfilling safeguarding duties.
answer_text: I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive of the Northern Ireland Housing Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The use of non-standard accommodation is concerned solely with securing the availability of accommodation units to enable the Housing Executive to discharge its statutory duty to make available temporary accommodation to individuals presenting as homeless. In such circumstances, the Housing Executive is acquiring the use of accommodation in the same manner as other customer groups who avail of Hotels/B&Bs whereby the respective Hotel/B&B is subject to the relevant checks and accreditation in order to operate as short-term accommodation.An exercise took place during November and December 2023 whereby non-standard properties were subject to a process which involved visits from Housing Executive staff and a repeat exercise was conducted in July 2024. While the primary focus of these visits was to confirm occupancy of such accommodation, staff were asked to highlight if they identified any obvious issues with the accommodation; no issues were identified in respect of the quality of non-standard premises used by the Housing Executive.While the Housing Executive is not responsible for inspection of such premises, or for checking that staff in such premises are fulfilling safeguarding duties, it should be noted that should any individual find themselves placed in non-standard accommodation a Housing Advisor will work to identify the most appropriate temporary accommodation placement in respect of move-on accommodation.”

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she will undertake to work with her Ministerial colleagues to mandate hospitality venues to provide spiking testing kits.
answer_text: My Department has no locus over licensed or hospitality venues and would therefore have no role in mandating that such venues provide drink-spiking kits. I am however willing to work with Ministerial colleagues with responsibility for hospitality venues to offer any assistance I can in progressing this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what steps she has taken to ensure that girls in primary and secondary education are afforded the choice over whether to wear skirts or trousers to school.
answer_text: School uniform policy is not currently governed by legislation and it is a matter for the Principal and Board of Governors to develop and implement their own school uniform policy. To assist schools in developing their school uniform policies the Department has previously issued guidance in circular 2011/04. This is available at https://www.education-ni.gov.uk/publications/circular-201104-guidance-schools-school-uniform-policy-revised-5-june-2018. The guidance advises that schools should ensure that their school uniform policy is fair and reasonable, in practical and financial terms, and should have regard to their duties under relevant equality and other legislation. It encourages schools to consult widely when drawing up uniform policy and consider how they can involve their pupils directly in the process of developing a uniform policy. For example, through the school council providing input into designing the uniform or communicating the school’s policy to parents/prospective parents and other pupils.  You will be aware that my Department is also continuing to carry out a review of the cost of school uniforms with a view to potentially bringing forward legislation on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many children in each constituency are waiting for a special educational needs assessment.
answer_text: The figures provided by the Education Authority (EA) are as of 6 February 2024.ConstituencyNumber of Children undergoing Statutory AssessmentBelfast East85Belfast North146Belfast South67Belfast West150East Antrim43East Londonderry60Fermanagh and South Tyrone81Foyle113Lagan Valley81Mid Ulster50Newry and Armagh75North Antrim55North Down40South Antrim85South Down90Strangford70Upper Bann57West Tyrone54Total1402*Data only includes pupils who are undergoing their initial assessment and not those with cases open following a tribunal

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what action he is taking (i) to improve access to blood tests for those experiencing symptoms of leukaemia; and (ii) to encourage uptake of the training available to GPs about the diagnosis of leukaemia.
To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the New Decade, New Approach commitment to provide three funded cycles of IVF treatment.

To ask the Minister of Justice what steps she will take to act on the findings in the research published by Women’s Aid following its Come Together to End Domestic Abuse: a survey of UK attitudes to domestic abuse 2022.
answer_text: My Department, along with the Department of Health, jointly lead on the implementation of the current Stopping Domestic and Sexual Violence and Abuse in Northern Ireland Strategy as well as the development of a new seven year Domestic and Sexual Abuse strategy to be brought forward next year.    Earlier this year we launched a Call for Views to inform the development of the new strategy.  Responses to the Call for Views were received from a wide range of people and organisations across Northern Ireland, as well as other jurisdictions.  The findings in the report published by Women’s Aid following its Come Together to End Domestic Abuse survey, as well as other research and reports, will be considered in taking forward the new strategy and associated action plans that will be developed under this.  There are a number of areas raised in the report in which we have an interest, and in which work is being progressed, including awareness raising and working to change attitudes and make domestic abuse more abhorrent across all of society.  We are also all very conscious of the importance of increased knowledge and education regarding healthy relationships reflected in the report.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) when the Novavax Covid-19 vaccine will be available in Northern Ireland; (ii) how many doses will be available; and (iii) how individuals will be able to access it.
answer_text: Nuvaxovid, the COVID-19 vaccine developed by the Novavax Company, is now available in Northern Ireland and approximately 27,000 doses have been delivered for use here.The use of the Nuvaxovid COVID-19 vaccine is guided by the recommendations of the Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation (JCVI) as set out in the Green Book guidance on Immunisation.   For the autumn COVID-19 booster programme the Green book states:Nuvaxovid is indicated as a booster in exceptional circumstances for individuals aged 18 years and over when no clinically suitable vaccine (i.e. mRNA vaccine) is available.In addition the Green Book also states:When mRNA vaccines are not considered clinically suitable, Novavax COVID-19 vaccine may be used for primary vaccination of adults.In order to manage the stock, supplies of the Nuvaxovid COVID-19 vaccine will be restricted to Trusts.  The number of individuals who will need to receive the Nuvaxovid COVID-19 vaccine is expected to be very small and GPs will identify these patients and refer them to their local Trust to administer the vaccine at an allergy clinic.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what actions her Department is taking to support registered childminders through the cost of living crisis.
answer_text: Registered childminders are self-employed.  Their pay, or the fees charged for their service, is a contractual arrangement between the individual registered childminder and the parents using their service.  This does not fall within the remit of my Department.The cost of living crisis is being felt across all sectors.  Any response needs to be in the context of national measures.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what steps his Department is taking to ensure that customers who are struggling with debt are pro-actively identified and referred to appropriate advice, as per the recommendation in the Women’s Regional Consortium’s Women Living with Debt report.
answer_text: Since assuming responsibility for the commissioning and delivery of debt advice in 2019, my Department funds the delivery of debt advice services.  Advice NI is responsible for developing referral mechanisms across the public, private and voluntary sectors to ensure that debt advice can reach those who need it. Banks, energy companies and other essential providers pro-actively refer customers struggling with debt to Advice NI.In addition, my Department has facilitated collaboration between Make the Call and Advice NI to develop relationships and engagement specifically around improving and strengthening the debt referral process between both bodies.My Department uses contract information from Advice NI to assist in identifying demands, trends and unmet need, to inform policy change.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what assessment her Department has made of the need for judges to receive training on domestic abuse.
answer_text: I consider it essential that frontline professionals across a range of sectors have an appropriate awareness and understanding of domestic abuse in all its forms and how to recognise it.  This applies across the criminal justice system as a whole, as well as more widely, in terms of those who have a role to play in ensuring that offenders are brought to justice and that we can better support victims through the criminal justice system.  I am aware that the issue of judicial training in this area was also raised by a significant number of respondents as part of a recent Call for Views on a new Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy. As you will be aware, the judiciary are independent and judicial guidance and training is a matter for the Lady Chief Justice, delivered through the Judicial Studies Board. My officials engaged with Judicial Studies Board prior to introduction of the domestic abuse offence, including considering lessons to be learnt from other jurisdictions.  The matter of sentencing guidelines was also considered ahead of introduction of the new domestic abuse offence.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department will look at ways of improving access to affordable credit for low income families as a way of reducing reliance on high cost lending, as per the recommendation in the Women’s Regional Consortium’s Women Living with Debt report.
answer_text: While I welcome the Women’s Regional Consortium’s Women Living with Debt report, the issue of access to affordable credit is not within the remit of my Department. 		Regarding future Departmental policies being informed by the report, my Department conducts impact assessments on all proposed policies, including assessing the equality impact of such policies as required by Section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has carried out research into the financial wellbeing of higher and further education students and apprentices, over the last five years.
answer_text: My Department has not undertaken any of its own research into the financial wellbeing of higher students over the last five years.There has not been research carried out for the financial wellbeing of Further Education (FE) students over the last five years. However, a review of FE student support has commenced within my Department, which seeks to revisit FE student support arrangements and ensure that they remain affordable, accessible, aligned to wider strategy and policy, and appropriately address financial wellbeing needs of FE students.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) when the maintenance loan available for undergraduate students last increased; and (ii) what assessment he has made of the need to increase the maintenance loan with inflation, given the cost of living crisis.
answer_text: The last time the maintenance loan available for Northern Ireland undergraduate students was increased was in academic year 2010/11.The level of support provided through the higher education student support package, including loan support, is assessed on an annual basis. The support available for the current academic year reflects the financial position of my Department at the time I took the decision, at the start of this year.However, in recognition of the cost-of-living crisis for the current academic year, I have been able to allocate additional funding of £2.8m to the Higher Education Support Funds which are administered on my Department's behalf by the five local higher education institutions. This additional funding brings the total allocation for the year to £5.6m. My Department has provided greater flexibility for the local higher education institutions, when dealing with student applications, to ensure that any student who is experiencing genuine financial difficulty has access to support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) how many customers have received compensation through the Translink Delay-Repay scheme on each of the five routes in the last 12 months; (ii) whether his Department records an estimation of the number of customers eligible for compensation under the Delay-Repay scheme; and (iii) what steps have been taken to promote the Delay-Repay scheme to Translink customers.
answer_text: Details regarding the Delay-Repay compensation scheme is an operational matter for Translink. You should, therefore, direct your question to Translink at aqw.request@translink.co.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the Women Living with Debt Report by the Women’s Regional Consortium and whether the findings will inform the work of his Department going forward.
answer_text: While I welcome the Women’s Regional Consortium’s Women Living with Debt report, the issue of access to affordable credit is not within the remit of my Department. 		Regarding future Departmental policies being informed by the report, my Department conducts impact assessments on all proposed policies, including assessing the equality impact of such policies as required by Section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) what action his Department is taking to improve the punctuality of the Derry to Belfast train line; and (ii) whether he will commit to introducing an hourly train service on the Derry to Belfast train line on Sundays.
answer_text: Management of train services is an operational matter for Translink and you should, therefore, direct your question to Translink at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.Whilst I can see the benefits that an hourly Sunday service would bring, my Department is facing significant resource pressures in 2022-23, impacted by the unprecedented increases in energy costs and rising inflation. Therefore current funding does not allow an extension of any existing services on the NIR network however, where opportunities exist to bid for funding to extend services, I can confirm that my Department will do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for the total amount of funding allocated by his Department to Higher Education Student Support Funds for each of the last five years.
answer_text: My Department has allocated the following amounts to Higher Education Student Support Funds for each of the last five years as follows;Financial Year	Amount Allocated to Higher Education Support Funds £’000s2017/18			 £2,7662018/19			 £2,7682019/20			 £2,7842020/21			 £5,5792021/22			 £3,386For the current academic year, and in response to the cost-of-living crisis, I have been able to allocate additional funding of £2.8m to the Higher Education Support Funds bringing the total allocation for the year to £5.6m.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what assessment his Department has made of the accessibility to Higher Education Student Support Funds for students, including the extent to which the criteria for these funds appropriately take into consideration the Covid-19 pandemic and the cost of living crisis; and (ii) whether there has been any underspend in the Higher Education Student Support Funds in any of the last five years.
answer_text: The Higher Education Student Support Fund is open to any eligible student who meets the criteria as set by my Department. The Fund is administered by the local higher education institutions on my Department's behalf.  In recognition of the cost-of-living crisis for the current academic year, I have been able to allocate additional funding of £2.8m to the Higher Education Support Funds bringing the total allocation for the year to £5.6m.As was the case during the Covid 19 pandemic, my Department has impressed the need for appropriate flexibility for the local higher education institutions, when dealing with student applications, to ensure that any student who is experiencing genuine financial difficulty has access to support.  I have also stressed with the institutions the importance of ensuring a flexible approach when assessing student applications in light of the continuing impact of the cost-of-living crisis in this academic year.Given the demand led nature of support funds there is always the likelihood of small annual variances. My Department regularly assesses the need on funds and where appropriate additional monies are provided. Over the last five financial years (2017/18 to 2021/22), of the £17.2 million allocated to the local higher education institutions through the Support Funds, there has been an underspend of £18,092, representing 0.1% of the overall allocation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether she will undertake to work with her Ministerial colleagues to mandate hospitality venues to provide spiking testing kits.
answer_text: I share the Member’s concerns at the reported levels of drinks being spiked in licensed premises here.Should a Minister with the relevant powers propose bringing forward legislation to mandate hospitality venues to provide spiking testing kits, I will work with Ministerial colleagues to consider the policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 1902/22-27, for an update on the development of a Maternity Strategy.
To ask the Minister of Health what steps his Department will take to address the current challenges with the midwifery workforce.
answer_text: Since I took office in January 2020, significant progress has been made in addressing the staffing issues faced by the midwifery profession.  A key success has been the increase in the number of pre-registration training places commissioned by my department for midwifery. These places have increased by over 25% to 115 places per annum. Phase 11 of the Delivering Care policy framework has been commissioned by CNO to determine a safe and effective staffing model for the Midwifery workforce in Northern Ireland. This work is progressing within the PHA, and we endeavour to have a paper proposing the workforce model and requirements for midwifery by the end of 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps he has taken to secure the necessary funding for the continuation of the Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme.
answer_text: In my written assembly statement on 20 June 2022, regarding the reform of stroke services and the recovery of elective orthopaedic services in Northern Ireland, I announced my decision to extend the Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme (ROIRS) by allocating a further £5m, to continue it beyond the planned June 30 2022 deadline, until the £5m of new funding had been committed. I was clear at the time that this was money I did not have within my baseline and therefore was proceeding at risk. New applications were accepted from 1 July 2022 and the scheme closed to new applications at 5pm on Wednesday 21 September as the additional £5m funding had been committed.I am very conscious of pressing demands for extra funding across many areas of health and social care. As Minister, I will continue to do the best I can to deliver for the public with the resources I have available. Amid ongoing budget uncertainty, I am on record as warning that funding pressures in health may be significant by the second half of 2022/23. The financial situation will undoubtedly be constrained whatever the final budget settlement. However, as a result of these pressures, I regret that it has not been possible to assign funding to further extend the ROIRS. I still believe however it was the correct decision to extend it for the additional length of time that I did.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how many individuals have received the Sure Start Maternity Grant, over the last 12 months.
answer_text: The number of Sure Start Maternity Grant applications received and awarded in each of the last 12 months is shown in the table below:MonthApplications ReceivedApplications  AwardedSep 21327183Oct 21326189Nov 21324162Dec 21267146Jan 22317190Feb 22305170Mar 22365196Apr 22*321181May 22*277159June 22*302177Jul 22*309178Aug 22*307173*The management information included in this response while accurate at the time it was provided may change when subjected to final reconciliation/verification checks prior to publication as applicable.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) whether he has had any engagement with relevant Ministers or officials in the UK government on the lowering of the VAT rate for tourism and hospitality businesses; and (ii) what the outcome of that engagement has been.
answer_text: I have raised this issue at regular meetings of the UK Government and Devolved Administrations Inter-Ministerial Group on Tourism chaired by the DCMS Minister of Sport & Tourism. Through these meetings, Ministers have agreed to continue to raise the issue for an extension of the VAT rate reduction (at 12.5%) for UK tourism & hospitality businesses until 31 March 2023.In addition, my Department’s Tourism Recovery Action Plan included an action to support the tourism industry by continuing to lobby UK Government on this issue. This action was assigned to the Department of Finance (and the NI Tourism Alliance) as the convention is that the Department of Finance leads on lobbying HM Treasury on any such excepted fiscal matters. I have, however, also written to the Minister of Finance to request that he makes representations through his offices to HM Treasury. I know that he supports my view and has raised this and other fiscal lever issues with HM Treasury.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what consideration she has given to the benefits of a Child Payment Scheme, similar to the one in place in Scotland.
answer_text: The independent review of welfare mitigations will be published at the earliest opportunity. The implementation of any new payment schemes will require Executive agreement on the allocation of necessary funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps his Department is taking to support students of health and life sciences during the current cost of living crisis.
To ask the Minister of Education what estimation her Department has made of the increased costs, over the last six months, to childcare providers.
answer_text: I am aware through ongoing engagement with sector representatives that childcare providers, like all businesses, have faced increased costs in recent months. The Department of Health has responsibility for the regulation and inspection of childcare providers.  I have been engaging with the Health Minister to identify potential easements within the regulatory framework which would help to relieve some of the current pressures experienced by providers.A childcare provider survey has been issued as part of the Review of Childcare Services commissioned by my Department.  This includes questions on the financial health of the sector and will provide a more quantitative assessment of cost pressures and sustainability challenges.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what assessment her Department has made of the campaign for universal free school meals in all primary schools.
answer_text: The Department is continuing to progress a review of Free School Meals and Uniform Grant eligibility criteria. The review is considering a wide range of options that include the provision of a universal free school meal for some/all year groups as well as raising the current income thresholds that apply to the eligibility criteria for applicants in receipt of Universal Credit, Working Tax Credit or Child Tax Credit. It is planned that a public consultation will commence later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps his Department has taken in the last twelve months to raise awareness of ovarian cancer.
answer_text: My Department recognises the importance of raising awareness of ovarian cancer and the need to promote early intervention.  This important activity is being progressed as part of the NI Cancer Strategy 2022-2032 and specific actions relating to improving public awareness of cancer.The ‘Living Well’ service involves 500 local community pharmacies across Northern Ireland that are currently supporting a ‘Spot Cancer Early’ campaign. This campaign will run throughout September and October 2022 and aims to raise awareness of the signs and symptoms of all types of cancer and to help with early diagnosis.  Information on ovarian cancer, including its signs and symptoms, has also been included on the Public Health Agency’s ‘Be Cancer Aware’ website.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps his Department is taking to support the speech and language therapist workforce.
answer_text: My department has completed a Workforce Review report on Speech and Language Therapy 2019-2029. This is available at Speech and Language Therapy Workforce Review 2019 – 2029 | Department of Health (health-ni.gov.uk). The AHP workforce reviews will help to address one of the immediate priorities of the Northern Ireland Executive which is to transform HSC services. This Workforce Review Report, and the clear recommendations it contains, is the result of a wider Workforce Review Programme covering all thirteen AHPs in Northern Ireland including SLT. My Department is currently working on a collaborative implementation plan, aligned to the wider health and social care workforce strategy, for all the workforce reviews which can be taken forward immediately upon the restoration of the Executive. Link at Health and Social Care Workforce Strategy 2026 | Department of Health (health-ni.gov.uk)In addition, in May 2022 I announced a one-off expansion, for the 2022/23 academic year, of 5 additional pre-registration training places on the speech and language programme commissioned by my Department from Ulster University. This brings the overall total of commissioned speech and language places to 33, for 2022/2.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether she will make representations to the UK Chancellor for the removal of the £2,000 per annum cap on support available through Tax-Free Childcare.
answer_text: The Chief Secretary to the Treasury has recently responded to a Parliamentary Question advising that the UK Government does not intend to revise the maximum cap amount of £2,000 per child per annum.  You can view his answer at the following link Children: Day Care: 5 Sep 2022: Hansard Written Answers - TheyWorkForYou.As you are aware, I am awaiting a response from the UK Chancellor to my request that the Government contribution to Tax-Free Childcare be increased from 20% to 30%.   I consider that this measure would have a significant impact in helping affordability of childcare for many working parents, even if the overall cap is retained.  Data from HMRC indicates that only a very small percentage of families receiving Tax-Free Childcare in Northern Ireland currently reach the £2,000 cap per annum.  I will of course continue to make representations to HMT to increase the support available to families through this scheme at every opportunity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what steps she will take to pause social security debt deductions for low income households.
answer_text: The suspension of debt recovery in Northern Ireland would require a break in parity from the Department for Work and Pensions and the cost of any divergence requires Executive agreement.The Department for Communities recognises the financial hardship that many people may be experiencing because of the cost of living and is fully committed to supporting and helping individuals who may be finding difficulty with the repayment of social security benefit debt. Individuals should contact the Department’s Debt Management team, who will work with them to agree an affordable repayment plan. Contact details for Debt Management can be found on https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/contacts/debt-management-northern-ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) when his Department last published an annual report on air quality in Northern Ireland; and (ii) what plans his Department has to publish future annual reports on air quality in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department published the ‘Air Pollution in Northern Ireland 2020’ report on 15 December 2021. The report can be viewed on the DAERA website through the following link: https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/DAERA_Air_Pollution_in_NI_2020 or via the Air Quality NI website through the following link: https://www.airqualityni.co.uk/reports.		The Air Pollution in Northern Ireland 2021 report is currently being drafted and is scheduled for publication in November 2022. My Department has a statutory obligation to produce an annual report summarising air quality monitoring results in Northern Ireland and will continue to ensure this commitment is met each year.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many individuals were awaiting the outcome of their Free School meal application on 1 September 2022, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: The number of parents/guardians awaiting the outcome of their Free School Meal application on 1 September 2022 was 10,460. A breakdown of these applications by constituency is not able to be provided.As at 30 September, the Education Authority (EA) has processed over 81,000 applications for free school meals. The EA continues to work to a target of applications being processed within four weeks.  Following the opening of the EA’s Free School Meal application portal in early June, the EA undertook extensive communications via social media, the EA website, letters to schools and individual emails to parents/carers in receipt of FSM in 2021/22 academic year, encouraging them to apply before 31 July to ensure support would be in place for the start of the school year. Despite this publicity campaign, a significant number of applications were received after this date. Unfortunately this has resulted in these applications not being able to be verified ahead of the start of the school year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what discussions he has had with higher education institutions in Northern Ireland on introducing mandatory training for students on sexual consent.
answer_text: In April 2021, my officials wrote to the local Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) to provide them with the Department’s Statement of Expectations which is aimed at preventing and addressing harassment and sexual misconduct effecting higher education students in Northern Ireland.The Statement of Expectations provides a set of recommendations to help Northern Ireland’s HEIs develop and implement effective systems, policies and processes to prevent and respond to incidents of harassment and sexual misconduct. One of these recommendations is that HE institutions should implement adequate and effective staff and student training with the purpose of raising awareness of, and preventing, harassment and sexual misconduct.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for her assessment of each of the recommendations in the recent report on Women Living with Debt published by Consortium for the Regional Support for Women in Disadvantaged and Rural Areas; and (ii) what progress has been made on a debt respite scheme in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I very much welcome this report and I have tasked my officials to assess and consider those recommendations which fall within the remit of my Department.       Work to establish a Debt Respite Scheme has been initiated.  Officials are currently developing draft policy and consultation proposals, taking account of evidence, research and best practice models.Progress on the report and debt respite scheme will be held up by the continued absence of a functioning Executive and agreed budget which prevents additional measures being introduced.My Department continues to do all it can to support those experiencing financial hardship who are struggling to repay debt

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many continuing care packages remain unfulfilled or not fully implemented, broken down by Trust.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will consider the introduction of flood prevention measures for Rossmore, Derry, including the raising of kerbs and the implementation of a new drainage system to direct flood water away from homes.
answer_text: I understand that there are two properties within the development that have been affected by flooding during periods of exceptional rainfall. While this is indeed regrettable and I empathise with the homeowners, my officials advise that the existing drainage system is adequate for dealing with normal rainfall conditions and as such there are no proposals to put in a new drainage system. There is also no evidence to suggest that raising the kerbs would reduce the risk of flooding during potential future exceptional rainfall events.I am also advised the two property owners made applications under the Homeowner Flood Protection Grant scheme, both of which were successful, and that protection measures have recently been installed.  Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) whether she has received a response from the UK Chancellor to her request to uplift the rate of the Tax-Free Childcare scheme from 20 to 30 per cent; (ii) what measures her Department will be taking to address the immediate cost of living pressures on childcare providers and families in affording childcare; and (iii) whether she has made any representations to her Executive colleagues to agree immediate financial support for the childcare sector.
answer_text: I have not yet received a reply from the UK Chancellor regarding my request to uplift the rate of the Tax-Free Childcare scheme from 20% to 30%.Officials in my Department have been engaging with relevant NI and UK Government departments and key stakeholders, to encourage greater take-up of the financial support schemes already available to assist with the costs of childcare, including the Tax-Free Childcare scheme.The impact of the cost of living crisis on individuals and many business sectors is recognised across Departments.  Any response to the crisis needs to be in the context of national measures.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) what assessment his Department has made of the disproportionate economic impact of the cost of living crisis on women; and (ii) what measures or plans the Department has put in place to mitigate this impact.
answer_text: My Department has not carried out a formal distributional or gender-based  assessment of the economic impact of the current cost of living pressures. However, it is clear that inflationary pressures are stretching household budgets across the board with some households more vulnerable than others. That is why I welcome the UK Government’s commitment to deliver much needed energy support for the people of Northern Ireland – my Department will continue to engage with HM Treasury and others to ensure this is rolled out as quickly as possible.		In addition, a series of one-off cost of living payments is available to those on income-related benefits, including disabled claimants and pensioners, with more information, including eligibility criteria available on www.nidirect.gov.uk.	The outlook remains challenging but I am hopeful for the future. For my part, I am investing in those areas where we can gain the best long-term outcomes, which will help us build greater economic resilience for the future.  For example, I have published an energy strategy which will deliver a step change in how we move away from our reliance on overseas, imported fossil fuels. Alongside our focussed investment in skills and innovation this will support the delivery of our ambitious 10X Economic Vision for the benefit of all our people

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many children were in receipt of Free School Meals during each of the last five years, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: Please see below for information on all pupils entitled to free school meals broken down by constituency of school location.  Please note this data refers to pupils who have been awarded an entitlement to free school meals after completing the Education Authority’s free school meal application process.
